calendly.readme_md: # Calendly > Scheduling automation for meetings and events ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Calendly LLC | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [calendly.com](https://calendly.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.calendly.com](https://developer.calendly.com/api-docs) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 200 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Standard from $10/seat/month | --- ## Overview The Calendly API provides access to event types, scheduled events, invitees, and scheduling links. Automate meeting scheduling, sync calendar data, manage availability, and build custom booking experiences. ## Use Cases - Meeting scheduling automation - Calendar sync - Event analytics - Custom booking pages - Sales pipeline integration ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[calendly]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CALENDLY_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["calendly"], credentials={"calendly": "your-token"}) # Get the current authenticated user result = kit.execute("calendly_get_current_user", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["calendly"], credentials={"calendly": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["calendly"], credentials={"calendly": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Calendly](https://calendly.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://calendly.com/integrations/api_webhooks) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("calendly_get_current_user", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_event` :warning: Cancel a scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional cancellation reason. | **Returns:** `CalendlyEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_cancel_event", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `cancel_invitee` :warning: Cancel a scheduled event invitee | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional cancellation reason. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_cancel_invitee", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `create_invitee` :warning: Schedule a meeting (create invitee) | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The event UUID to schedule for. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Invitee's email address. | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Invitee's first name. | | `last_name` | `string` | No | Invitee's last name. | | `timezone` | `string` | No | Invitee's timezone (IANA format). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_create_invitee", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "email": "user@example.com", "first_name": "your-first_name", "last_name": "your-last_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `url` | `string` | Yes | The callback URL for webhook delivery. | | `events` | `array` | Yes | List of event types to subscribe to (e.g. ``["invitee.created", "invitee.canceled"]``). | | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly organization URI. | | `scope` | `string` | No | Webhook scope (``"organization"`` or ``"user"``). (default: `organization`) | **Returns:** `CalendlyWebhook` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_create_webhook", {"url": "https://example.com", "events": "your-events", "organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "scope": "organization"}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` :warning: Delete a webhook subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `webhook_id` | `string` | Yes | The webhook UUID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_delete_webhook", {"webhook_id": "your-webhook_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_current_user` Get the current authenticated user **Returns:** `CalendlyUser` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_current_user", {}) ``` --- ### `get_event` Get a single scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | **Returns:** `CalendlyEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_event", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid"}) ``` --- ### `get_event_type` Get a single event type by UUID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_type_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the event type. | **Returns:** `CalendlyEventType` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_event_type", {"event_type_uuid": "your-event_type_uuid"}) ``` --- ### `get_invitee` Get a single invitee for a scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `invitee_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the invitee. | **Returns:** `CalendlyInvitee` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_invitee", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "invitee_uuid": "your-invitee_uuid"}) ``` --- ### `get_organization` Get organization details **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_organization", {}) ``` --- ### `get_user_busy_times` Get user busy times | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The user's URI. | | `start_time` | `string` | Yes | Start of range (ISO 8601 format). | | `end_time` | `string` | Yes | End of range (ISO 8601 format). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_get_user_busy_times", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri", "start_time": "your-start_time", "end_time": "your-end_time"}) ``` --- ### `list_activity_log` List activity log events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The organization URI. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum entries to return. (default: `20`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[dict[str, Any]]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_activity_log", {"organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "limit": 20, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_availability_schedules` List user availability schedules | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The user's URI (e.g., from get_current_user). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_availability_schedules", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri"}) ``` --- ### `list_available_times` List available times for an event type | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_type_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the event type. | | `start` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 start of availability window. | | `end` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 end of availability window. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_available_times", {"event_type_uuid": "your-event_type_uuid", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end"}) ``` --- ### `list_event_types` List event types for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly user URI to list event types for. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyEventType]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_event_types", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri", "limit": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_invitees` List invitees for a scheduled event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_uuid` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the scheduled event. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyInvitee]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_invitees", {"event_uuid": "your-event_uuid", "limit": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_organization_members` List organization members | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly organization URI. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyOrganizationMembership]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_organization_members", {"organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "limit": 20, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_routing_forms` List routing forms **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_routing_forms", {}) ``` --- ### `list_scheduled_events` List scheduled events for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly user URI. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (``"active"`` or ``"canceled"``). | | `min_start_time` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 lower bound on event start time. | | `max_start_time` | `string` | No | ISO 8601 upper bound on event start time. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_scheduled_events", {"user_uri": "your-user_uri", "status": "active", "min_start_time": "your-min_start_time"}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhook subscriptions | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `organization_uri` | `string` | Yes | The Calendly organization URI. | | `scope` | `string` | No | Webhook scope (``"organization"`` or ``"user"``). (default: `organization`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendlyWebhook]` ```python result = kit.execute("calendly_list_webhooks", {"organization_uri": "your-organization_uri", "scope": "organization"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 200 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_event`, `cancel_invitee`, `create_invitee`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

cloudflare.readme_md: # Cloudflare > CDN, DNS, security, and edge computing platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-23-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/cloudflare/cloudflare-python?style=social)](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflare-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflare-python) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/cloudflare/cloudflare-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Cloudflare Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [cloudflare.com](https://cloudflare.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.cloudflare.com](https://developers.cloudflare.com/api/) | | **Auth** | API Token, API Key + Email | | **Rate Limit** | 1,200 requests/5 minutes | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $20/month | --- ## Overview The Cloudflare API provides access to DNS records, zones, firewall rules, Workers, and more. Manage your web infrastructure, configure security policies, deploy serverless functions, and monitor traffic analytics. ## Use Cases - DNS management - CDN configuration - Web security rules - Edge worker deployment - Traffic analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[cloudflare]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CLOUDFLARE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["cloudflare"], credentials={"cloudflare": "your-token"}) # Get Cloudflare zone analytics result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_analytics", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "since": "your-since"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["cloudflare"], credentials={"cloudflare": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["cloudflare"], credentials={"cloudflare": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Token 1. Create an account at [Cloudflare](https://cloudflare.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_analytics", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_dns_record` :warning: Create a DNS record in a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | DNS record type (``A``, ``AAAA``, ``CNAME``, ``MX``, etc.). | | `name` | `string` | Yes | DNS record name (e.g. ``example.com``). | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Record content (e.g. IP address or target). | | `ttl` | `integer` | No | Time to live in seconds. ``1`` means automatic. | **Returns:** `CFDNSRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_create_dns_record", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "type": "your-type", "name": "my-name", "content": "your-content", "ttl": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_page_rule` :warning: Create a page rule for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | | `targets` | `array` | Yes | List of target URL pattern dicts. | | `actions` | `array` | Yes | List of action configuration dicts. | **Returns:** `CFPageRule` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_create_page_rule", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "targets": "your-targets", "actions": "your-actions"}) ``` --- ### `create_zone` :warning: Create a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Domain name (e.g., 'example.com'). | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `type` | `string` | No | Zone type: 'full' or 'partial'. (default: `full`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_create_zone", {"name": "my-name", "account_id": "your-account_id", "type": "full"}) ``` --- ### `delete_dns_record` :warning: Delete a DNS record from a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The DNS record ID to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_dns_record", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_page_rule` :warning: Delete a page rule | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | | `rule_id` | `string` | Yes | The page rule ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_page_rule", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "rule_id": "your-rule_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_worker` :warning: Delete a worker script | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `script_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the worker script to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_worker", {"account_id": "your-account_id", "script_name": "your-script_name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_zone` :warning: Delete a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_delete_zone", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `deploy_worker` :warning: Deploy a worker script | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `script_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the worker script. | | `script_content` | `string` | Yes | The JavaScript worker code. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_deploy_worker", {"account_id": "your-account_id", "script_name": "your-script_name", "script_content": "your-script_content"}) ``` --- ### `get_analytics` Get Cloudflare zone analytics | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `since` | `string` | No | Start of time range. Accepts relative values like ``-1440`` (minutes ago) or ISO 8601 timestamps. | **Returns:** `CFAnalytics` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_analytics", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "since": "your-since"}) ``` --- ### `get_ssl_setting` Get SSL/TLS setting for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_ssl_setting", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_worker` Get a Worker script by name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | | `script_name` | `string` | Yes | The script name. | **Returns:** `CFWorker` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_worker", {"account_id": "your-account_id", "script_name": "your-script_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_zone` Get a single Cloudflare zone by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | **Returns:** `CFZone` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_get_zone", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_zone_settings` Get zone settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python record name. | | `page` | `string` | No | Page number string for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CFDNSRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_dns_records", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "type": "your-type", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `list_firewall_rules` List firewall rules for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_firewall_rules", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_page_rules` List page rules for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_page_rules", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_waf_rulesets` List WAF rulesets for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_waf_rulesets", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_workers` List Workers scripts for an account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | Cloudflare account ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_workers", {"account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_zones` List Cloudflare zones | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum zones per page (max 50). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Page number string for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CFZone]` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_list_zones", {"limit": 20, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `purge_cache` :warning: Purge Cloudflare cache for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `files` | `array` | No | List of specific URLs to purge. If omitted, purges all. | **Returns:** `CFPurgeResult` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_purge_cache", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "files": "your-files"}) ``` --- ### `toggle_development_mode` :warning: Toggle development mode for a zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `enable` | `boolean` | Yes | True to enable development mode, False to disable. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_toggle_development_mode", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "enable": True}) ``` --- ### `update_dns_record` :warning: Update a DNS record in a Cloudflare zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Cloudflare zone ID. | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The DNS record ID to update. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | DNS record type. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | DNS record name. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Updated record content. | **Returns:** `CFDNSRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_update_dns_record", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "record_id": "your-record_id", "type": "your-type", "name": "my-name", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `update_ssl_setting` :warning: Update SSL/TLS setting | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The zone ID. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | SSL mode: 'off', 'flexible', 'full', or 'strict'. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudflare_update_ssl_setting", {"zone_id": "your-zone_id", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 1,200 requests/5 minutes — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_dns_record`, `create_page_rule`, `create_zone`) cannot be undone — use with caution - cloudfront.readme_md: # AWS CloudFront > Fast, secure content delivery network (CDN) ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Networking-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-10-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/cloudfront](https://aws.amazon.com/cloudfront/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cloudfront/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[cloudfront]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CLOUDFRONT_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["cloudfront"], credentials={"cloudfront": "your-token"}) # Get a CloudFront distribution by ID result = kit.execute("cloudfront_get_distribution", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["cloudfront"], credentials={"cloudfront": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["cloudfront"], credentials={"cloudfront": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS CloudFront](https://aws.amazon.com/cloudfront/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("cloudfront_get_distribution", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_distribution` :warning: Create a new CloudFront distribution | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `origin_domain` | `string` | Yes | The domain name of the origin server (e.g. ``mybucket.s3.amazonaws.com``). | | `comment` | `string` | No | Optional description for the distribution. (default: ``) | | `enabled` | `boolean` | No | Whether the distribution should be enabled. (default: `True`) | | `default_root_object` | `string` | No | Default root object (e.g. ``index.html``). (default: `index.html`) | **Returns:** `CFDistribution` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_create_distribution", {"origin_domain": "your-origin_domain", "comment": "", "enabled": True}) ``` --- ### `create_invalidation` Create a cache invalidation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | | `paths` | `array` | Yes | List of URL paths to invalidate (e.g. ``["/images/*", "/index.html"]``). | **Returns:** `CFInvalidation` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_create_invalidation", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id", "paths": "your-paths"}) ``` --- ### `delete_distribution` :warning: Delete a CloudFront distribution | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | | `if_match` | `string` | No | The ETag value for conditional deletion. Required by CloudFront to prevent concurrent modifications. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_delete_distribution", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id", "if_match": ""}) ``` --- ### `get_distribution` Get a CloudFront distribution by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | **Returns:** `CFDistribution` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_get_distribution", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_distribution_config` Get the configuration of a CloudFront distribution | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_get_distribution_config", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_invalidation` Get an invalidation status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | | `invalidation_id` | `string` | Yes | The invalidation ID. | **Returns:** `CFInvalidation` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_get_invalidation", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id", "invalidation_id": "your-invalidation_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_distributions` List all CloudFront distributions **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_list_distributions", {}) ``` --- ### `list_invalidations` List invalidations for a distribution | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_list_invalidations", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List CloudFront distribution tags | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the CloudFront resource (e.g. ``arn:aws:cloudfront::123456789012:distribution/EDFDVBD6EXAMPLE``). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_list_tags", {"resource_arn": "your-resource_arn"}) ``` --- ### `update_distribution_enabled` Enable or disable a CloudFront distribution | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distribution_id` | `string` | Yes | The CloudFront distribution ID. | | `enabled` | `boolean` | Yes | True to enable, False to disable. | **Returns:** `CFDistribution` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudfront_update_distribution_enabled", {"distribution_id": "your-distribution_id", "enabled": True}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 100 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_distribution`, `delete_distribution`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

Create an account at [AWS CloudWatch](https://aws.amazon.com/cloudwatch/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_get_dashboard", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_log_group` Create a log group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new log group. | | `retention_in_days` | `integer` | No | Number of days to retain log events. Set to 0 for indefinite retention. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_create_log_group", {"log_group_name": "your-log_group_name", "retention_in_days": 0}) ``` --- ### `delete_alarms` :warning: Delete CloudWatch alarms | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `alarm_names` | `array` | Yes | List of alarm names to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_delete_alarms", {"alarm_names": "your-alarm_names"}) ``` --- ### `delete_dashboards` :warning: Delete CloudWatch dashboards | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_names` | `array` | Yes | List of dashboard names to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_delete_dashboards", {"dashboard_names": "your-dashboard_names"}) ``` --- ### `delete_log_group` :warning: Delete a log group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log group to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_delete_log_group", {"log_group_name": "your-log_group_name"}) ``` --- ### `describe_alarm_history` Describe alarm history | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `alarm_name` | `string` | No | Filter by alarm name. (default: ``) | | `history_item_type` | `string` | No | Filter by history item type (ConfigurationUpdate, StateUpdate, Action). (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_describe_alarm_history", {"alarm_name": "", "history_item_type": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_alarms` Describe CloudWatch alarms | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `alarm_names` | `array` | No | Optional list of alarm names to describe. | | `state_value` | `string` | No | Filter by alarm state (OK, ALARM, INSUFFICIENT_DATA). (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_describe_alarms", {"alarm_names": "your-alarm_names", "state_value": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_log_groups` Describe log groups | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name_prefix` | `string` | No | Filter by log group name prefix. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_describe_log_groups", {"log_group_name_prefix": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_log_streams` Describe log streams in a log group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log group. | | `log_stream_name_prefix` | `string` | No | Filter by stream name prefix. (default: ``) | | `order_by` | `string` | No | Order by LastEventTime or LogStreamName. (default: `LastEventTime`) | | `descending` | `boolean` | No | Whether to sort in descending order. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_describe_log_streams", {"log_group_name": "your-log_group_name", "log_stream_name_prefix": "", "order_by": "LastEventTime"}) ``` --- ### `filter_log_events` Filter log events across log streams | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log group. | | `filter_pattern` | `string` | No | CloudWatch Logs filter pattern syntax. (default: ``) | | `start_time` | `integer` | No | Start of the time range as epoch milliseconds. Set to 0 to omit. (default: `0`) | | `end_time` | `integer` | No | End of the time range as epoch milliseconds. Set to 0 to omit. (default: `0`) | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events to return. (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_filter_log_events", {"log_group_name": "your-log_group_name", "filter_pattern": "", "start_time": 0}) ``` --- ### `get_dashboard` Get a CloudWatch dashboard | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the dashboard to retrieve. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_get_dashboard", {"dashboard_name": "your-dashboard_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_log_events` Get log events from a log stream | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log group. | | `log_stream_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log stream. | | `start_time` | `integer` | No | Start of the time range as epoch milliseconds. Set to 0 to omit. (default: `0`) | | `end_time` | `integer` | No | End of the time range as epoch milliseconds. Set to 0 to omit. (default: `0`) | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events to return. (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_get_log_events", {"log_group_name": "your-log_group_name", "log_stream_name": "your-log_stream_name", "start_time": 0, "end_time": 0}) ``` --- ### `get_metric_data` Get metric data for a specific time range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `metric_data_queries` | `array` | Yes | List of MetricDataQuery dicts defining which metrics to retrieve. | | `start_time` | `string` | Yes | ISO 8601 start time for the query range. | | `end_time` | `string` | Yes | ISO 8601 end time for the query range. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_get_metric_data", {"metric_data_queries": "your-metric_data_queries", "start_time": "your-start_time", "end_time": "your-end_time"}) ``` --- ### `get_metric_statistics` Get metric statistics | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | AWS namespace (e.g. AWS/EC2). | | `metric_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the metric. | | `start_time` | `string` | Yes | ISO 8601 start time. | | `end_time` | `string` | Yes | ISO 8601 end time. | | `period` | `integer` | No | Aggregation period in seconds. (default: `300`) | | `statistics` | `array` | No | List of statistics to retrieve (e.g. Average, Sum, Maximum). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_get_metric_statistics", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "metric_name": "your-metric_name", "start_time": "your-start_time", "end_time": "your-end_time", "period": 300, "statistics": "your-statistics"}) ``` --- ### `list_dashboards` List CloudWatch dashboards | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_name_prefix` | `string` | No | Filter dashboards by name prefix. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_list_dashboards", {"dashboard_name_prefix": ""}) ``` --- ### `list_metrics` List available CloudWatch metrics | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | No | Filter by AWS namespace (e.g. AWS/EC2). (default: ``) | | `metric_name` | `string` | No | Filter by metric name. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_list_metrics", {"namespace": "", "metric_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `put_dashboard` Create or update a CloudWatch dashboard | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the dashboard. | | `dashboard_body` | `string` | Yes | JSON string defining the dashboard widgets. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_put_dashboard", {"dashboard_name": "your-dashboard_name", "dashboard_body": "your-dashboard_body"}) ``` --- ### `put_log_events` Put log events to a log stream | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `log_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log group. | | `log_stream_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the log stream. | | `log_events` | `array` | Yes | List of log event dicts with timestamp and message keys. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_put_log_events", {"log_group_name": "your-log_group_name", "log_stream_name": "your-log_stream_name", "log_events": "your-log_events"}) ``` --- ### `put_metric_alarm` Create or update a metric alarm | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `alarm_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the alarm. | | `metric_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the metric to alarm on. | | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | AWS namespace for the metric. | | `statistic` | `string` | No | Statistic to apply (Average, Sum, etc.). (default: `Average`) | | `period` | `integer` | No | Evaluation period in seconds. (default: `300`) | | `evaluation_periods` | `integer` | No | Number of periods to evaluate. (default: `1`) | | `threshold` | `number` | No | Threshold value for the alarm. (default: `0.0`) | | `comparison_operator` | `string` | No | Comparison operator (e.g. GreaterThanThreshold). (default: `GreaterThanThreshold`) | | `alarm_actions` | `array` | No | List of ARNs to notify when alarm triggers. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_put_metric_alarm", {"alarm_name": "your-alarm_name", "metric_name": "your-metric_name", "namespace": "your-namespace", "statistic": "Average", "period": 300}) ``` --- ### `put_metric_data` Publish custom metric data | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Custom namespace for the metrics. | | `metric_data` | `array` | Yes | List of MetricDatum dicts with MetricName, Value, Unit, etc. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_put_metric_data", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "metric_data": "your-metric_data"}) ``` --- ### `set_alarm_state` Set an alarm state manually | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `alarm_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the alarm to update. | | `state_value` | `string` | Yes | New state (OK, ALARM, or INSUFFICIENT_DATA). | | `state_reason` | `string` | Yes | Human-readable reason for the state change. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("cloudwatch_set_alarm_state", {"alarm_name": "your-alarm_name", "state_value": "your-state_value", "state_reason": "your-state_reason"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 50 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_alarms`, `delete_dashboards`, `delete_log_group`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

cohere.description: Connect to Cohere for chat completions, text embeddings, document reranking, text classification, tokenization, batch embed jobs, dataset management, and model fine-tuning.

A Cohere batch embedding job from the v1 ``/embed-jobs`` endpoint.

Created by :meth:`Cohere.create_embed_job` and inspected via

``status`` progresses through ``processing`` -> ``complete`` (or
``cancelling`` -> ``cancelled`` / ``failed``).

cohere.readme_md: # Cohere > Chat, embeddings, rerank, and classification models ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ai_Ml-7C3AED) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-22-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/cohere-ai/cohere-python?style=social)](https://github.com/cohere-ai/cohere-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/cohere-ai/cohere-python) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/cohere-ai/cohere-typescript) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Cohere | | **Category** | Ai Ml | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [cohere.com](https://cohere.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.cohere.com](https://docs.cohere.com/reference/about) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint and tier | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-token (varies by model) | --- ## Overview The Cohere API provides enterprise-grade language models: chat with the Command family, multilingual text embeddings, document reranking for retrieval, and few-shot text classification. Cohere mixes v1 and v2 endpoints, so this connector carries the API version in each request path. ## Use Cases - Chat and text generation (`command-r-plus`) - Semantic search with embeddings (`embed-english-v3.0`) - Retrieval reranking (`rerank-english-v3.0`) - Few-shot text classification - Tokenization and detokenization ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[cohere]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_COHERE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["cohere"], credentials={"cohere": "your-token"}) # Generate a chat response result = kit.execute( "cohere_chat", { "model": "command-r-plus", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hi"}], }, ) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["cohere"], credentials={"cohere": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["cohere"], credentials={"cohere": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Dashboard 2. API Keys 3. Create New [Get credentials →](https://dashboard.cohere.com/api-keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("cohere_list_models", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_embed_job` :warning: Cancel a running batch embed job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `embed_job_id` | `string` | Yes | The embed-job ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_cancel_embed_job", {"embed_job_id": "your-embed_job_id"}) ``` --- ### `chat` Generate a chat response | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., ``'command-r-plus'``). | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | List of message dicts with ``'role'`` and ``'content'`` keys, e.g. ``[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}]``. | | `temperature` | `number` | No | Sampling temperature (Cohere accepts 0.0-1.0). | | `max_tokens` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `tools` | `array` | No | Tool/function definitions the model may call, each an OpenAI-style ``{"type": "function", "function": {...}}`` dict. | | `documents` | `array` | No | Grounding documents for retrieval-augmented generation, each a dict (e.g. ``{"id": "doc1", "data": {...}}``). | | `response_format` | `object` | No | Structured-output spec, e.g. ``{"type": "json_object"}`` or a JSON schema. | **Returns:** `ChatResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_chat", {"model": "your-model", "messages": "your-messages", "temperature": 10, "max_tokens": 10}) ``` --- ### `check_api_key` Check whether the API key is valid **Returns:** `ApiKeyCheck` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_check_api_key", {}) ``` --- ### `classify` Classify text into labelled categories | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Classification model ID (e.g., ``'embed-english-v3.0'``). | | `inputs` | `array` | Yes | List of strings to classify. | | `examples` | `array` | Yes | Labelled example dicts, each with ``'text'`` and ``'label'`` keys, used as few-shot training data. | **Returns:** `ClassifyResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_classify", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "examples": "your-examples"}) ``` --- ### `create_dataset` Create a dataset from an uploaded file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Human-readable name for the dataset. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Dataset type, e.g. ``'embed-input'``, ``'embed-result'``, ``'reranker-finetune-input'``, or ``'chat-finetune-input'``. | | `data` | `string` | Yes | Raw bytes of the dataset file (JSONL / CSV / TXT). | | `filename` | `string` | No | Name for the uploaded file part (default ``"data.jsonl"``). | | `keep_original_file` | `boolean` | No | Store the original uploaded file alongside the validated dataset. | | `skip_malformed_input` | `boolean` | No | Drop malformed rows instead of failing validation. | | `eval_data` | `string` | No | Optional raw bytes of an evaluation split file. | **Returns:** `Dataset` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_create_dataset", {"name": "my-name", "type": "your-type", "data": "your-data", "filename": "your-filename", "keep_original_file": True}) ``` --- ### `create_embed_job` Create a batch embed job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Embedding model ID (e.g., ``'embed-english-v3.0'``). | | `dataset_id` | `string` | Yes | ID of a validated ``embed-input`` dataset to embed. | | `input_type` | `string` | Yes | Embedding input type. One of ``'search_document'``, ``'search_query'``, ``'classification'``, ``'clustering'``, or ``'image'``. | | `name` | `string` | No | Optional human-readable name for the job. | | `embedding_types` | `array` | No | Embedding formats to produce (e.g. ``['float']``). | | `truncate` | `string` | No | How to handle over-length inputs -- ``'START'`` or ``'END'`` (default ``'END'``). | **Returns:** `EmbedJob` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_create_embed_job", {"model": "your-model", "dataset_id": "your-dataset_id", "input_type": "your-input_type", "name": "my-name", "embedding_types": "your-embedding_types"}) ``` --- ### `create_finetuned_model` Create a fine-tuned model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the fine-tuned model. | | `settings` | `object` | Yes | Training configuration dict. Must include a ``base_model`` object (e.g. ``{"base_type": "BASE_TYPE_CHAT"}``) and a ``dataset_id``; may include a ``hyperparameters`` object. | **Returns:** `FinetunedModel` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_create_finetuned_model", {"name": "my-name", "settings": "your-settings"}) ``` --- ### `delete_dataset` :warning: Delete a dataset | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dataset_id` | `string` | Yes | The dataset ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_delete_dataset", {"dataset_id": "your-dataset_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_finetuned_model` :warning: Delete a fine-tuned model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `finetuned_model_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuned model ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_delete_finetuned_model", {"finetuned_model_id": "your-finetuned_model_id"}) ``` --- ### `detokenize` Convert token IDs back into text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID whose tokenizer to use (e.g., ``'command-r-plus'``). | | `tokens` | `array` | Yes | List of integer token IDs to convert back to text. | **Returns:** `DetokenizeResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_detokenize", {"model": "your-model", "tokens": "your-tokens"}) ``` --- ### `embed` Create text embeddings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., ``'embed-english-v3.0'``). | | `texts` | `array` | Yes | List of strings to embed. | | `input_type` | `string` | Yes | Embedding input type. One of ``'search_document'``, ``'search_query'``, ``'classification'``, ``'clustering'``, or ``'image'``. | | `embedding_types` | `array` | No | Embedding formats to return (e.g. ``['float']``, ``['int8']``, ``['binary']``). Defaults to ``['float']`` when omitted. | | `truncate` | `string` | No | How to handle inputs longer than the context length. One of ``'NONE'``, ``'START'``, or ``'END'`` (default ``'END'``). | **Returns:** `EmbedResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_embed", {"model": "your-model", "texts": "your-texts", "input_type": "your-input_type", "embedding_types": "your-embedding_types", "truncate": "your-truncate"}) ``` --- ### `get_dataset` Get a dataset by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dataset_id` | `string` | Yes | The dataset ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `Dataset` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_get_dataset", {"dataset_id": "your-dataset_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_dataset_usage` Get organization dataset storage usage **Returns:** `DatasetUsage` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_get_dataset_usage", {}) ``` --- ### `get_embed_job` Get a batch embed job by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `embed_job_id` | `string` | Yes | The embed-job ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `EmbedJob` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_get_embed_job", {"embed_job_id": "your-embed_job_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_finetuned_model` Get a fine-tuned model by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `finetuned_model_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuned model ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `FinetunedModel` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_get_finetuned_model", {"finetuned_model_id": "your-finetuned_model_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_model` Get a Cohere model by name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | The model name to retrieve (e.g., ``'command-r-plus'``). | **Returns:** `CohereModel` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_get_model", {"model": "your-model"}) ``` --- ### `list_datasets` List datasets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dataset_type` | `string` | No | Filter by dataset type (e.g. ``'embed-input'``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of datasets to return. | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Number of datasets to skip (for paging). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_list_datasets", {"dataset_type": "your-dataset_type", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_embed_jobs` List batch embed jobs **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_list_embed_jobs", {}) ``` --- ### `list_finetuned_models` List fine-tuned models **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_list_finetuned_models", {}) ``` --- ### `list_models` List available Cohere models | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `endpoint` | `string` | No | Filter to models that support this endpoint (e.g. ``'chat'``, ``'embed'``, ``'rerank'``, ``'classify'``). | | `default_only` | `boolean` | No | When True, return only Cohere's default models. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_list_models", {"endpoint": "your-endpoint", "default_only": True}) ``` --- ### `rerank` Rerank documents by relevance to a query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Rerank model ID (e.g., ``'rerank-english-v3.0'``). | | `query` | `string` | Yes | The search query to rank documents against. | | `documents` | `array` | Yes | List of document strings to rerank. | | `top_n` | `integer` | No | Number of top results to return. Returns all documents ranked when omitted. | | `max_tokens_per_doc` | `integer` | No | Truncate documents to this many tokens before reranking. | **Returns:** `RerankResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_rerank", {"model": "your-model", "query": "is:unread", "documents": "your-documents", "top_n": 10, "max_tokens_per_doc": 10}) ``` --- ### `tokenize` Tokenize text into token IDs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID whose tokenizer to use (e.g., ``'command-r-plus'``). | | `text` | `string` | Yes | The text to tokenize. | **Returns:** `TokenizeResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("cohere_tokenize", {"model": "your-model", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Embeddings require an `input_type` (`search_document`, `search_query`, `classification`, or `clustering`) — pick the one matching your downstream task - `rerank` returns document indices ordered by `relevance_score`, not the documents themselves — index back into your original list - Rate limit varies by endpoint and tier — batch `texts` into a single `embed` call where possible ## Related Connectors - [OpenAI](../openai_connector/) — GPT models and embeddings - [Anthropic](../anthropic_connector/) — Claude models - [Pinecone](../pinecone/) — Vector database --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

meta.rate_limit: Varies by endpoint and key type

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

confluence.readme_md: # Confluence > Team wiki, documentation, and knowledge management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Knowledge-0EA5E9) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/atlassian-api/atlassian-python-api?style=social)](https://github.com/atlassian-api/atlassian-python-api) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/atlassian-api/atlassian-python-api) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Atlassian | | **Category** | Knowledge | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [www.atlassian.com/software/confluence](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence) | | **API Docs** | [developer.atlassian.com](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/confluence/rest/v2/intro/) | | **Auth** | Basic Auth (email:api_token), OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free up to 10 users, Standard from $6.05/user/month | --- ## Overview The Confluence REST API provides access to spaces, pages, blog posts, comments, and attachments. Create and organize documentation, search content, manage permissions, and build knowledge management workflows. ## Use Cases - Documentation automation - Knowledge base management - Content publishing - Team collaboration - Compliance documentation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[confluence]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_CONFLUENCE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["confluence"], credentials={"confluence": "your-token"}) # List pages in a space or across all spaces result = kit.execute("confluence_list_pages", {"space_id": "space-123", "limit": 25}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["confluence"], credentials={"confluence": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["confluence"], credentials={"confluence": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Basic Auth (email:api_token) 1. Create an account at [Confluence](https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("confluence_list_pages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID to comment on. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment body in Confluence storage format (XHTML). | **Returns:** `ConfluenceComment` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_add_comment", {"page_id": "page-123", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `add_page_label` :warning: Add a label to a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the page to label. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | The label name (e.g., ``"important"``). | **Returns:** `ConfluenceLabel` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_add_page_label", {"page_id": "page-123", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_blog_post` :warning: Create a blog post | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The space ID. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Blog post title. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Content in storage format (HTML). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_create_blog_post", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_page` Create a new page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the space to create the page in. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Page title. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Page body content in Confluence storage format (XHTML). | | `parent_id` | `string` | No | Optional parent page ID for nested pages. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_create_page", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!", "parent_id": "your-parent_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_page` :warning: Delete a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the page to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_delete_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_blog_post` Get a blog post by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `blog_post_id` | `string` | Yes | The blog post ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_blog_post", {"blog_post_id": "your-blog_post_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_page` Get a single page by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the page. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_by_title` Get a page by title within a space | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the space to search in. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Exact page title to match. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_by_title", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_history` Get version history for a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_history", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_labels` Get labels on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the page to get labels for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_labels", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_properties` Get content properties for a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_page_properties", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_space` Get a single space by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the space. | **Returns:** `ConfluenceSpace` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_get_space", {"space_id": "your-space_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_attachments` List attachments on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum attachments to return. (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_attachments", {"page_id": "page-123", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_blog_posts` List blog posts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | No | Filter by space ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum posts to return. (default: `25`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_blog_posts", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID to list comments for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_comments", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_page_ancestors` List ancestor pages of a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the page to get ancestors for. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_page_ancestors", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_page_children` List child pages of a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the parent page. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of child pages to return (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for paginating through children. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_page_children", {"page_id": "page-123", "limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_pages` List pages in a space or across all spaces | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | No | Optional space ID to filter pages by. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of pages per request (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for fetching the next page of results. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_pages", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "limit": 25}) ``` --- ### `list_space_pages` List pages in a specific space | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the space to list pages from. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum pages per request (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_space_pages", {"space_id": "your-space_id", "limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_spaces` List all spaces | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of spaces per page (max 250). (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluenceSpace]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_list_spaces", {"limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `move_page` Move a page to a new parent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID to move. | | `target_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the new parent page. | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_move_page", {"page_id": "page-123", "target_id": "your-target_id"}) ``` --- ### `remove_page_label` :warning: Remove a label from a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | | `label_id` | `string` | Yes | The label ID to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_remove_page_label", {"page_id": "page-123", "label_id": "your-label_id"}) ``` --- ### `search` Search for pages using CQL | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query string. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page. (default: `25`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Cursor token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ConfluencePage]` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_search", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 25, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `set_page_property` :warning: Set a content property on a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The page ID. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Property key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | JSON-serializable value (max 32KB). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_set_page_property", {"page_id": "page-123", "key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `update_page` Update an existing page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the page to update. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | New page title. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | New page body content in storage format (XHTML). | | `version_number` | `integer` | Yes | New version number (current version + 1). | **Returns:** `ConfluencePage` ```python result = kit.execute("confluence_update_page", {"page_id": "page-123", "title": "My Title", "body": "Hello!", "version_number": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search` for filtered queries and `list_attachments` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_comment`, `add_page_label`, `create_blog_post`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Notion](../notion/) — Knowledge workspace --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
