datadog.readme_md: # Datadog > Monitoring, APM, and log management platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-22-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/DataDog/datadog-api-client-python?style=social)](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-python) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/DataDog/datadog-api-client-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Datadog Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [datadoghq.com](https://datadoghq.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.datadoghq.com](https://docs.datadoghq.com/api/latest/) | | **Auth** | API Key + Application Key | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/hour for some endpoints | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $15/host/month | --- ## Overview The Datadog API lets you manage dashboards, monitors, metrics, logs, and incidents. Query time-series data, create alerts, manage service catalogs, and build observability automation for your infrastructure. ## Use Cases - Infrastructure monitoring - Alert management - Dashboard automation - Log analysis - Incident response ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[datadog]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_DATADOG_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["datadog"], credentials={"datadog": "your-token"}) # List Datadog events result = kit.execute("datadog_list_events", {"start": 10, "end": 10, "priority": "your-priority"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["datadog"], credentials={"datadog": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["datadog"], credentials={"datadog": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key + Application Key 1. Create an account at [Datadog](https://datadoghq.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.datadoghq.com/organization-settings/api-keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("datadog_list_events", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_downtime` :warning: Cancel a scheduled downtime | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `downtime_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric downtime ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_cancel_downtime", {"downtime_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_downtime` :warning: Schedule a downtime window | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `scope` | `string` | Yes | Scope of the downtime (e.g. ``"host:myhost"``). | | `start` | `integer` | Yes | POSIX timestamp for the start of the downtime. | | `end` | `integer` | No | Optional POSIX timestamp for the end. Omit for indefinite. | **Returns:** `DatadogDowntime` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_downtime", {"scope": "your-scope", "start": 10, "end": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_event` :warning: Create a Datadog event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Event title. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Event body text. Supports Markdown. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Event priority (``normal`` or ``low``). | | `tags` | `array` | No | List of tags to attach (e.g. ``["env:prod"]``). | **Returns:** `DatadogEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_event", {"title": "My Title", "text": "Hello!", "priority": "your-priority", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `create_incident` :warning: Create an incident | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Incident title. | | `severity` | `string` | No | Severity level (SEV-1 through SEV-5). (default: `SEV-3`) | | `customer_impact_scope` | `string` | No | Description of customer impact. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_incident", {"title": "My Title", "severity": "SEV-3", "customer_impact_scope": "your-customer_impact_scope"}) ``` --- ### `create_monitor` :warning: Create a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the monitor. | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Monitor type (e.g. ``metric alert``, ``service check``). | | `query` | `string` | Yes | The monitor query string. | | `message` | `string` | No | Notification message body. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_create_monitor", {"name": "my-name", "type": "your-type", "query": "is:unread", "message": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `delete_dashboard` :warning: Delete a dashboard | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_id` | `string` | Yes | The dashboard ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_delete_dashboard", {"dashboard_id": "your-dashboard_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_monitor` :warning: Delete a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The monitor ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_delete_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_dashboard` Get a dashboard by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dashboard_id` | `string` | Yes | The dashboard ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_get_dashboard", {"dashboard_id": "your-dashboard_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_metric_metadata` Get metadata for a metric | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `metric_name` | `string` | Yes | The fully qualified metric name. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_get_metric_metadata", {"metric_name": "your-metric_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_monitor` Get a single Datadog monitor by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric monitor ID. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_get_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_dashboards` List Datadog dashboards **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogDashboard]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_dashboards", {}) ``` --- ### `list_downtimes` List scheduled downtimes **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogDowntime]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_downtimes", {}) ``` --- ### `list_events` List Datadog events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `start` | `integer` | Yes | Start timestamp in epoch seconds. | | `end` | `integer` | Yes | End timestamp in epoch seconds. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Filter by priority (``normal`` or ``low``). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_events", {"start": 10, "end": 10, "priority": "your-priority"}) ``` --- ### `list_hosts` List hosts reporting to Datadog | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter string (e.g. host name or tag). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_hosts", {"filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `list_incidents` List incidents | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum incidents to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_incidents", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_monitors` List Datadog monitors | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of monitors to return (max 1000). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogMonitor]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_monitors", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_slos` List SLOs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | Search query to filter SLOs. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum SLOs to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_list_slos", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `mute_monitor` :warning: Mute a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric monitor ID to mute. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_mute_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `query_metrics` Query Datadog metrics | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Metrics query string (e.g. ``avg:system.cpu.user{*}``). | | `from_ts` | `integer` | Yes | Start timestamp in epoch seconds. | | `to_ts` | `integer` | Yes | End timestamp in epoch seconds. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_query_metrics", {"query": "is:unread", "from_ts": 10, "to_ts": 10}) ``` --- ### `search_logs` Search logs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Log search query (Datadog log search syntax). | | `time_from` | `string` | Yes | Start time (ISO 8601 or relative like 'now-1h'). | | `time_to` | `string` | Yes | End time (ISO 8601 or 'now'). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum log entries to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_search_logs", {"query": "is:unread", "time_from": "your-time_from", "time_to": "your-time_to", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `search_monitors` Search Datadog monitors by query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query (e.g. ``"tag:env:prod"`` or monitor name). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of monitors to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DatadogMonitor]` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_search_monitors", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `unmute_monitor` Unmute a Datadog monitor | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `monitor_id` | `integer` | Yes | The numeric monitor ID to unmute. | **Returns:** `DatadogMonitor` ```python result = kit.execute("datadog_unmute_monitor", {"monitor_id": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_logs` for filtered queries and `list_dashboards` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 300 requests/hour for some endpoints — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_downtime`, `create_downtime`, `create_event`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 22 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment - [Dockerhub](../dockerhub/) — Container registry --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

discord.readme_md: # Discord > Messaging, voice, and community management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Rapptz/discord.py?style=social)](https://github.com/Rapptz/discord.py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/Rapptz/discord.py) [![API Spec](https://img.shields.io/badge/API_Spec-SDK-6366F1?logo=github)](https://github.com/discord/discord-api-docs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Discord Inc. | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [discord.com](https://discord.com) | | **API Docs** | [discord.com](https://discord.com/developers/docs) | | **Auth** | Bot Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 50 requests/second global | | **Pricing** | Free, Nitro from $9.99/month | --- ## Overview The Discord API provides access to Discord servers (guilds), channels, messages, and members. Build bots that moderate communities, send notifications, manage roles, and create interactive experiences. Supports bot tokens and OAuth 2.0. ## Use Cases - Community moderation bots - Server management automation - Notification systems - Gaming integrations - Event management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[discord]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_DISCORD_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["discord"], credentials={"discord": "your-token"}) # List messages in a channel result = kit.execute("discord_list_messages", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "before": "your-before"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["discord"], credentials={"discord": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["discord"], credentials={"discord": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bot Token 1. Developer Portal 2. New Application 3. Bot Token [Get credentials →](https://discord.com/developers/applications) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("discord_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_member_role` :warning: Add a role to a guild member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role snowflake ID to assign. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_add_member_role", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "role_id": "your-role_id"}) ``` --- ### `add_reaction` Add a reaction to a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID. | | `emoji` | `string` | Yes | Unicode emoji (e.g. ``\U0001f44d``) or custom emoji in ``name:id`` format (e.g. ``custom_emoji:123456``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_add_reaction", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `ban_member` :warning: Ban a member from a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID to ban. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional audit log reason for the ban. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_ban_member", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `create_channel` :warning: Create a new channel in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new channel (2-100 characters, lowercase, no spaces). | | `type` | `integer` | No | Channel type integer. 0 = text, 2 = voice, 4 = category. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `DiscordChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_channel", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "name": "my-name", "type": 0}) ``` --- ### `create_role` :warning: Create a role in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new role. | | `permissions` | `string` | No | Permission bit set as a string. Defaults to ``@everyone`` permissions of the guild. | | `color` | `integer` | No | RGB color value for the role (integer). | **Returns:** `DiscordRole` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_role", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "name": "my-name", "permissions": "your-permissions", "color": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_thread` :warning: Create a thread in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The parent channel snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the thread (1-100 characters). | | `message_id` | `string` | No | Optional message snowflake ID to start the thread from. | **Returns:** `DiscordChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_thread", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "name": "my-name", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook for a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the webhook (1-80 characters). | | `avatar` | `string` | No | Optional base64-encoded 128x128 image for the webhook's default avatar. | **Returns:** `DiscordWebhook` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_create_webhook", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "name": "my-name", "avatar": "your-avatar"}) ``` --- ### `delete_channel` :warning: Delete a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_delete_channel", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_delete_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `edit_guild` :warning: Edit guild settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | Optional new name for the guild (2-100 characters). | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional new description (for Community guilds). | | `icon` | `string` | No | Optional base64-encoded 128x128 image for the guild icon, or ``None`` to remove the icon. | **Returns:** `DiscordGuild` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_edit_guild", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `edit_message` Edit a message in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID containing the message. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to edit. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | New message text content (max 2000 characters). | **Returns:** `DiscordMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_edit_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `get_channel` Get a single channel by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `DiscordChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_channel", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_guild` Get guild information | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `DiscordGuild` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_guild", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_member` Get a single guild member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `GuildMember` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_member", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `DiscordUser` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `kick_member` :warning: Kick a member from a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID to kick. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Optional audit log reason for the kick. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_kick_member", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `list_channels` List channels in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_channels", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_guild_members` List members of a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of members to return (1-1000, default 100). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_guild_members", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "limit": 100}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List messages in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages to return (1-100, default 50). (default: `50`) | | `before` | `string` | No | Get messages before this message snowflake ID. | | `after` | `string` | No | Get messages after this message snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_messages", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "before": "your-before"}) ``` --- ### `list_pinned_messages` List pinned messages in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_pinned_messages", {"channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `list_roles` List roles in a guild | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_list_roles", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id"}) ``` --- ### `pin_message` Pin a message in a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to pin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_pin_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ### `remove_member_role` :warning: Remove a role from a guild member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `guild_id` | `string` | Yes | The guild snowflake ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user snowflake ID. | | `role_id` | `string` | Yes | The role snowflake ID to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_remove_member_role", {"guild_id": "your-guild_id", "user_id": "user-123", "role_id": "your-role_id"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` :warning: Send a message to a Discord channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Message text content (max 2000 characters). | | `embeds` | `array` | No | Optional list of embed objects (max 10). Each embed is a dict matching the Discord embed structure. | **Returns:** `DiscordMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_send_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "content": "your-content", "embeds": "your-embeds"}) ``` --- ### `unpin_message` Unpin a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel snowflake ID. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The message snowflake ID to unpin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("discord_unpin_message", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 50 requests/second global — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_member_role`, `ban_member`, `create_channel`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 25 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration - [Telegram](../telegram/) — Bot messaging --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.

dockerhub.readme_md: # Docker Hub > Container registry and image management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Docker Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [hub.docker.com](https://hub.docker.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.docker.com](https://docs.docker.com/docker-hub/api/latest/) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token | | **Rate Limit** | 100 pulls/6 hours (anonymous), 200 (authenticated) | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $5/month | --- ## Overview The Docker Hub API provides access to repositories, images, tags, and organizations. Search for images, manage repository settings, check image vulnerabilities, and automate container image lifecycle management. ## Use Cases - Image registry management - Vulnerability scanning automation - CI/CD image publishing - Repository access control - Image tag management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[dockerhub]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_DOCKERHUB_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["dockerhub"], credentials={"dockerhub": "your-token"}) # List repositories for a Docker Hub namespace result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_repos", {"namespace": "default", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["dockerhub"], credentials={"dockerhub": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["dockerhub"], credentials={"dockerhub": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Docker Hub](https://hub.docker.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://hub.docker.com/settings/security) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_repos", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_repo` :warning: Create a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace (user or org). | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional short description. | | `is_private` | `boolean` | No | Whether the repository is private. | **Returns:** `DockerRepo` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_create_repo", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description", "is_private": True}) ``` --- ### `delete_tag` :warning: Delete a repository tag | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `tag` | `string` | Yes | Tag name to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_delete_tag", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "tag": "your-tag"}) ``` --- ### `get_build_history` Get build history for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_build_history", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `get_org` Get a Docker Hub organisation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `orgname` | `string` | Yes | Organisation name. | **Returns:** `DockerOrg` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_org", {"orgname": "your-orgname"}) ``` --- ### `get_repo` Get a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `DockerRepo` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_repo", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `get_tag` Get a single Docker Hub repository tag | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `tag` | `string` | Yes | Tag name (e.g. ``latest``). | **Returns:** `DockerTag` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_tag", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "tag": "your-tag"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a Docker Hub user profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `username` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub username. | **Returns:** `DockerUser` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_get_user", {"username": "your-username"}) ``` --- ### `list_build_triggers` List build triggers for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_build_triggers", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `list_collaborators` List collaborators of a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Docker Hub namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_collaborators", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo"}) ``` --- ### `list_orgs` List Docker Hub organisations for the authenticated user **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_orgs", {}) ``` --- ### `list_repos` List repositories for a Docker Hub namespace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DockerRepo]` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_repos", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List tags for a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace. | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum tags per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DockerTag]` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_list_tags", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `search_repos` Search Docker Hub repositories | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query string. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `25`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[DockerRepo]` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_search_repos", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 25, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `update_repo` Update a Docker Hub repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `namespace` | `string` | Yes | Repository namespace (user or org). | | `repo` | `string` | Yes | Repository name. | | `description` | `string` | No | New short description for the repository. | **Returns:** `DockerRepo` ```python result = kit.execute("dockerhub_update_repo", {"namespace": "your-namespace", "repo": "owner/repo", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_repos` for filtered queries and `list_build_triggers` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 pulls/6 hours (anonymous), 200 (authenticated) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_repo`, `delete_tag`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

ec2.readme_md: # AWS EC2 > Resizable compute capacity in the cloud ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Compute-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-30-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Compute | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/ec2](https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use (from $0.0116/hour for t3.micro) | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[ec2]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_EC2_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["ec2"], credentials={"ec2": "your-token"}) # Get instance console output result = kit.execute("ec2_get_console_output", {"instance_id": "your-instance_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["ec2"], credentials={"ec2": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["ec2"], credentials={"ec2": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS EC2](https://aws.amazon.com/ec2/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("ec2_get_console_output", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `allocate_address` Allocate an Elastic IP address | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `domain` | `string` | No | Scope for the address -- ``vpc`` (default) or ``standard``. (default: `vpc`) | **Returns:** `EC2Address` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_allocate_address", {"domain": "vpc"}) ``` --- ### `associate_address` Associate an Elastic IP with an instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `allocation_id` | `string` | Yes | Allocation ID of the Elastic IP. | | `instance_id` | `string` | Yes | Instance ID to associate with. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_associate_address", {"allocation_id": "your-allocation_id", "instance_id": "your-instance_id"}) ``` --- ### `authorize_security_group_egress` Add an outbound rule to a security group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Security group ID. | | `ip_protocol` | `string` | Yes | IP protocol (tcp, udp, icmp, or -1 for all). | | `from_port` | `integer` | Yes | Start of port range. | | `to_port` | `integer` | Yes | End of port range. | | `cidr_ip` | `string` | No | CIDR IP range to allow. (default: `0.0.0.0/0`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_authorize_security_group_egress", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "ip_protocol": "your-ip_protocol", "from_port": 10, "to_port": 10, "cidr_ip": "0.0.0.0/0"}) ``` --- ### `authorize_security_group_ingress` Add an inbound rule to a security group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Security group ID. | | `ip_protocol` | `string` | Yes | IP protocol (tcp, udp, icmp, or -1 for all). | | `from_port` | `integer` | Yes | Start of port range. | | `to_port` | `integer` | Yes | End of port range. | | `cidr_ip` | `string` | No | CIDR IP range to allow. (default: `0.0.0.0/0`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_authorize_security_group_ingress", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "ip_protocol": "your-ip_protocol", "from_port": 10, "to_port": 10, "cidr_ip": "0.0.0.0/0"}) ``` --- ### `create_key_pair` Create a key pair | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the key pair. | **Returns:** `EC2KeyPair` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_create_key_pair", {"key_name": "your-key_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_security_group` Create a security group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the security group. | | `description` | `string` | Yes | Description of the security group. | | `vpc_id` | `string` | No | VPC in which to create the group. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `EC2SecurityGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_create_security_group", {"group_name": "your-group_name", "description": "your-description", "vpc_id": ""}) ``` --- ### `create_tags` Create or update tags on EC2 resources | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_ids` | `array` | Yes | Resource IDs to tag (instances, volumes, etc.). | | `tags` | `object` | Yes | Dict of tag key-value pairs. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_create_tags", {"resource_ids": "your-resource_ids", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `create_volume` Create an EBS volume | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `availability_zone` | `string` | Yes | AZ for the volume (e.g. us-east-1a). | | `size` | `integer` | No | Volume size in GiB. (default: `20`) | | `volume_type` | `string` | No | Volume type (gp3, gp2, io1, io2, st1, sc1, standard). (default: `gp3`) | | `encrypted` | `boolean` | No | Whether to encrypt the volume. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `EC2Volume` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_create_volume", {"availability_zone": "your-availability_zone", "size": 20, "volume_type": "gp3"}) ``` --- ### `delete_key_pair` :warning: Delete a key pair | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the key pair to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_delete_key_pair", {"key_name": "your-key_name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_security_group` :warning: Delete a security group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Security group ID to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_delete_security_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id"}) ``` --- ### `describe_addresses` Describe Elastic IP addresses | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `allocation_ids` | `array` | No | Specific allocation IDs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_addresses", {"allocation_ids": "your-allocation_ids"}) ``` --- ### `describe_availability_zones` Describe availability zones **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_availability_zones", {}) ``` --- ### `describe_images` Describe AMI images | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `image_ids` | `array` | No | Specific image IDs to describe. | | `owners` | `array` | No | Filter by owner (e.g. ``["self"]``, ``["amazon"]``). | | `filters` | `object` | No | Additional filter criteria. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_images", {"image_ids": "your-image_ids", "owners": "your-owners"}) ``` --- ### `describe_instance_types` Describe available instance types | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_types` | `array` | No | Specific instance types to describe (e.g. ``["t3.micro", "m5.large"]``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_instance_types", {"instance_types": "your-instance_types"}) ``` --- ### `describe_instances` Describe EC2 instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_ids` | `array` | No | Specific instance IDs to describe. | | `filters` | `object` | No | Filter criteria (e.g. ``{"instance-state-name": "running"}``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_instances", {"instance_ids": "your-instance_ids", "filters": "your-filters"}) ``` --- ### `describe_key_pairs` Describe key pairs **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_key_pairs", {}) ``` --- ### `describe_security_groups` Describe security groups | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_ids` | `array` | No | Specific security group IDs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_security_groups", {"group_ids": "your-group_ids"}) ``` --- ### `describe_subnets` Describe subnets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subnet_ids` | `array` | No | Specific subnet IDs to describe. | | `filters` | `object` | No | Additional filter criteria. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_subnets", {"subnet_ids": "your-subnet_ids", "filters": "your-filters"}) ``` --- ### `describe_tags` Describe tags | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `filters` | `object` | No | Filter criteria (e.g. ``{"resource-type": "instance"}``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_tags", {"filters": "your-filters"}) ``` --- ### `describe_volumes` Describe EBS volumes | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `volume_ids` | `array` | No | Specific volume IDs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_volumes", {"volume_ids": "your-volume_ids"}) ``` --- ### `describe_vpcs` Describe VPCs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `vpc_ids` | `array` | No | Specific VPC IDs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_describe_vpcs", {"vpc_ids": "your-vpc_ids"}) ``` --- ### `get_console_output` Get instance console output | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_id` | `string` | Yes | Instance ID to get console output for. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_get_console_output", {"instance_id": "your-instance_id"}) ``` --- ### `reboot_instances` Reboot EC2 instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_ids` | `array` | Yes | Instance IDs to reboot. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_reboot_instances", {"instance_ids": "your-instance_ids"}) ``` --- ### `release_address` :warning: Release an Elastic IP address | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `allocation_id` | `string` | Yes | Allocation ID of the address to release. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_release_address", {"allocation_id": "your-allocation_id"}) ``` --- ### `revoke_security_group_egress` Remove an outbound rule from a security group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Security group ID. | | `ip_protocol` | `string` | Yes | IP protocol of the rule to remove. | | `from_port` | `integer` | Yes | Start of port range. | | `to_port` | `integer` | Yes | End of port range. | | `cidr_ip` | `string` | No | CIDR IP range of the rule. (default: `0.0.0.0/0`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_revoke_security_group_egress", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "ip_protocol": "your-ip_protocol", "from_port": 10, "to_port": 10, "cidr_ip": "0.0.0.0/0"}) ``` --- ### `revoke_security_group_ingress` Remove an inbound rule from a security group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Security group ID. | | `ip_protocol` | `string` | Yes | IP protocol of the rule to remove. | | `from_port` | `integer` | Yes | Start of port range. | | `to_port` | `integer` | Yes | End of port range. | | `cidr_ip` | `string` | No | CIDR IP range of the rule. (default: `0.0.0.0/0`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_revoke_security_group_ingress", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "ip_protocol": "your-ip_protocol", "from_port": 10, "to_port": 10, "cidr_ip": "0.0.0.0/0"}) ``` --- ### `run_instances` :warning: Launch new EC2 instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `image_id` | `string` | Yes | AMI image ID to launch. | | `instance_type` | `string` | No | Instance type (e.g. t3.micro, m5.large). (default: `t3.micro`) | | `min_count` | `integer` | No | Minimum number of instances to launch. (default: `1`) | | `max_count` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of instances to launch. (default: `1`) | | `key_name` | `string` | No | Name of the SSH key pair. (default: ``) | | `security_group_ids` | `array` | No | Security group IDs to attach. | | `subnet_id` | `string` | No | Subnet to launch into. (default: ``) | | `user_data` | `string` | No | Base64-encoded user data script. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_run_instances", {"image_id": "your-image_id", "instance_type": "t3.micro", "min_count": 1}) ``` --- ### `start_instances` Start stopped EC2 instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_ids` | `array` | Yes | Instance IDs to start. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_start_instances", {"instance_ids": "your-instance_ids"}) ``` --- ### `stop_instances` Stop running EC2 instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_ids` | `array` | Yes | Instance IDs to stop. | | `force` | `boolean` | No | Force the instances to stop without graceful shutdown. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_stop_instances", {"instance_ids": "your-instance_ids", "force": False}) ``` --- ### `terminate_instances` :warning: Terminate EC2 instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_ids` | `array` | Yes | Instance IDs to terminate. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ec2_terminate_instances", {"instance_ids": "your-instance_ids"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 100 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_key_pair`, `delete_security_group`, `release_address`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 30 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

ecr.readme_md: # AWS ECR > Fully managed container image registry ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-12-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/ecr](https://aws.amazon.com/ecr/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECR/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use ($0.10/GB/month) | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[ecr]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_ECR_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["ecr"], credentials={"ecr": "your-token"}) # Get an authorization token for Docker login result = kit.execute("ecr_get_authorization_token", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["ecr"], credentials={"ecr": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["ecr"], credentials={"ecr": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS ECR](https://aws.amazon.com/ecr/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("ecr_get_authorization_token", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `batch_delete_image` :warning: Batch delete images from a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository. | | `image_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of image identifier dicts to delete. Each dict should contain imageDigest and/or imageTag. | **Returns:** `ECRBatchDeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_batch_delete_image", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "image_ids": "your-image_ids"}) ``` --- ### `create_repository` Create a new ECR repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new repository. | | `image_tag_mutability` | `string` | No | Tag mutability setting (MUTABLE or IMMUTABLE). (default: `MUTABLE`) | | `scan_on_push` | `boolean` | No | Whether to scan images on push. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `ECRRepository` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_create_repository", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "image_tag_mutability": "MUTABLE", "scan_on_push": False}) ``` --- ### `delete_repository` :warning: Delete an ECR repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository to delete. | | `force` | `boolean` | No | If True, delete the repository even if it contains images. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `ECRRepository` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_delete_repository", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "force": False}) ``` --- ### `describe_images` Describe images in an ECR repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository. | | `image_ids` | `array` | No | Optional list of image identifier dicts to describe. Each dict may contain imageDigest and/or imageTag. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_describe_images", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "image_ids": "your-image_ids"}) ``` --- ### `describe_repositories` List ECR repositories | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_names` | `array` | No | Optional list of repository names to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_describe_repositories", {"repository_names": "your-repository_names"}) ``` --- ### `get_authorization_token` Get an authorization token for Docker login **Returns:** `ECRAuthorizationData` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_get_authorization_token", {}) ``` --- ### `get_lifecycle_policy` Get the lifecycle policy for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository. | **Returns:** `ECRLifecyclePolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_get_lifecycle_policy", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name"}) ``` --- ### `list_images` List images in an ECR repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository. | | `tag_status` | `string` | No | Filter by tag status (TAGGED, UNTAGGED, or ANY). (default: `ANY`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_list_images", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "tag_status": "ANY"}) ``` --- ### `put_lifecycle_policy` Set the lifecycle policy for a repository | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository. | | `lifecycle_policy_text` | `string` | Yes | JSON lifecycle policy document as a string. | **Returns:** `ECRLifecyclePolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_put_lifecycle_policy", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "lifecycle_policy_text": "your-lifecycle_policy_text"}) ``` --- ### `set_repository_policy` Set the repository policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repository_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the repository. | | `policy_text` | `string` | Yes | JSON policy document as a string. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_set_repository_policy", {"repository_name": "your-repository_name", "policy_text": "your-policy_text"}) ``` --- ### `tag_resource` Add tags to an ECR resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the ECR resource to tag. | | `tags` | `array` | Yes | List of tag dicts, each with Key and Value. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_tag_resource", {"resource_arn": "your-resource_arn", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `untag_resource` Remove tags from an ECR resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the ECR resource to untag. | | `tag_keys` | `array` | Yes | List of tag keys to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ecr_untag_resource", {"resource_arn": "your-resource_arn", "tag_keys": "your-tag_keys"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_delete_image`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is 100 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`batch_delete_image`, `delete_repository`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

ecs.readme_md: # AWS ECS > Run and manage Docker containers at scale ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Compute-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/ecs](https://aws.amazon.com/ecs/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonECS/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 20 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use (Fargate: per vCPU/GB-hour) | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[ecs]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_ECS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["ecs"], credentials={"ecs": "your-token"}) # List tasks in a cluster result = kit.execute("ecs_list_tasks", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "service_name": "your-service_name", "desired_status": "RUNNING"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["ecs"], credentials={"ecs": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["ecs"], credentials={"ecs": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS ECS](https://aws.amazon.com/ecs/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("ecs_list_tasks", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_cluster` Create an ECS cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster_name` | `string` | Yes | The name of the cluster to create. | | `capacity_providers` | `array` | No | List of capacity provider names to associate. | | `settings` | `array` | No | Cluster settings (e.g. containerInsights). | **Returns:** `ECSCluster` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_create_cluster", {"cluster_name": "your-cluster_name", "capacity_providers": "your-capacity_providers", "settings": "your-settings"}) ``` --- ### `create_service` Create an ECS service | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `service_name` | `string` | Yes | The name of the service. | | `task_definition` | `string` | Yes | The task definition family:revision or ARN. | | `desired_count` | `integer` | No | Number of task instances to run. (default: `1`) | | `launch_type` | `string` | No | Launch type (FARGATE or EC2). (default: `FARGATE`) | | `network_configuration` | `object` | No | Network configuration for awsvpc mode. | | `load_balancers` | `array` | No | List of load balancer configurations. | **Returns:** `ECSService` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_create_service", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "service_name": "your-service_name", "task_definition": "your-task_definition", "desired_count": 1, "launch_type": "FARGATE"}) ``` --- ### `delete_cluster` :warning: Delete an ECS cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN to delete. | **Returns:** `ECSCluster` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_delete_cluster", {"cluster": "your-cluster"}) ``` --- ### `delete_service` :warning: Delete an ECS service | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `service` | `string` | Yes | The service name or ARN. | | `force` | `boolean` | No | Force deletion even if the service has running tasks. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `ECSService` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_delete_service", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "service": "your-service", "force": False}) ``` --- ### `deregister_task_definition` Deregister a task definition | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_definition` | `string` | Yes | The task definition family:revision or ARN. | **Returns:** `ECSTaskDefinition` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_deregister_task_definition", {"task_definition": "your-task_definition"}) ``` --- ### `describe_clusters` Describe ECS clusters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `clusters` | `array` | Yes | List of cluster names or ARNs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_describe_clusters", {"clusters": "your-clusters"}) ``` --- ### `describe_container_instances` Describe container instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `container_instances` | `array` | Yes | List of container instance IDs or ARNs. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_describe_container_instances", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "container_instances": "your-container_instances"}) ``` --- ### `describe_services` Describe ECS services | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `services` | `array` | Yes | List of service names or ARNs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_describe_services", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "services": "your-services"}) ``` --- ### `describe_task_definition` Describe a task definition | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `task_definition` | `string` | Yes | The task definition family:revision or ARN. | **Returns:** `ECSTaskDefinition` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_describe_task_definition", {"task_definition": "your-task_definition"}) ``` --- ### `describe_tasks` Describe tasks | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `tasks` | `array` | Yes | List of task IDs or ARNs to describe. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_describe_tasks", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "tasks": "your-tasks"}) ``` --- ### `list_account_settings` List account-level ECS settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `effective_settings` | `boolean` | No | Whether to include effective settings. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_account_settings", {"effective_settings": True}) ``` --- ### `list_clusters` List ECS clusters **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_clusters", {}) ``` --- ### `list_container_instances` List container instances in a cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (ACTIVE, DRAINING). (default: `ACTIVE`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_container_instances", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "status": "ACTIVE"}) ``` --- ### `list_services` List ECS services in a cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `launch_type` | `string` | No | Filter by launch type (FARGATE or EC2). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_services", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "launch_type": "your-launch_type"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags_for_resource` List tags for an ECS resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the resource. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_tags_for_resource", {"resource_arn": "your-resource_arn"}) ``` --- ### `list_task_definition_families` List task definition families | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `family_prefix` | `string` | No | Filter families by name prefix. (default: ``) | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (ACTIVE, INACTIVE, ALL). (default: `ACTIVE`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_task_definition_families", {"family_prefix": "", "status": "ACTIVE"}) ``` --- ### `list_task_definitions` List task definition revisions | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `family_prefix` | `string` | No | Filter by family name prefix. (default: ``) | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (ACTIVE, INACTIVE). (default: `ACTIVE`) | | `sort` | `string` | No | Sort order for results (ASC or DESC). (default: `DESC`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_task_definitions", {"family_prefix": "", "status": "ACTIVE"}) ``` --- ### `list_tasks` List tasks in a cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `service_name` | `string` | No | Filter tasks by service name. | | `desired_status` | `string` | No | Filter by desired status (RUNNING or STOPPED). (default: `RUNNING`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_list_tasks", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "service_name": "your-service_name", "desired_status": "RUNNING"}) ``` --- ### `put_cluster_capacity_providers` Put cluster capacity providers | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `capacity_providers` | `array` | Yes | List of capacity provider names. | | `default_capacity_provider_strategy` | `array` | Yes | Default strategy dicts. | **Returns:** `ECSCluster` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_put_cluster_capacity_providers", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "capacity_providers": "your-capacity_providers", "default_capacity_provider_strategy": "your-default_capacity_provider_strategy"}) ``` --- ### `register_task_definition` Register a new task definition | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `family` | `string` | Yes | The family name for the task definition. | | `container_definitions` | `array` | Yes | List of container definition dicts. | | `cpu` | `string` | No | CPU units for the task (e.g. 256, 512, 1024). (default: `256`) | | `memory` | `string` | No | Memory in MiB for the task (e.g. 512, 1024). (default: `512`) | | `network_mode` | `string` | No | Docker networking mode (awsvpc, bridge, host, none). (default: `awsvpc`) | | `requires_compatibilities` | `array` | No | Launch type compatibilities (FARGATE, EC2). | | `execution_role_arn` | `string` | No | ARN of the task execution IAM role. (default: ``) | | `task_role_arn` | `string` | No | ARN of the task IAM role for container permissions. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `ECSTaskDefinition` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_register_task_definition", {"family": "your-family", "container_definitions": "your-container_definitions", "cpu": "256", "memory": "512"}) ``` --- ### `run_task` Run a standalone task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `task_definition` | `string` | Yes | The task definition family:revision or ARN. | | `count` | `integer` | No | Number of task instances to run (1-10). (default: `1`) | | `launch_type` | `string` | No | Launch type (FARGATE or EC2). (default: `FARGATE`) | | `network_configuration` | `object` | No | Network configuration for awsvpc mode. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_run_task", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "task_definition": "your-task_definition", "count": 1, "launch_type": "FARGATE"}) ``` --- ### `stop_task` :warning: Stop a running task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `task` | `string` | Yes | The task ID or ARN. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Reason for stopping the task. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `ECSTask` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_stop_task", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "task": "your-task", "reason": ""}) ``` --- ### `tag_resource` Add tags to an ECS resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the resource to tag. | | `tags` | `array` | Yes | List of tag dicts with 'key' and 'value'. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_tag_resource", {"resource_arn": "your-resource_arn", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `update_cluster_settings` Update cluster settings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `settings` | `array` | Yes | List of cluster setting dicts (name/value pairs). | **Returns:** `ECSCluster` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_update_cluster_settings", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "settings": "your-settings"}) ``` --- ### `update_service` Update an ECS service | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `cluster` | `string` | Yes | The cluster name or ARN. | | `service` | `string` | Yes | The service name or ARN. | | `task_definition` | `string` | No | New task definition family:revision or ARN. | | `desired_count` | `integer` | No | New desired count of running tasks. | | `force_new_deployment` | `boolean` | No | Force a new deployment of the service. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `ECSService` ```python result = kit.execute("ecs_update_service", {"cluster": "your-cluster", "service": "your-service", "task_definition": "your-task_definition", "desired_count": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 20 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_cluster`, `delete_service`, `stop_task`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 25 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Vercel](../vercel/) — Frontend deployment --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:

    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
