figma.readme_md: # Figma > Collaborative design and prototyping tool ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-22-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Figma Inc. (Adobe) | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [figma.com](https://figma.com) | | **API Docs** | [www.figma.com](https://www.figma.com/developers/api) | | **Auth** | Personal Access Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 30 requests/minute per token | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Professional from $15/editor/month | --- ## Overview The Figma API provides access to files, components, styles, comments, and project metadata. Extract design tokens, automate asset export, manage team libraries, and build design-to-code workflows. ## Use Cases - Design token extraction - Automated asset export - Design system management - Comment and review automation - Design analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[figma]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_FIGMA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["figma"], credentials={"figma": "your-token"}) # List projects for a Figma team result = kit.execute("figma_list_projects", {"team_id": "T01234567"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["figma"], credentials={"figma": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["figma"], credentials={"figma": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Personal Access Token 1. Create an account at [Figma](https://figma.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://www.figma.com/developers/api#access-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("figma_list_projects", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The team ID to attach the webhook to. | | `event_type` | `string` | Yes | Event type (e.g., 'FILE_UPDATE', 'FILE_COMMENT', 'FILE_VERSION_UPDATE', 'LIBRARY_PUBLISH'). | | `endpoint` | `string` | Yes | URL to receive webhook notifications. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional description. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_create_webhook", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "event_type": "your-event_type", "endpoint": "your-endpoint", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The comment ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_delete_comment", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` :warning: Delete a webhook | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `webhook_id` | `string` | Yes | The webhook ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_delete_webhook", {"webhook_id": "your-webhook_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_comments` Get comments on a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaComment]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_comments", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_component` Get a specific component from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `component_id` | `string` | Yes | The node ID of the component. | **Returns:** `FigmaComponent` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_component", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "component_id": "your-component_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_component_set` Get component sets from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_component_set", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_file` Get a Figma file by key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `FigmaFile` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_nodes` Get specific nodes from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | | `ids` | `array` | Yes | List of node IDs to retrieve. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file_nodes", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "ids": "your-ids"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_pages` Get pages in a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file_pages", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_styles` List styles in a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_file_styles", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_image` Export images from a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `ids` | `string` | Yes | Comma-separated list of node IDs to render. | | `format` | `string` | No | Image format (``jpg``, ``png``, ``svg``, ``pdf``). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaImage]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_image", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "ids": "your-ids", "format": "your-format"}) ``` --- ### `get_local_variables` Get local variables in a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_local_variables", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_published_variables` Get published variables in a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The Figma file key. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_published_variables", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_team_components` List published components in a team library | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of results per page (max 30). (default: `30`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaComponent]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_team_components", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "page_size": 30, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `get_team_styles` List published styles in a team library | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of results per page (max 30). (default: `30`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaStyle]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_get_team_styles", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "page_size": 30, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_components` List components in a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaComponent]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_components", {"file_key": "your-file_key"}) ``` --- ### `list_file_versions` List version history for a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of versions to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaVersion]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_file_versions", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_project_files` List files in a Figma project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma project ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaProjectFile]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_project_files", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List projects for a Figma team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FigmaProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_projects", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_team_projects` List projects in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The Figma team ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of projects to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_team_projects", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhooks | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The team ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_list_webhooks", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `post_comment` :warning: Post a comment on a Figma file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_key` | `string` | Yes | The file key from the Figma URL. | | `message` | `string` | Yes | The comment message text. | | `client_meta` | `object` | No | Optional positioning data (x, y, node_id, etc.). | **Returns:** `FigmaComment` ```python result = kit.execute("figma_post_comment", {"file_key": "your-file_key", "message": "Hello!", "client_meta": "your-client_meta"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 30 requests/minute per token — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_webhook`, `delete_comment`, `delete_webhook`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses - This connector has 22 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gcalendar](../gcalendar/) — Calendar - [Gdocs](../gdocs/) — Documents - [Gsheets](../gsheets/) — Spreadsheets - [Gtasks](../gtasks/) — Task lists --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

firestore.readme_md: # Firebase Firestore > Serverless NoSQL document database ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-17-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/firebase/firebase-admin-python?style=social)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-node) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/firebase/firebase-admin-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google (Firebase) | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [firebase.google.com/products/firestore](https://firebase.google.com/products/firestore) | | **API Docs** | [firebase.google.com](https://firebase.google.com/docs/firestore/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Firebase Auth), Service Account | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan | | **Pricing** | Free tier (Spark), Pay-as-you-go (Blaze) | --- ## Overview The Firestore REST API provides access to documents and collections in your Firebase Firestore database. Query data with filters and ordering, perform batch operations, manage indexes, and build real-time applications. ## Use Cases - Document storage and retrieval - Real-time data sync - Serverless backend - Mobile app data layer - IoT data collection ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[firestore]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_FIRESTORE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["firestore"], credentials={"firestore": "your-token"}) # Get a Firestore document by ID result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document", {"project": "my-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "doc-123"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["firestore"], credentials={"firestore": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["firestore"], credentials={"firestore": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Firebase Auth) 1. Create an account at [Firebase Firestore](https://firebase.google.com/products/firestore) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.firebase.google.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `batch_get` Batch get multiple documents | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of document IDs to retrieve. | | `database` | `string` | No | Database ID (default: '(default)'). (default: `(default)`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_batch_get", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_ids": "your-document_ids", "database": "(default)"}) ``` --- ### `batch_write` Execute a batch write in Firestore | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `writes` | `array` | Yes | List of write operation dicts. | **Returns:** `FirestoreBatchWriteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_batch_write", {"project": "your-project", "writes": "your-writes"}) ``` --- ### `count_documents` Count documents in a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `filter` | `array` | No | Optional list of field filter dicts (same format as the ``query`` action's ``where`` parameter). | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_count_documents", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `create_collection_group_index` :warning: Create a collection group index in Firestore | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection_group` | `string` | Yes | The collection group ID. | | `fields` | `array` | Yes | List of index field configurations. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_create_collection_group_index", {"project": "your-project", "collection_group": "your-collection_group", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `create_document` Create a document in a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to Python-native values. | | `document_id` | `string` | No | Optional document ID; auto-generated if omitted. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_create_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "fields": "your-fields", "document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_document` :warning: Delete a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_delete_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_index` :warning: Delete a composite index | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database ID (usually '(default)'). | | `index_id` | `string` | Yes | The index ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_delete_index", {"project": "your-project", "database": "your-database", "index_id": "your-index_id"}) ``` --- ### `export_documents` Export documents from a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | The collection path. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_export_documents", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection"}) ``` --- ### `get_document` Get a Firestore document by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_document_field` Get a single field value from a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID. | | `field_path` | `string` | Yes | Dot-separated field path (e.g. ``"address.city"``). | **Returns:** `typing.Any` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_get_document_field", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id", "field_path": "your-field_path"}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List collections in a Firestore project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_list_collections", {"project": "your-project"}) ``` --- ### `list_documents` List documents in a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum documents per page. (default: `100`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token from a previous response for next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FirestoreDocument]` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_list_documents", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "limit": 100, "page_token": "your-page_token"}) ``` --- ### `list_indexes` List indexes for a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | The collection group ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_list_indexes", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection"}) ``` --- ### `query` Run a structured query on a Firestore collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `where` | `array` | No | List of field filter dicts. | | `order_by` | `array` | No | List of ``{"field": "name", "direction": "ASCENDING"}`` dicts. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of documents to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_query", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "where": "your-where", "order_by": "your-order_by"}) ``` --- ### `run_transaction` Run a transaction with multiple operations | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | Google Cloud project ID. | | `operations` | `array` | Yes | List of write operation dicts. Each should have either ``"update"`` or ``"delete"`` keys. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_run_transaction", {"project": "your-project", "operations": "your-operations"}) ``` --- ### `update_document` Update a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID to update. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to new values. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_update_document", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `update_fields` Update specific fields in a Firestore document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project` | `string` | Yes | GCP project ID. | | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | Document ID to update. | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to new Python-native values. | | `update_mask` | `array` | No | Explicit list of field paths to update. Defaults to the keys of ``fields``. | **Returns:** `FirestoreDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("firestore_update_fields", {"project": "your-project", "collection": "my-collection", "document_id": "your-document_id", "fields": "your-fields", "update_mask": "your-update_mask"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_get`, `batch_write`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is Varies by plan — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_collection_group_index`, `delete_document`, `delete_index`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

freshdesk.readme_md: # Freshdesk > Customer support and helpdesk platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-23-purple) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Freshworks Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [freshdesk.com](https://freshdesk.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.freshdesk.com](https://developers.freshdesk.com/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan (min 50 requests/minute) | | **Pricing** | Free up to 10 agents, Growth from $15/agent/month | --- ## Overview The Freshdesk API provides access to tickets, contacts, agents, companies, and knowledge base articles. Manage support workflows, automate ticket routing, track SLAs, and build custom helpdesk integrations. ## Use Cases - Ticket management - Customer support automation - Knowledge base management - SLA tracking - Multi-channel support ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[freshdesk]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_FRESHDESK_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["freshdesk"], credentials={"freshdesk": "your-token"}) # List contacts result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_contacts", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["freshdesk"], credentials={"freshdesk": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["freshdesk"], credentials={"freshdesk": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [Freshdesk](https://freshdesk.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://support.freshdesk.com/en/support/solutions/articles/215517) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_note` Add a note to a ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Note body (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `FreshdeskNote` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_add_note", {"ticket_id": 10, "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_company` :warning: Create a new company | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Company name. | | `domains` | `array` | No | List of email domains associated with the company. | | `description` | `string` | No | Company description. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskCompany` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_create_company", {"name": "my-name", "domains": "your-domains", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Contact's full name. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Contact's email address. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskContact` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_create_contact", {"name": "my-name", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_ticket` :warning: Create a new support ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Ticket subject line. | | `description` | `string` | Yes | Ticket body (HTML allowed). | | `email` | `string` | Yes | Requester email address. | | `priority` | `integer` | No | Priority (1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Urgent). (default: `1`) | | `status` | `integer` | No | Initial status (2=Open, 3=Pending, 4=Resolved, 5=Closed). (default: `2`) | **Returns:** `FreshdeskTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_create_ticket", {"subject": "Hello", "description": "your-description", "email": "user@example.com", "priority": 1, "status": 2}) ``` --- ### `delete_ticket` :warning: Delete a Freshdesk ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_delete_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_agent` Get a single agent by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `agent_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk agent ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskAgent` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_agent", {"agent_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_company` Get a single company by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk company ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskCompany` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_company", {"company_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_contact` Get a single contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk contact ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskContact` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_contact", {"contact_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_ticket` Get a single ticket by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_get_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_agents` List agents in Freshdesk | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of agents to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_agents", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_canned_responses` List canned responses **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_canned_responses", {}) ``` --- ### `list_companies` List companies in Freshdesk | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of companies per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskCompany]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_companies", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List contacts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of contacts per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_contacts", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) ``` --- ### `list_email_configs` List email configurations **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_email_configs", {}) ``` --- ### `list_groups` List agent groups in Freshdesk **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_groups", {}) ``` --- ### `list_satisfaction_ratings` List satisfaction ratings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of ratings per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[dict[str, Any]]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_satisfaction_ratings", {"limit": 30, "page": 1}) ``` --- ### `list_ticket_fields` List ticket fields in Freshdesk **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_ticket_fields", {}) ``` --- ### `list_tickets` List support tickets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status` | `integer` | No | Filter by status (2=Open, 3=Pending, 4=Resolved, 5=Closed). | | `priority` | `integer` | No | Filter by priority (1=Low, 2=Medium, 3=High, 4=Urgent). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Number of tickets per page (max 100). (default: `30`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-indexed). (default: `1`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskTicket]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_list_tickets", {"status": 10, "priority": 10}) ``` --- ### `merge_tickets` :warning: Merge multiple tickets into one | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `primary_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ticket ID that will remain open. | | `secondary_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of ticket IDs to merge into the primary. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_merge_tickets", {"primary_id": 10, "secondary_ids": "your-secondary_ids"}) ``` --- ### `reply_to_ticket` :warning: Reply to a ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Reply body (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `FreshdeskReply` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_reply_to_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10, "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `search_tickets` Search tickets using Freshdesk query language | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Freshdesk search query string. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[FreshdeskTicket]` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_search_tickets", {"query": "is:unread"}) ``` --- ### `update_contact` Update an existing contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk contact ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name for the contact. | | `email` | `string` | No | New email for the contact. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskContact` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_update_contact", {"contact_id": 10, "name": "my-name", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `update_ticket` Update an existing ticket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `ticket_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Freshdesk ticket ID. | | `status` | `integer` | No | New status value. | | `priority` | `integer` | No | New priority value. | **Returns:** `FreshdeskTicket` ```python result = kit.execute("freshdesk_update_ticket", {"ticket_id": 10, "status": 10, "priority": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_tickets` for filtered queries and `list_agents` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by plan (min 50 requests/minute) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_company`, `create_contact`, `create_ticket`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 23 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Salesforce](../salesforce/) — Enterprise CRM - [Hubspot](../hubspot/) — Marketing & sales CRM - [Intercom](../intercom/) — Customer messaging - [Zendesk](../zendesk/) — Support ticketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gcalendar.readme_md: # Google Calendar > Events + calendars + ACLs + free/busy via the Calendar API v3 ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google LLC | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **API Version** | v3 | | **Website** | [calendar.google.com](https://calendar.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com/calendar/api](https://developers.google.com/calendar/api/v3/reference) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0 bearer token (`ya29.…`) | | **Rate Limit** | 600 req/min per project — connector throttle: 600/min | | **Pricing** | Free with Google account | --- ## Overview 20 actions covering: event lifecycle (CRUD + quick-add + recurring instances + move-between-calendars), calendar management (create / update / delete / clear / subscribe / unsubscribe), ACL rules (list / add / remove for granting calendar access to other users), free/busy queries across calendars, and color enumeration. ## Use Cases - Meeting scheduling automation - Calendar sync (external system → Google Calendar) - Free/busy lookup for scheduling assistants - Shared calendar provisioning - Event reminders and notifications ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gcalendar]" ``` ## Credentials ```python # Programmatic kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"], credentials={"gcalendar": "ya29.your_token"}) # Env (any one wins) # export TC_GCALENDAR_CREDENTIALS=ya29.… # export TC_GCALENDAR_TOKEN=ya29.… kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"]) ``` Access token needs at least `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` scope. See [Credentials Guide](../../../docs/guides/credentials.md). ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"], credentials={"gcalendar": "ya29.…"}) # Create an event 7 days from now start = (datetime.now(timezone.utc) + timedelta(days=7)).replace(microsecond=0) evt = kit.execute("gcalendar_create_event", { "summary": "Quarterly review", "start": start.isoformat(), "end": (start + timedelta(hours=1)).isoformat(), "calendar_id": "primary", "description": "Agenda…", "attendees": ["alice@example.com"], "send_updates": "all", # email invites }) print(evt["id"], evt["html_link"]) # Free/busy lookup busy = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_free_busy", { "calendar_ids": ["primary"], "time_min": start.isoformat(), "time_max": (start + timedelta(days=1)).isoformat(), }) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit.serve_mcp() ``` ## Authentication Same paths as the other Google Workspace connectors: ### Path 1 — OAuth Playground (fastest) 1. https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground 2. Step 1: paste `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` → **Authorize APIs** 3. Sign in + consent → Step 2: **Exchange authorization code for tokens** → copy `access_token` ### Path 2 — gcloud CLI ```bash gcloud auth application-default login --scopes=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar gcloud auth application-default print-access-token ``` ## Required scope | Action group | Minimum scope | |---|---| | All reads (8: `list_events` / `get_event` / `list_event_instances` / `list_calendars` / `list_calendar_acl` / `get_free_busy` / `get_colors` / `move_event`) | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.readonly` (note: `move_event` actually requires write on both calendars) | | All 12 mutating actions | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar` | ## Error Handling | Typed exception | HTTP | When | |---|---|---| | `InvalidCredentialsError` | 401 | Access token expired/revoked | | `PermissionDeniedError` | 403 | Token lacks `calendar` scope OR not invited to the calendar | | `NotFoundError` | 404 | event_id / calendar_id doesn't exist | | `APIError` | 410 | Event was deleted (Google returns 410 Gone for already-deleted events) | | `ValidationError` | 400/422 | Bad RRULE, bad date/time, malformed body | | `RateLimitError` | 429 | Per-project quota exhausted | | `ServerError` | 5xx | Google-side outage | | `ConnectionError` / `TimeoutError` / `TransportError` | n/a | Network-layer failures (typed wrappers) | ### Path-traversal protection Calendar IDs (commonly email addresses like `alice@example.com` and the synthetic `c_…@group.calendar.google.com` for secondary calendars) + event IDs + ACL rule IDs are percent-encoded via the `_p()` helper before URL interpolation. Pinned by `test_calendar_id_with_slash_percent_encoded` + `test_event_with_email_calendar_id`. ## Verification Status All 20 actions verified — **17 Live verified** + **3 Probe-skipped intentionally** (would mutate the user's persistent `calendarList` or require touching the primary calendar destructively): | Live verified (17) | Probe-skipped (3) | |---|---| | `list_calendars`, `create_calendar`, `update_calendar`, `delete_calendar`, `list_events`, `get_event`, `create_event`, `update_event`, `delete_event`, `quick_add_event`, `list_event_instances` (real recurring event with `RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;COUNT=3`), `move_event`, `get_free_busy`, `list_calendar_acl`, `add_calendar_acl`, `remove_calendar_acl`, `get_colors` | `subscribe_calendar` / `unsubscribe_calendar` (would alter user's persistent calendarList view), `clear_calendar` (only valid on the primary calendar — would erase user's real events) | End-to-end live run on 2026-05-28 against `www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3` with a real OAuth 2.0 access token: created throwaway secondary calendar → created/updated/got/deleted events (with unicode + emoji round-trip `你好 🚀`) → seeded a recurring event and verified 3 instances → exercised ACL CRUD → moved an event between calendars → cleaned up (throwaway calendar deleted via Calendar API DELETE, HTTP 204). Zero leftover artifacts. **42 respx unit tests** pin request/response shapes across 5 rounds (happy path × 20 actions, defensive parsing, URL-path injection guards including the email-calendar-id round-trip and recurring-event RRULE parsing, error matrix including 410 Gone, transport errors, MCP exposure, OpenAI schema sweep, dangerous-flag audit, sync wrappers, concurrency). | Action | Endpoint | Status | |---|---|---| | `list_events` | `GET /v3/calendars/{id}/events` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_event` | `GET /v3/calendars/{id}/events/{eventId}` | ✅ Live verified | | `create_event` | `POST /v3/calendars/{id}/events` | ✅ Live verified | | `update_event` | `PATCH /v3/calendars/{id}/events/{eventId}` | ✅ Live verified | | `delete_event` | `DELETE /v3/calendars/{id}/events/{eventId}` | ✅ Live verified | | `quick_add_event` | `POST /v3/calendars/{id}/events/quickAdd?text=…` | ✅ Live verified | | `list_event_instances` | `GET /v3/calendars/{id}/events/{eventId}/instances` | ✅ Live verified (RRULE) | | `move_event` | `POST /v3/calendars/{src}/events/{eventId}/move?destination=…` | ✅ Live verified | | `list_calendars` | `GET /v3/users/me/calendarList` | ✅ Live verified | | `create_calendar` | `POST /v3/calendars` | ✅ Live verified | | `update_calendar` | `GET + PUT /v3/calendars/{id}` (PUT requires full body) | ✅ Live verified | | `delete_calendar` | `DELETE /v3/calendars/{id}` | ✅ Live verified | | `clear_calendar` | `POST /v3/calendars/{id}/clear` | Probe-skipped | | `subscribe_calendar` | `POST /v3/users/me/calendarList` | Probe-skipped | | `unsubscribe_calendar` | `DELETE /v3/users/me/calendarList/{id}` | Probe-skipped | | `list_calendar_acl` | `GET /v3/calendars/{id}/acl` | ✅ Live verified | | `add_calendar_acl` | `POST /v3/calendars/{id}/acl` | ✅ Live verified | | `remove_calendar_acl` | `DELETE /v3/calendars/{id}/acl/{ruleId}` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_free_busy` | `POST /v3/freeBusy` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_colors` | `GET /v3/colors` | ✅ Live verified | ## Actions ### `add_calendar_acl` :warning: Add an access-control rule to a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to share. | | `email` | `string` | Yes | The email address of the user to grant access to. | | `role` | `string` | No | The access level: 'none', 'freeBusyReader', 'reader', 'writer', or 'owner'. (default: `reader`) | **Returns:** `CalendarACL` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_add_calendar_acl", {"calendar_id": "primary", "email": "user@example.com", "role": "reader"}) ``` --- ### `clear_calendar` :warning: Clear all events from a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to clear. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_clear_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `create_calendar` :warning: Create a new calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `summary` | `string` | Yes | Title of the new calendar. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional calendar description. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | IANA timezone (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the authenticated user's timezone. | **Returns:** `Calendar` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_create_calendar", {"summary": "your-summary", "description": "your-description", "time_zone": "your-time_zone"}) ``` --- ### `create_event` :warning: Create a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `summary` | `string` | Yes | Event title/summary. | | `start` | `string` | Yes | Start time as ISO 8601 string (date or datetime). | | `end` | `string` | Yes | End time as ISO 8601 string (date or datetime). | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional event description (supports HTML). | | `location` | `string` | No | Optional event location. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | IANA timezone (e.g., 'America/New_York'). Defaults to the calendar's timezone. | | `attendees` | `array` | No | Optional list of attendee email addresses. | | `send_updates` | `string` | No | Notification policy: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. (default: `none`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_create_event", {"summary": "your-summary", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end", "calendar_id": "primary", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_calendar` :warning: Delete a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_delete_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `delete_event` :warning: Delete a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the event to delete. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `send_updates` | `string` | No | Notification policy: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. (default: `none`) | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_delete_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id", "calendar_id": "primary", "send_updates": "none"}) ``` --- ### `get_colors` Get available calendar and event colors **Returns:** `CalendarColors` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_colors", {}) ``` --- ### `get_event` Get a single event by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the event to retrieve. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id", "calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `get_free_busy` Query free/busy information for calendars | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `time_min` | `string` | Yes | Start of the time range (ISO 8601 datetime). | | `time_max` | `string` | Yes | End of the time range (ISO 8601 datetime). | | `calendar_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of calendar IDs to query. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_get_free_busy", {"time_min": "your-time_min", "time_max": "your-time_max", "calendar_ids": "your-calendar_ids"}) ``` --- ### `list_calendar_acl` List access-control rules for a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_calendar_acl", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `list_calendars` List calendars | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page. | | `max_results` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of calendars per page (max 250). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Calendar]` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_calendars", {"page_token": "your-page_token", "max_results": 100}) ``` --- ### `list_event_instances` List instances of a recurring event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | Calendar ID containing the event. | | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The recurring event ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_event_instances", {"calendar_id": "primary", "event_id": "your-event_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_events` List calendar events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `time_min` | `string` | No | Lower bound (inclusive) as RFC 3339 timestamp. | | `time_max` | `string` | No | Upper bound (exclusive) as RFC 3339 timestamp. | | `query` | `string` | No | Free-text search query across event fields. | | `max_results` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events per page (max 2500). (default: `25`) | | `single_events` | `boolean` | No | If True, expand recurring events into instances. (default: `True`) | | `order_by` | `string` | No | Sort order: 'startTime' (requires single_events=True) or 'updated'. (default: `startTime`) | | `page_token` | `string` | No | Token for fetching the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[CalendarEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_list_events", {"calendar_id": "primary", "time_min": "your-time_min"}) ``` --- ### `move_event` Move an event to a different calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | Source calendar ID. | | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The event ID to move. | | `destination_calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | Destination calendar ID. | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_move_event", {"calendar_id": "primary", "event_id": "your-event_id", "destination_calendar_id": "your-destination_calendar_id"}) ``` --- ### `quick_add_event` :warning: Quick-add an event using natural language | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `text` | `string` | Yes | Natural language description of the event. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_quick_add_event", {"text": "Hello!", "calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `remove_calendar_acl` :warning: Remove an access-control rule from a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar. | | `rule_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the ACL rule to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_remove_calendar_acl", {"calendar_id": "primary", "rule_id": "your-rule_id"}) ``` --- ### `subscribe_calendar` :warning: Subscribe to an external calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to subscribe to. | **Returns:** `Calendar` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_subscribe_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `unsubscribe_calendar` :warning: Unsubscribe from a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to unsubscribe from. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_unsubscribe_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary"}) ``` --- ### `update_calendar` :warning: Update a calendar | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `calendar_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the calendar to update. | | `summary` | `string` | No | New calendar title. | | `description` | `string` | No | New calendar description. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | New IANA timezone. | **Returns:** `Calendar` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_update_calendar", {"calendar_id": "primary", "summary": "your-summary", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `update_event` :warning: Update a calendar event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the event to update. | | `calendar_id` | `string` | No | Calendar ID ('primary' for the user's main calendar). (default: `primary`) | | `summary` | `string` | No | New event title/summary. | | `start` | `string` | No | New start time as ISO 8601 string. | | `end` | `string` | No | New end time as ISO 8601 string. | | `description` | `string` | No | New event description. | | `location` | `string` | No | New event location. | | `time_zone` | `string` | No | IANA timezone for start/end. | | `attendees` | `array` | No | Replace attendees with this list of email addresses. | | `send_updates` | `string` | No | Notification policy: 'all', 'externalOnly', or 'none'. (default: `none`) | **Returns:** `CalendarEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("gcalendar_update_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id", "calendar_id": "primary", "summary": "your-summary"}) ``` --- ## Tips - **Date vs DateTime**: `create_event(start="2026-06-01")` (10-char date) creates an all-day event; `create_event(start="2026-06-01T10:00:00-07:00")` creates a timed event. The connector detects the format automatically. - **Recurring events**: `recurrence` is a list of RFC 5545 RRULE strings (e.g. `"RRULE:FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE,FR;COUNT=10"`). Set via `batch_update` payload — the connector's `create_event` doesn't expose `recurrence` directly; use direct PATCH if you need it (the live verification covers this path). - **`send_updates` notification policy**: `"all"` emails everyone, `"externalOnly"` emails only non-organization attendees, `"none"` sends nothing. Default is `"none"` — safe for bulk operations. - **Time zones**: pass IANA timezone strings (e.g. `"America/New_York"`, `"UTC"`). If omitted, defaults to the calendar's timezone setting. - **`move_event` is NOT dangerous-flagged** — it relocates rather than destroys data. Available in `exclude_dangerous=True` mode. - **`clear_calendar` only works on the primary calendar** — and it deletes ALL events in your primary. Use with extreme care. - **Secondary calendar IDs look like `c_@group.calendar.google.com`**, returned from `create_calendar`. Save the ID; it's the only way to reference the calendar afterward (the summary is mutable). - **Dangerous actions**: 12 of 20. Use `kit = ToolKit(["gcalendar"], exclude_dangerous=True)` for agent-safe read-only mode (leaves 8 actions including `move_event`). ## Related Connectors - [Google Docs](../gdocs/) — document CRUD - [Google Sheets](../gsheets/) — spreadsheet CRUD - [Google Drive](../gdrive/) — file management --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

gdocs.readme_md: # Google Docs > Create + edit + extract text from Google Docs programmatically ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Productivity-F97316) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-5-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/googleapis/google-api-python-client?style=social)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/googleapis/google-api-python-client) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Google LLC | | **Category** | Productivity | | **Protocol** | REST | | **API Version** | v1 | | **Website** | [docs.google.com](https://docs.google.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.google.com/docs/api](https://developers.google.com/docs/api/reference/rest) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0 bearer token (`ya29.…`) | | **Rate Limit** | 300 write requests/minute · 3,000 read requests/minute (per project) — connector throttle: 300 req/min | | **Pricing** | Free with a Google account | --- ## Overview Five focused actions covering document CRUD + content read/write via the Google Docs REST API v1 at `docs.googleapis.com/v1`. Pairs naturally with the Drive connector for file management (move/share/delete) — gdocs handles content, gdrive handles container operations. ## Use Cases - Automated document generation (contracts, reports, invoices) - Template-based content workflows - Plain-text extraction from existing docs - Content sync from external systems - Agent-driven document editing ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[gdocs]" ``` ## Credentials Two equivalent ways to provide the token — same primitives every ToolsConnector connector uses: ```python # Programmatic kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "ya29.your_access_token"}) # Environment variable (any one of these; first match wins) # export TC_GDOCS_CREDENTIALS=ya29.… # preferred # export TC_GDOCS_API_KEY=ya29.… # export TC_GDOCS_TOKEN=ya29.… kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"]) # no credentials arg — resolved from env ``` The token must be a Google OAuth 2.0 access token with at least the `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents` scope. Access tokens are short-lived (~1 hour) — see the [Credentials Guide](../../../docs/guides/credentials.md) for refresh patterns + multi-account. ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "ya29.your_token"}) # Create a new document doc = kit.execute("gdocs_create_document", {"title": "Q1 Report"}) print(doc) # {"id": "1abc…", "title": "Q1 Report", ...} # Insert text at the beginning kit.execute("gdocs_insert_text", { "document_id": doc["id"], "text": "# Quarterly Highlights\n\n", }) # Read it back as plain text text = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document_text", {"document_id": doc["id"]}) print(text) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "ya29.…"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], credentials={"gdocs": "ya29.…"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication The connector accepts any Google OAuth 2.0 access token format (the `ya29.…` prefix). Several paths to obtain one: ### Path 1 — OAuth 2.0 Playground (fastest, no install) 1. Go to https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground 2. Step 1: scroll to the bottom, paste the scope `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents` into the "Input your own scopes" box, click **Authorize APIs** 3. Sign in with your Google account → grant consent 4. Step 2: click **Exchange authorization code for tokens** 5. Copy the `access_token` value (starts with `ya29.…`) — valid ~1 hour ### Path 2 — gcloud CLI ```bash brew install --cask google-cloud-sdk # or platform equivalent gcloud auth application-default login --scopes=https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents gcloud auth application-default print-access-token ``` ### Path 3 — Your own OAuth flow For production deployments, run your own OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow with `client_id` + `client_secret` from Google Cloud Console. Refresh tokens via `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`. See [docs/guides/credentials.md](../../../docs/guides/credentials.md) for patterns. ### Path 4 — Service account For server-to-server (no end-user OAuth), use a service-account JSON key with domain-wide delegation. The connector accepts the resulting access token from the JWT-bearer flow. **Note**: service accounts can only access documents explicitly shared with them, or any document if domain-wide delegation is granted by a Workspace admin. [Get credentials →](https://developers.google.com/docs/api/quickstart) ## Required scope | Action | Minimum scope | |---|---| | `get_document`, `get_document_text` | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents.readonly` | | `create_document`, `batch_update`, `insert_text` | `https://www.googleapis.com/auth/documents` | `documents` (read+write) covers all five actions. `drive.file` is recommended additionally if you want to delete docs you created (the Docs API itself has no delete; use `DELETE /drive/v3/files/{id}`). ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import ( InvalidCredentialsError, PermissionDeniedError, NotFoundError, RateLimitError, ValidationError, ServerError, ConnectionError, TimeoutError, TransportError, ) try: text = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document_text", {"document_id": "..."}) except InvalidCredentialsError as e: print(f"Token expired or invalid (401): {e.suggestion}") except PermissionDeniedError as e: print(f"Token lacks the documents scope (403)") except NotFoundError: print("Document doesn't exist or isn't shared with this token") except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry after {e.retry_after_seconds}s") ``` | Typed exception | HTTP status | When it fires | |---|---|---| | `InvalidCredentialsError` | 401 | Access token expired (1-hour lifetime) or revoked. Refresh via your OAuth flow. | | `PermissionDeniedError` | 403 | Token doesn't carry the required `documents` scope, OR the document isn't shared with the OAuth user. | | `NotFoundError` | 404 | Document ID doesn't exist OR you don't have read access (Google returns 404 for both — same ambiguity as Drive). | | `ValidationError` | 400 / 422 | Bad request shape (e.g. malformed `requests` array in `batch_update`). | | `RateLimitError` | 429 | Per-project quota exhausted. `Retry-After` header populated. | | `ServerError` | 5xx | Google-side outage. Retry with backoff. | | `ConnectionError` / `TimeoutError` / `TransportError` | n/a | Network-layer failures — typed wrappers of `httpx.ConnectError` / `TimeoutException` / `TransportError`. | ### Path-traversal protection URL path segments (e.g. `{document_id}`) are percent-encoded via the `_p()` helper before f-string interpolation. Adversarial `document_id="../admin"` becomes `..%2Fadmin`, preserving the `/documents/` prefix. Pinned by `test_document_id_with_slash_percent_encoded`. ### Token redaction Google OAuth 2.0 access tokens (`ya29.…`) and Google API keys (`AIza…`) are redacted from error-body previews via the shared `_CREDENTIAL_PATTERNS` regex. A misbehaving upstream that echoes your token back in an error body will never see it land in `details["body_preview"]`. Same redaction also enforced at CI secret-scan + pre-commit hook level. ## Verification Status All 5 actions are **Live verified** — exercised end-to-end against `docs.googleapis.com` with a real OAuth 2.0 access token on 2026-05-28. The verification covered: - Document creation + metadata round-trip - Plain-text extraction (empty body → `"\n"`, populated body, unicode + emoji round-trip) - Single-request `insert_text` via the convenience wrapper - Multi-request `batch_update` - Real 404 → typed `NotFoundError` against the live API 26 respx unit tests pin the request/response shapes across 5 rounds: happy path, defensive parsing (table walk, empty body, unknown fields), URL-path injection guard, error matrix (401/403/404/429/500), transport errors, MCP exposure, OpenAI schema sweep, dangerous-flag audit, sync wrappers, lifecycle, concurrency. **MCP end-to-end verified**: subprocess `tc serve mcp gdocs --transport stdio` over JSON-RPC, real document created + read via MCP tool dispatch, clean shutdown. | Action | REST Endpoint | Status | |---|---|---| | `get_document` | `GET /v1/documents/{id}` | ✅ Live verified | | `create_document` | `POST /v1/documents` | ✅ Live verified | | `batch_update` | `POST /v1/documents/{id}:batchUpdate` | ✅ Live verified | | `insert_text` | `POST /v1/documents/{id}:batchUpdate` (insertText request) | ✅ Live verified | | `get_document_text` | `GET /v1/documents/{id}` → walk body content | ✅ Live verified | ## Actions ### `batch_update` :warning: Batch update a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document to update. | | `requests` | `array` | Yes | List of Docs API request objects. See `Google Docs API reference `_ for the full request specification. | **Returns:** `BatchUpdateResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_batch_update", {"document_id": "your-document_id", "requests": "your-requests"}) ``` --- ### `create_document` :warning: Create a new document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new document. | **Returns:** `Document` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_create_document", {"title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ### `get_document` Get a document by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document to retrieve. | **Returns:** `Document` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document", {"document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_document_text` Extract plain text from a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_get_document_text", {"document_id": "your-document_id"}) ``` --- ### `insert_text` :warning: Insert text into a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `document_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the document. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | The text content to insert. | | `index` | `integer` | No | The zero-based character index to insert at. If omitted, defaults to index 1 (start of document body, after the implicit newline). | **Returns:** `BatchUpdateResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("gdocs_insert_text", {"document_id": "your-document_id", "text": "Hello!", "index": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - **First call**: `create_document` with auto-generated title is the cheapest sanity check that the token has the `documents` scope. - **Doc deletion**: Google Docs API has no `DELETE /documents/{id}`. Use the Drive API instead: `DELETE /drive/v3/files/{id}` (requires `drive.file` scope at minimum). - **Body indexes**: Google Docs uses 1-based character indexes. Index `1` is the start of the body (after the implicit section break). Index `0` is invalid. - **`get_document` vs `get_document_text`**: the former returns metadata only (one API call, fast). The latter also fetches + walks the body to extract plain text (one API call, slower parse). Calling both means **two API calls** to the same document — fetch once and parse twice locally if you need both. - **Tables are walked**: `get_document_text` extracts text from inside table cells, not just top-level paragraphs. - **Custom verb suffix**: `:batchUpdate` is a Google REST custom-verb idiom, not a sub-resource. The connector's `_p()` percent-encoding wraps only `{document_id}`, leaving the literal `:batchUpdate` suffix intact. - **Dangerous actions**: `create_document`, `batch_update`, `insert_text` mutate state. Use `kit = ToolKit(["gdocs"], exclude_dangerous=True)` for agent-safe read-only mode. ## Related Connectors - [Google Drive](../gdrive/) — file management + sharing - [Google Sheets](../gsheets/) — spreadsheet CRUD - [Notion](../notion/) — alternative document platform --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
