huggingface.readme_md: # Hugging Face > Hosted model inference and Hub model/dataset search ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ai_Ml-7C3AED) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-30-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/huggingface/huggingface_hub?style=social)](https://github.com/huggingface/huggingface_hub) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/huggingface/huggingface_hub) [![JavaScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/JavaScript-SDK-ED8B00?logo=github)](https://github.com/huggingface/huggingface.js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Hugging Face | | **Category** | Ai Ml | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [huggingface.co](https://huggingface.co) | | **API Docs** | [huggingface.co/docs](https://huggingface.co/docs/inference-providers) | | **Auth** | API Key (user access token) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan (free Inference API is throttled) | | **Pricing** | Free tier + pay-as-you-go Inference | | **Verification** | ✅ Tier 1 — Live verified (24/30 actions, 2026-06-05; 6 generative/audio inference actions are partner-provider-dependent) | --- ## Overview The Hugging Face connector runs hosted model inference via the Inference Providers router (`router.huggingface.co`) — text generation, embeddings, summarization, translation, fill-mask, classification, zero-shot classification, question answering, table-QA, sentence similarity, and vision tasks — and browses model, dataset, and Space metadata via the Hub API (`huggingface.co/api`). Task inference posts to `/{provider}/models/{model}/pipeline/{task}`; the `provider` defaults to `hf-inference` (HF's serverless pool) and can be switched per call (e.g. `fal-ai`, `replicate`, `together`) to route heavier tasks elsewhere. Bring your own user access token (`hf_...`). > **Provider coverage.** The default `hf-inference` provider serves the lighter "traditional ML" tasks (chat, summarize, translate, fill-mask, classification, NER, zero-shot, QA, table-QA, embeddings, sentence-similarity, and image classification/detection/segmentation) — all **live verified** below. The heavier generative + audio tasks (`text_generation` of large LLMs, `text_to_image`, `text_to_speech`, `image_to_text`, `automatic_speech_recognition`, `audio_classification`) route to **partner providers**: pass `provider=` pointing at a provider that hosts your chosen model. For chat-style generation, prefer `chat_completion` (it auto-routes). Always use a model's **canonical, org-prefixed** repo ID for inference (e.g. `google-bert/bert-base-uncased`, not `bert-base-uncased`) — the router does not resolve legacy aliases the way the Hub API does. ## Use Cases - Text generation and summarization - Sentence embeddings for semantic search (single or batch) - Translation and fill-mask - Text and zero-shot classification - Model, dataset, and Space discovery on the Hub - **Model / provider / cost comparison** — list which providers serve a model, their routing status, context window, and per-token pricing (`get_model_providers`, `list_inference_catalog`) - **Model inspection** — parameter counts, config, model-card metadata, and the repo file tree (`get_model(expand=...)`, `list_repo_files`) ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[huggingface]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_HUGGINGFACE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["huggingface"], credentials={"huggingface": "your-token"}) # Search the Hub for text-classification models result = kit.execute("huggingface_list_models", {"filter": "text-classification", "limit": 5}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["huggingface"], credentials={"huggingface": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["huggingface"], credentials={"huggingface": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Settings 2. Access Tokens 3. New token (read scope is enough for inference + search) [Get credentials →](https://huggingface.co/settings/tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("huggingface_whoami", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Verification Status All 30 actions are pinned by **46 respx tests** in [tests/connectors/test_huggingface.py](../../../tests/connectors/test_huggingface.py): happy path per action, two-host routing (router vs Hub), pipeline-path pinning, custom-provider routing, heterogeneous inference shapes (list-of-dicts, list-of-lists, single dict, raw bytes), base64 binary inputs, optional-param omission, the error matrix (auth / rate-limit / not-found / validation / cold-start 503), and the MCP + OpenAI-schema sweeps. Regression tests cover the live-confirmed quirks fixed during verification (zero-shot router list shape, Hub legacy-alias redirect, batch-embedding union schema, and the discovery actions). **24 of 30 actions are Live verified** — exercised end-to-end against the real Hugging Face API on **2026-06-05** with a real user access token (`hf_...`), routed through the default `hf-inference` provider (Hub metadata, the model/provider/pricing discovery actions, chat, and the `hf-inference` task set). The remaining **6 are partner-provider-dependent**: they route correctly (the router forwards the request and returns a clean provider-level response), but `hf-inference` does not serve them — they require a partner provider (e.g. `fal-ai`, `replicate`, `together`) that hosts the chosen model, supplied via `provider=`. This is Hugging Face's multi-provider architecture, not a connector limitation. | Action | Task / Endpoint | Status | |---|---|---| | `whoami` | `GET /api/whoami-v2` | ✅ Live verified | | `list_models` | `GET /api/models` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_model` | `GET /api/models/{id}` (follows legacy-alias 307) | ✅ Live verified | | `list_datasets` | `GET /api/datasets` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_dataset` | `GET /api/datasets/{id}` (follows legacy-alias 307) | ✅ Live verified | | `list_spaces` | `GET /api/spaces` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_space` | `GET /api/spaces/{id}` | ✅ Live verified | | `get_model_providers` | `GET /api/models/{id}?expand[]=inferenceProviderMapping` | ✅ Live verified | | `list_repo_files` | `GET /api/{models,datasets,spaces}/{id}/tree/{rev}` | ✅ Live verified | | `list_inference_catalog` | `GET /v1/models` (providers + pricing) | ✅ Live verified | | `chat_completion` | `POST /v1/chat/completions` (auto-routes) | ✅ Live verified | | `summarize` | `…/pipeline/summarization` | ✅ Live verified | | `translate` | `…/pipeline/translation` | ✅ Live verified | | `fill_mask` | `…/pipeline/fill-mask` | ✅ Live verified | | `text_classification` | `…/pipeline/text-classification` | ✅ Live verified | | `token_classification` | `…/pipeline/token-classification` | ✅ Live verified | | `zero_shot_classification` | `…/pipeline/zero-shot-classification` | ✅ Live verified | | `question_answering` | `…/pipeline/question-answering` | ✅ Live verified | | `table_question_answering` | `…/pipeline/table-question-answering` | ✅ Live verified | | `feature_extraction` | `…/pipeline/feature-extraction` | ✅ Live verified | | `sentence_similarity` | `…/pipeline/sentence-similarity` | ✅ Live verified | | `image_classification` | `…/pipeline/image-classification` | ✅ Live verified | | `object_detection` | `…/pipeline/object-detection` | ✅ Live verified | | `image_segmentation` | `…/pipeline/image-segmentation` | ✅ Live verified | | `text_generation` | `…/pipeline/text-generation` | 🔌 Provider-dependent (large LLMs route to a partner; or use `chat_completion`) | | `text_to_image` | `…/pipeline/text-to-image` | 🔌 Provider-dependent (`provider="fal-ai"`, etc.) | | `text_to_speech` | `…/pipeline/text-to-speech` | 🔌 Provider-dependent | | `image_to_text` | `…/pipeline/image-to-text` | 🔌 Provider-dependent | | `automatic_speech_recognition` | `…/pipeline/automatic-speech-recognition` | 🔌 Provider-dependent | | `audio_classification` | `…/pipeline/audio-classification` | 🔌 Provider-dependent | Three gaps were found and fixed during the live verification + adversarial re-test sweep: (1) `zero_shot_classification` returned empty labels because the `hf-inference` router emits a score-sorted list of `{label, score}` rather than the classic `{sequence, labels, scores}` object — the parser now handles both; (2) `get_model`/`get_dataset` failed to parse because the Hub `307`-redirects legacy repo aliases (`bert-base-uncased` → `google-bert/bert-base-uncased`) and the client wasn't following redirects — it now does; (3) **batch embeddings** were impossible via the typed/MCP interface — `feature_extraction(inputs)` is now `Union[str, list[str]]`, and the schema generator renders multi-type unions as `anyOf` so a list passes validation instead of being rejected as "expects string" (a cross-cutting fix that also unblocked Mistral batch embeddings and Gemini structured `contents`). ## Actions ### `audio_classification` Classify audio into labels | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Audio-classification model repo ID (e.g. ``'superb/hubert-large-superb-er'``). | | `audio` | `string` | Yes | The audio to classify, as raw ``bytes`` or a base64-encoded string. | | `top_k` | `integer` | No | Limit the output to the top-k most probable classes. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_audio_classification", {"model": "your-model", "audio": "your-audio", "top_k": 10, "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `automatic_speech_recognition` Transcribe speech audio to text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | ASR model repo ID (e.g. ``'openai/whisper-large-v3'``). | | `audio` | `string` | Yes | The audio to transcribe, as raw ``bytes`` or a base64-encoded string. | | `return_timestamps` | `boolean` | No | If True, also return per-chunk timestamps. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `HFTranscription` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_automatic_speech_recognition", {"model": "your-model", "audio": "your-audio", "return_timestamps": True, "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `chat_completion` Create a chat completion via the OpenAI-compatible router | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model id, optionally with a routing suffix (e.g. ``'openai/gpt-oss-120b'`` or ``'...:fastest'``). | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | OpenAI-style message dicts with ``role`` and ``content`` keys. | | `temperature` | `number` | No | Sampling temperature between 0 and 2. | | `max_tokens` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `top_p` | `number` | No | Nucleus-sampling probability mass cutoff. | | `frequency_penalty` | `number` | No | Penalty in [-2, 2] for token frequency. | | `presence_penalty` | `number` | No | Penalty in [-2, 2] for token presence. | | `seed` | `integer` | No | Seed for deterministic sampling, when supported. | | `stop` | `array` | No | Up to 4 sequences at which generation stops. | | `tools` | `array` | No | Tool/function definitions the model may call. | | `tool_choice` | `string` | No | ``'auto'``, ``'none'``, ``'required'``, or a specific ``{"type": "function", ...}`` selector. | | `response_format` | `object` | No | Structured-output spec (e.g. ``{"type": "json_object"}`` or a ``json_schema`` block). | **Returns:** `HFChatCompletion` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_chat_completion", {"model": "your-model", "messages": "your-messages", "temperature": 10, "max_tokens": 10}) ``` --- ### `feature_extraction` Extract embedding vectors from text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Embedding model repo ID, e.g. ``'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2'``. | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to embed, or a list of texts to embed as a batch (one row per input, index-aligned). | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_feature_extraction", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `fill_mask` Fill a masked token in text with a hosted model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Fill-mask model repo ID (e.g. ``'google-bert/bert-base-uncased'``). Use the model's canonical (org-prefixed) ID -- the router does not resolve legacy aliases. | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | Text containing exactly one mask token -- ``[MASK]`` for BERT-family models, ```` for RoBERTa-family models. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_fill_mask", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `get_dataset` Get metadata for a dataset on the Hub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dataset_id` | `string` | Yes | Dataset repo ID (e.g. ``'squad'``). | **Returns:** `HFDatasetInfo` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_get_dataset", {"dataset_id": "your-dataset_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_model` Get metadata for a model on the Hub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | Model repo ID (e.g. ``'bert-base-uncased'``). Legacy aliases are resolved automatically (the client follows the Hub's redirect to the canonical id). | | `expand` | `array` | No | Optional list of extra fields to include, each requested as an ``expand[]`` query param. Useful values: ``'inferenceProviderMapping'`` (which providers serve the model), ``'safetensors'`` (parameter counts), ``'config'``, ``'cardData'`` (the model card front-matter), and ``'downloadsAllTime'``. These populate the matching (otherwise-``None``) fields on HFModelInfo. | **Returns:** `HFModelInfo` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_get_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id", "expand": "your-expand"}) ``` --- ### `get_model_providers` List the inference providers that serve a Hub model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | Model repo ID (e.g. ``'meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct'``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_get_model_providers", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_space` Get metadata for a Space on the Hub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `space_id` | `string` | Yes | Space repo ID (e.g. ``'huggingface/diffuse-the-rest'``). | **Returns:** `HFSpaceInfo` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_get_space", {"space_id": "your-space_id"}) ``` --- ### `image_classification` Classify an image into labels | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Image-classification model repo ID (e.g. ``'google/vit-base-patch16-224'``). | | `image` | `string` | Yes | The image to classify, as raw ``bytes`` or a base64-encoded string. | | `top_k` | `integer` | No | Limit the output to the top-k most probable classes. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_image_classification", {"model": "your-model", "image": "your-image", "top_k": 10, "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `image_segmentation` Segment an image into labelled masks | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Image-segmentation model repo ID (e.g. ``'facebook/mask2former-swin-large-coco-panoptic'``). | | `image` | `string` | Yes | The image to segment, as raw ``bytes`` or a base64-encoded string. | | `mask_threshold` | `number` | No | Threshold for binarising predicted masks. | | `threshold` | `number` | No | Probability threshold to filter predicted masks. | | `subtask` | `string` | No | Segmentation subtask -- ``'instance'``, ``'panoptic'``, or ``'semantic'``. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_image_segmentation", {"model": "your-model", "image": "your-image", "mask_threshold": 10, "threshold": 10}) ``` --- ### `image_to_text` Generate a caption for an image | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Image-to-text model repo ID (e.g. ``'Salesforce/blip-image-captioning-large'``). | | `image` | `string` | Yes | The image to caption, as raw ``bytes`` or a base64-encoded string. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_image_to_text", {"model": "your-model", "image": "your-image", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `list_datasets` Search datasets on the Hugging Face Hub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Free-text search query over dataset IDs. | | `author` | `string` | No | Restrict to a given author/organisation. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter tag (e.g. ``'task_categories:translation'``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Field to sort by (e.g. ``'downloads'``, ``'likes'``). | | `direction` | `integer` | No | Sort direction; ``-1`` for descending. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of datasets to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_list_datasets", {"search": "your-search", "author": "your-author"}) ``` --- ### `list_inference_catalog` List the Inference Providers model catalog with pricing **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_list_inference_catalog", {}) ``` --- ### `list_models` Search models on the Hugging Face Hub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Free-text search query over model IDs. | | `author` | `string` | No | Restrict to a given author/organisation. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter tag (e.g. ``'text-classification'``, ``'pytorch'``). | | `pipeline_tag` | `string` | No | Restrict to a single pipeline/task (e.g. ``'text-classification'``, ``'text-to-image'``). Unlike ``filter`` (which matches any tag, so e.g. text-ranking models tagged ``text-classification`` slip in), ``pipeline_tag`` matches the model's canonical task -- prefer it for clean task-based discovery. | | `library` | `string` | No | Restrict to a framework/library (e.g. ``'transformers'``, ``'diffusers'``, ``'sentence-transformers'``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Field to sort by (e.g. ``'downloads'``, ``'likes'``). | | `direction` | `integer` | No | Sort direction; ``-1`` for descending. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of models to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_list_models", {"search": "your-search", "author": "your-author"}) ``` --- ### `list_repo_files` List files in a Hub repository's tree | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `repo_id` | `string` | Yes | Repo ID (e.g. ``'google-bert/bert-base-uncased'``). | | `repo_type` | `string` | No | One of ``'model'``, ``'dataset'``, or ``'space'`` (default ``'model'``). (default: `model`) | | `revision` | `string` | No | Git revision (branch, tag, or commit SHA); defaults to ``'main'``. (default: `main`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_list_repo_files", {"repo_id": "your-repo_id", "repo_type": "model", "revision": "main"}) ``` --- ### `list_spaces` Search Spaces on the Hugging Face Hub | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Free-text search query over Space IDs. | | `author` | `string` | No | Restrict to a given author/organisation. | | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter tag (e.g. ``'gradio'``, ``'streamlit'``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Field to sort by (e.g. ``'likes'``). | | `direction` | `integer` | No | Sort direction; ``-1`` for descending. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of Spaces to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_list_spaces", {"search": "your-search", "author": "your-author"}) ``` --- ### `object_detection` Detect objects and bounding boxes in an image | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Object-detection model repo ID (e.g. ``'facebook/detr-resnet-50'``). | | `image` | `string` | Yes | The image to analyse, as raw ``bytes`` or a base64-encoded string. | | `threshold` | `number` | No | Minimum confidence required to report a detection. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_object_detection", {"model": "your-model", "image": "your-image", "threshold": 10, "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `question_answering` Answer a question from a context passage | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | QA model repo ID (e.g. ``'deepset/roberta-base-squad2'``). | | `question` | `string` | Yes | The question to answer. | | `context` | `string` | Yes | The passage to extract the answer from. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `HFQuestionAnswer` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_question_answering", {"model": "your-model", "question": "your-question", "context": "your-context", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `sentence_similarity` Score a sentence against candidates for similarity | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Sentence-similarity model repo ID, e.g. ``'sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2'``. | | `source_sentence` | `string` | Yes | The sentence to compare against. | | `sentences` | `array` | Yes | Candidate sentences to score for similarity. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_sentence_similarity", {"model": "your-model", "source_sentence": "your-source_sentence", "sentences": "your-sentences", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `summarize` Summarize text with a hosted model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Summarization model repo ID (e.g. ``'facebook/bart-large-cnn'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to summarize. | | `min_length` | `integer` | No | Minimum length (in tokens) of the summary. | | `max_length` | `integer` | No | Maximum length (in tokens) of the summary. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_summarize", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "min_length": 10, "max_length": 10}) ``` --- ### `table_question_answering` Answer a question about a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Table-QA model repo ID (e.g. ``'google/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq'``). | | `query` | `string` | Yes | The question to answer about the table. | | `table` | `object` | Yes | The table as a dict mapping each column name to its list of cell values (all values are strings). Every column must have the same number of rows. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `HFTableQuestionAnswer` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_table_question_answering", {"model": "your-model", "query": "is:unread", "table": "your-table", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `text_classification` Classify text with a hosted model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Classification model repo ID, e.g. ``'distilbert/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english'``. | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to classify. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_text_classification", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `text_generation` Generate text from a prompt with a hosted model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model repo ID (e.g. ``'gpt2'``, ``'bigscience/bloom'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The prompt to continue. | | `max_new_tokens` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `temperature` | `number` | No | Sampling temperature (higher is more random). | | `top_p` | `number` | No | Nucleus-sampling probability mass cutoff. | | `top_k` | `integer` | No | Keep only the top-k most probable tokens when sampling. | | `repetition_penalty` | `number` | No | Penalty (>1.0) for repeating tokens. | | `do_sample` | `boolean` | No | Whether to sample instead of greedy decoding. | | `return_full_text` | `boolean` | No | If False, return only the newly generated text rather than the prompt plus continuation. | | `wait_for_model` | `boolean` | No | If True, block until the model is loaded instead of returning a 503 while it warms up. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_text_generation", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "max_new_tokens": 10, "temperature": 10}) ``` --- ### `text_to_image` Generate an image from a text prompt | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Text-to-image model repo ID (e.g. ``'black-forest-labs/FLUX.1-schnell'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text prompt describing the desired image. | | `negative_prompt` | `string` | No | A prompt describing what to avoid. | | `guidance_scale` | `number` | No | How strongly to follow the prompt (higher is closer but may over-saturate). | | `num_inference_steps` | `integer` | No | Number of denoising steps (higher is usually higher quality but slower). | | `width` | `integer` | No | Output image width in pixels. | | `height` | `integer` | No | Output image height in pixels. | | `seed` | `integer` | No | Seed for the random number generator (for reproducibility). | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `bytes` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_text_to_image", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "negative_prompt": "your-negative_prompt", "guidance_scale": 10}) ``` --- ### `text_to_speech` Synthesize speech audio from text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Text-to-speech model repo ID (e.g. ``'espnet/kan-bayashi_ljspeech_vits'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to synthesize into speech. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `bytes` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_text_to_speech", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `token_classification` Tag tokens in text (NER / part-of-speech) | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Token-classification model repo ID (e.g. ``'dslim/bert-base-NER'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to tag. | | `aggregation_strategy` | `string` | No | How to group sub-word tokens into entities -- one of ``'none'``, ``'simple'``, ``'first'``, ``'average'``, or ``'max'``. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_token_classification", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "aggregation_strategy": "your-aggregation_strategy", "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ### `translate` Translate text with a hosted model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Translation model repo ID (e.g. ``'Helsinki-NLP/opus-mt-en-fr'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to translate. | | `src_lang` | `string` | No | Source language code, for multilingual models that require it (e.g. ``'en_XX'`` for mBART). | | `tgt_lang` | `string` | No | Target language code, for multilingual models that require it (e.g. ``'fr_XX'`` for mBART). | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_translate", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "src_lang": "your-src_lang", "tgt_lang": "your-tgt_lang"}) ``` --- ### `whoami` Get the authenticated Hugging Face identity **Returns:** `HFWhoAmI` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_whoami", {}) ``` --- ### `zero_shot_classification` Classify text against candidate labels without training | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Zero-shot model repo ID (e.g. ``'facebook/bart-large-mnli'``). | | `inputs` | `string` | Yes | The text to classify. | | `candidate_labels` | `array` | Yes | Candidate labels to score the text against. | | `multi_label` | `boolean` | No | If True, scores are independent per label rather than forming a single distribution. | | `provider` | `string` | No | Inference provider to route through (default ``'hf-inference'``); set to a partner provider (e.g. ``'fal-ai'``) to run heavy models elsewhere. (default: `hf-inference`) | **Returns:** `HFZeroShotResult` ```python result = kit.execute("huggingface_zero_shot_classification", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs", "candidate_labels": "your-candidate_labels", "multi_label": True, "provider": "hf-inference"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Inference outputs are heterogeneous per task — each action returns a typed model for its stable shape (e.g. `HFGeneratedText`, `HFClassification`); embeddings return raw `list[list[float]]` - `feature_extraction` accepts a single string **or a list of strings** — pass a list to embed a batch in one request (one `list[float]` row per input, index-aligned), which is far cheaper than one call per text - For model discovery, prefer `list_models(pipeline_tag="text-to-image")` over `filter=` — `pipeline_tag` matches a model's canonical task, while `filter` matches any tag (so e.g. text-ranking models tagged `text-classification` slip into a `filter` result). `library=` narrows by framework (`transformers`, `diffusers`, `sentence-transformers`) - For inference, always pass a model's **canonical, org-prefixed** repo ID (e.g. `google-bert/bert-base-uncased`, `distilbert/distilbert-base-uncased-finetuned-sst-2-english`) — the router does not resolve legacy aliases, and a bare alias returns `Model not supported by provider`. The Hub metadata actions (`get_model`/`get_dataset`) *do* resolve aliases (the connector follows the Hub's `307` redirect) - Pass `wait_for_model=True` on `text_generation` to block while a cold model warms up instead of getting a 503 - Serverless inference (the default `hf-inference` provider) is throttled — cache results and prefer batching where possible; route heavy/generative tasks to a partner provider via `provider=` (e.g. `"fal-ai"`, `"replicate"`, `"together"`) - **Resilience contract:** a cold model returns `503` → typed `ServerError` with `retry_eligible=True` and the "currently loading" reason in the message (back off and retry; the router usually auto-loads within seconds). A `429` → `RateLimitError` with `retry_after_seconds` parsed from `Retry-After`. Both are credential-redacted. The default request timeout is **30 s** — raise it for slow/cold inference with `HuggingFace(timeout=120)` (or the equivalent ToolKit credential config) ## Related Connectors - [OpenAI](../openai_connector/) — GPT models and embeddings - [Anthropic](../anthropic_connector/) — Claude models --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

iam.readme_md: # AWS IAM > Manage access to AWS services and resources securely ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Security-059669) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Security | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/iam](https://aws.amazon.com/iam/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 15 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | Free | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[iam]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_IAM_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["iam"], credentials={"iam": "your-token"}) # List IAM users result = kit.execute("iam_list_users", {"path_prefix": "/"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["iam"], credentials={"iam": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["iam"], credentials={"iam": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS IAM](https://aws.amazon.com/iam/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("iam_list_users", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_role_to_instance_profile` Add a role to an instance profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_profile_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the instance profile. | | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role to add. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_add_role_to_instance_profile", {"instance_profile_name": "your-instance_profile_name", "role_name": "your-role_name"}) ``` --- ### `attach_role_policy` Attach a managed policy to a role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role. | | `policy_arn` | `string` | Yes | ARN of the policy to attach. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_attach_role_policy", {"role_name": "your-role_name", "policy_arn": "your-policy_arn"}) ``` --- ### `create_access_key` Create an access key for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_name` | `string` | No | IAM user name. If empty, creates a key for the calling user. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `IAMAccessKey` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_create_access_key", {"user_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `create_instance_profile` Create an instance profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_profile_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the instance profile. | | `path` | `string` | No | Path prefix for the instance profile. (default: `/`) | **Returns:** `IAMInstanceProfile` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_create_instance_profile", {"instance_profile_name": "your-instance_profile_name", "path": "/"}) ``` --- ### `create_policy` Create an IAM policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `policy_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the policy. | | `policy_document` | `string` | Yes | JSON policy document. | | `description` | `string` | No | Description of the policy. (default: ``) | | `path` | `string` | No | Path prefix for the policy. (default: `/`) | **Returns:** `IAMPolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_create_policy", {"policy_name": "your-policy_name", "policy_document": "your-policy_document", "description": "", "path": "/"}) ``` --- ### `create_role` Create an IAM role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the role. | | `assume_role_policy_document` | `string` | Yes | JSON trust policy document. | | `description` | `string` | No | Description of the role. (default: ``) | | `path` | `string` | No | Path prefix for the role. (default: `/`) | **Returns:** `IAMRole` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_create_role", {"role_name": "your-role_name", "assume_role_policy_document": "your-assume_role_policy_document", "description": "", "path": "/"}) ``` --- ### `delete_access_key` :warning: Delete an access key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `access_key_id` | `string` | Yes | Access key ID to delete. | | `user_name` | `string` | No | IAM user name. If empty, defaults to the calling user. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_delete_access_key", {"access_key_id": "your-access_key_id", "user_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `delete_policy` :warning: Delete an IAM policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `policy_arn` | `string` | Yes | ARN of the policy to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_delete_policy", {"policy_arn": "your-policy_arn"}) ``` --- ### `delete_role` :warning: Delete an IAM role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_delete_role", {"role_name": "your-role_name"}) ``` --- ### `detach_role_policy` Detach a managed policy from a role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role. | | `policy_arn` | `string` | Yes | ARN of the policy to detach. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_detach_role_policy", {"role_name": "your-role_name", "policy_arn": "your-policy_arn"}) ``` --- ### `get_policy` Get an IAM policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `policy_arn` | `string` | Yes | ARN of the policy to retrieve. | **Returns:** `IAMPolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_get_policy", {"policy_arn": "your-policy_arn"}) ``` --- ### `get_role` Get an IAM role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role to retrieve. | **Returns:** `IAMRole` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_get_role", {"role_name": "your-role_name"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get the current IAM user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_name` | `string` | No | IAM user name. If empty, returns the calling user. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `IAMUser` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_get_user", {"user_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `list_access_keys` List access keys for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_name` | `string` | No | IAM user name. If empty, lists keys for the calling user. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_list_access_keys", {"user_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `list_attached_role_policies` List managed policies attached to a role | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_list_attached_role_policies", {"role_name": "your-role_name"}) ``` --- ### `list_instance_profiles` List instance profiles | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `path_prefix` | `string` | No | Path prefix to filter (default ``/``). (default: `/`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_list_instance_profiles", {"path_prefix": "/"}) ``` --- ### `list_policies` List IAM policies | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `scope` | `string` | No | Scope filter -- ``Local`` for customer-managed, ``AWS`` for AWS-managed, or ``All``. (default: `Local`) | | `only_attached` | `boolean` | No | If True, only return policies attached to an IAM entity. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_list_policies", {"scope": "Local", "only_attached": False}) ``` --- ### `list_roles` List IAM roles | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `path_prefix` | `string` | No | Path prefix to filter roles (default ``/``). (default: `/`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_list_roles", {"path_prefix": "/"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List IAM users | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `path_prefix` | `string` | No | Path prefix to filter users (default ``/``). (default: `/`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_list_users", {"path_prefix": "/"}) ``` --- ### `remove_role_from_instance_profile` Remove a role from an instance profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `instance_profile_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the instance profile. | | `role_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the role to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("iam_remove_role_from_instance_profile", {"instance_profile_name": "your-instance_profile_name", "role_name": "your-role_name"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 15 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_access_key`, `delete_policy`, `delete_role`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Okta](../okta/) — Identity management - [Auth0](../auth0/) — Authentication --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

intercom.readme_md: # Intercom > Customer messaging and engagement platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/intercom/python-intercom?style=social)](https://github.com/intercom/python-intercom) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/intercom/python-intercom) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Intercom Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [intercom.com](https://intercom.com) | | **API Docs** | [developers.intercom.com](https://developers.intercom.com/docs/references/rest-api/api.intercom.io/) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Access Token) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan and endpoint | | **Pricing** | From $39/seat/month | --- ## Overview The Intercom API provides access to contacts, conversations, articles, teams, and tags. Manage customer communication, automate messaging workflows, build help center content, and integrate with your CRM. ## Use Cases - Customer communication - In-app messaging - Help center management - Lead qualification - User engagement tracking ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[intercom]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_INTERCOM_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["intercom"], credentials={"intercom": "your-token"}) # List contacts result = kit.execute("intercom_list_contacts", {"limit": 50, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["intercom"], credentials={"intercom": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["intercom"], credentials={"intercom": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Access Token) 1. Create an account at [Intercom](https://intercom.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.intercom.com/a/apps/_/developer-hub) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("intercom_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `close_conversation` Close a conversation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `conversation_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom conversation ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_close_conversation", {"conversation_id": "your-conversation_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `role` | `string` | No | Contact role: ``"user"`` or ``"lead"``. (default: `user`) | | `email` | `string` | No | Email address. | | `name` | `string` | No | Full name. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | **Returns:** `IntercomContact` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_create_contact", {"role": "user", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_message` :warning: Create and send a new message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_email` | `string` | Yes | Admin email sending the message. | | `to_email` | `string` | Yes | Recipient contact email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Message subject line. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Message body (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `IntercomMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_create_message", {"from_email": "your-from_email", "to_email": "your-to_email", "subject": "Hello", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `create_note` :warning: Create a note on a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Note body text (HTML allowed). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_create_note", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `get_admin` Get a single admin by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `admin_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom admin ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomAdmin` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_get_admin", {"admin_id": "your-admin_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_contact` Get a single contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomContact` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_get_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_conversation` Get a single conversation by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `conversation_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom conversation ID. | **Returns:** `IntercomConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_get_conversation", {"conversation_id": "your-conversation_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_admins` List all admins in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_admins", {}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List contacts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 150). (default: `50`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[IntercomContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_contacts", {"limit": 50, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_conversations` List conversations | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 150). (default: `20`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Cursor from a previous response for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[IntercomConversation]` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_conversations", {"limit": 20, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_notes` List notes on a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_notes", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags` List all tags in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_list_tags", {}) ``` --- ### `reply_to_conversation` :warning: Reply to a conversation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `conversation_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom conversation ID. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Reply body text. | | `message_type` | `string` | No | Type of reply: ``"comment"`` or ``"note"``. (default: `comment`) | **Returns:** `IntercomConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_reply_to_conversation", {"conversation_id": "your-conversation_id", "body": "Hello!", "message_type": "comment"}) ``` --- ### `search_contacts` Search contacts using filters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | The value to search for. | | `field` | `string` | No | The contact field to search on (e.g. ``"email"``, ``"name"``, ``"phone"``). (default: `email`) | | `operator` | `string` | No | Comparison operator (``"="``, ``"!="``, ``"~"``, ``"contains"``, ``"starts_with"``). (default: `=`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[IntercomContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_search_contacts", {"query": "is:unread", "field": "email", "operator": "="}) ``` --- ### `tag_contact` :warning: Tag a contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | | `tag_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the tag to apply. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_tag_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "tag_name": "your-tag_name"}) ``` --- ### `update_contact` Update an existing contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The Intercom contact ID. | | `name` | `string` | No | New full name. | | `email` | `string` | No | New email address. | **Returns:** `IntercomContact` ```python result = kit.execute("intercom_update_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id", "name": "my-name", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_contacts` for filtered queries and `list_admins` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by plan and endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_contact`, `create_message`, `create_note`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Salesforce](../salesforce/) — Enterprise CRM - [Hubspot](../hubspot/) — Marketing & sales CRM - [Freshdesk](../freshdesk/) — Helpdesk - [Zendesk](../zendesk/) — Support ticketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

jira.readme_md: # Jira > Project tracking, issue management, and agile boards ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Project_Management-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-28-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/atlassian/python-jira?style=social)](https://github.com/atlassian/python-jira) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/atlassian/python-jira) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Atlassian | | **Category** | Project Management | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [www.atlassian.com/software/jira](https://www.atlassian.com/software/jira) | | **API Docs** | [developer.atlassian.com](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/jira/platform/rest/v3/) | | **Auth** | Basic Auth (email:api_token), OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/minute | | **Pricing** | Free up to 10 users, Standard from $8.15/user/month | --- ## Overview The Jira REST API provides access to issues, projects, sprints, boards, and workflows. Search with JQL, manage issue transitions, track time with worklogs, and automate agile project management across teams. ## Use Cases - Issue tracking automation - Sprint planning - Bug triage workflows - Cross-tool project sync - Custom reporting ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[jira]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_JIRA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["jira"], credentials={"jira": "your-token"}) # List projects accessible to the user result = kit.execute("jira_list_projects", {"limit": 50, "start_at": 0}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["jira"], credentials={"jira": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["jira"], credentials={"jira": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Basic Auth (email:api_token) 1. Account Settings 2. Security 3. API Tokens [Get credentials →](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("jira_list_projects", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_attachment` :warning: Add an attachment to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `filename` | `string` | Yes | The name of the file being uploaded. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Raw file content as bytes. | **Returns:** `JiraAttachment` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_attachment", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "filename": "your-filename", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Comment text. Sent as ADF (Atlassian Document Format). | **Returns:** `JiraComment` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_comment", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `add_watcher` Add a watcher to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user to add as a watcher. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_watcher", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `add_worklog` :warning: Add a worklog entry to an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `time_spent` | `string` | Yes | Time spent in Jira duration format (e.g., ``"2h 30m"``, ``"1d"``, ``"45m"``). | | `comment` | `string` | No | Optional plain-text comment describing the work done. | **Returns:** `JiraWorklog` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_add_worklog", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "time_spent": "your-time_spent", "comment": "your-comment"}) ``` --- ### `assign_issue` Assign an issue to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `assignee_account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user to assign. Pass ``"-1"`` to set unassigned. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_assign_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "assignee_account_id": "your-assignee_account_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_issue` :warning: Create a new issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_key` | `string` | Yes | Key of the project (e.g., ``"PROJ"``). | | `summary` | `string` | Yes | One-line summary of the issue. | | `issue_type` | `string` | No | Issue type name (e.g., ``"Task"``, ``"Bug"``, ``"Story"``). (default: `Task`) | | `description` | `string` | No | Detailed description in Atlassian Document Format or plain text. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority name (e.g., ``"High"``, ``"Medium"``). | | `assignee` | `string` | No | Account ID of the assignee. | **Returns:** `JiraIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_create_issue", {"project_key": "your-project_key", "summary": "your-summary", "issue_type": "Task", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_project` :warning: Create a new project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the project. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Unique project key (e.g., ``"PROJ"``). Must be uppercase alphanumeric, 2-10 characters. | | `project_type_key` | `string` | Yes | The type of project (e.g., ``"software"``, ``"service_desk"``, ``"business"``). | | `lead_account_id` | `string` | Yes | Atlassian account ID of the project lead. | **Returns:** `JiraProject` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_create_project", {"name": "my-name", "key": "my-key", "project_type_key": "your-project_type_key", "lead_account_id": "your-lead_account_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment from an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the comment to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_delete_comment", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_issue` :warning: Delete an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_delete_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_board` Get an Agile board by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ID of the board to retrieve. | **Returns:** `JiraBoard` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_board", {"board_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_issue` Get a single issue by key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key or ID. | **Returns:** `JiraIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_transitions` Get available transitions for an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_transitions", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a Jira user by account ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user. | **Returns:** `JiraUser` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_get_user", {"account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `link_issues` :warning: Create a link between two issues | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `inward_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key for the inward issue (e.g., ``"PROJ-100"``). | | `outward_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key for the outward issue (e.g., ``"PROJ-200"``). | | `link_type` | `string` | Yes | The name of the link type (e.g., ``"Blocks"``, ``"Duplicate"``, ``"Relates"``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_link_issues", {"inward_key": "your-inward_key", "outward_key": "your-outward_key", "link_type": "your-link_type"}) ``` --- ### `list_boards` List Agile boards | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_key` | `string` | No | Filter boards by project key. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum boards per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination (0-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraBoard]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_boards", {"project_key": "your-project_key", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_comments", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `list_issue_types` List issue types available in the instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | No | Optional project ID to scope the issue types to a specific project. When omitted, returns all global issue types. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_issue_types", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_priorities` List available priorities **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_priorities", {}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List projects accessible to the user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum projects per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination (0-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_projects", {"limit": 50, "start_at": 0}) ``` --- ### `list_resolutions` List available resolutions **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_resolutions", {}) ``` --- ### `list_sprints` List sprints for a board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ID of the Agile board. | | `state` | `string` | No | Filter by sprint state: ``active``, ``closed``, or ``future``. Omit to list all. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum sprints per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Offset for pagination (0-based). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraSprint]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_sprints", {"board_id": 10, "state": "open", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_watchers` List watchers on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_watchers", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `list_worklogs` List worklog entries on an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_list_worklogs", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key"}) ``` --- ### `remove_watcher` Remove a watcher from an issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `account_id` | `string` | Yes | The Atlassian account ID of the user to remove. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_remove_watcher", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `search_issues` Search issues using JQL | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `jql` | `string` | Yes | JQL query string (e.g., ``"project = PROJ AND status = Open"``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `start_at` | `integer` | No | Index of the first result to return (0-based offset). (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[JiraIssue]` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_search_issues", {"jql": "your-jql", "limit": 50, "start_at": 0}) ``` --- ### `search_users` Search for Jira users | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | The search string to match against user display names and email addresses. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of results to return (max 1000). (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_search_users", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `transition_issue` :warning: Transition an issue to a new status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `transition_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the transition to execute. Use ``get_transitions`` to discover available transitions. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_transition_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "transition_id": "your-transition_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_issue` Update an existing issue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `issue_key` | `string` | Yes | The issue key (e.g., ``"PROJ-123"``). | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field names to new values following the Jira field schema. | **Returns:** `JiraIssue` ```python result = kit.execute("jira_update_issue", {"issue_key": "your-issue_key", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_issues` for filtered queries and `list_boards` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 100 requests/minute — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_attachment`, `add_comment`, `add_worklog`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 28 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Asana](../asana/) — Work management - [Linear](../linear/) — Modern issue tracker - [Trello](../trello/) — Kanban boards --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
