mistral.readme_md: # Mistral > Mistral AI models for chat, embeddings, and code ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ai_Ml-7C3AED) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-30-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mistralai/client-python?style=social)](https://github.com/mistralai/client-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/mistralai/client-python) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/mistralai/client-ts) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Mistral AI | | **Category** | Ai Ml | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [mistral.ai](https://mistral.ai) | | **API Docs** | [docs.mistral.ai](https://docs.mistral.ai/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by model and tier | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-token (varies by model) | --- ## Overview The Mistral AI API provides access to Mistral models for chat completions, text embeddings, fill-in-the-middle (FIM) code completions with Codestral, and content moderation. The API is OpenAI-compatible and authenticated with a Bearer API key. ## Use Cases - Chat and text generation - Semantic search with embeddings - Inline code completion (FIM) - Content moderation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[mistral]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_MISTRAL_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["mistral"], credentials={"mistral": "your-token"}) # Generate a chat completion result = kit.execute( "mistral_chat_completion", { "model": "mistral-large-latest", "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": "Say hi"}], }, ) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["mistral"], credentials={"mistral": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["mistral"], credentials={"mistral": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Console 2. API Keys 3. Create New Key [Get credentials →](https://console.mistral.ai/api-keys/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("mistral_list_models", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `agents_completion` Create an agent completion | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `agent_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the agent to use for this completion. | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | List of message dicts with ``'role'`` and ``'content'``. | | `max_tokens` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `tools` | `array` | No | List of tool definitions for function calling. | | `response_format` | `object` | No | Output format spec, e.g. ``{"type": "json_object"}`` or a ``json_schema`` block. | **Returns:** `AgentsCompletion` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_agents_completion", {"agent_id": "your-agent_id", "messages": "your-messages", "max_tokens": 10, "tools": "your-tools"}) ``` --- ### `archive_model` Archive a fine-tuned model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuned model ID to archive. | **Returns:** `ArchiveModelResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_archive_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `cancel_batch_job` :warning: Cancel a batch job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `job_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch job ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `BatchJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_cancel_batch_job", {"job_id": "your-job_id"}) ``` --- ### `cancel_finetuning_job` :warning: Cancel a fine-tuning job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `job_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuning job ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `FineTuningJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_cancel_finetuning_job", {"job_id": "your-job_id"}) ``` --- ### `chat_completion` Create a chat completion | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., ``'mistral-large-latest'``, ``'mistral-small-latest'``). | | `messages` | `array` | Yes | List of message dicts with ``'role'`` and ``'content'`` keys (e.g., ``[{"role": "user", "content": "Hi"}]``). | | `temperature` | `number` | No | Sampling temperature between 0 and 1. | | `max_tokens` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | | `top_p` | `number` | No | Nucleus sampling probability mass between 0 and 1. | **Returns:** `ChatCompletion` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_chat_completion", {"model": "your-model", "messages": "your-messages", "temperature": 10, "max_tokens": 10}) ``` --- ### `chat_moderations` Run content moderation on a conversation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Moderation model ID (e.g., ``'mistral-moderation-latest'``). | | `inputs` | `array` | Yes | A list of message dicts with ``'role'`` and ``'content'``, or a list of such conversations. | **Returns:** `ModerationResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_chat_moderations", {"model": "your-model", "inputs": "your-inputs"}) ``` --- ### `classifications` Classify text with a classifier model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Classifier model ID (e.g., ``'mistral-moderation-latest'`` or a fine-tuned classifier). | | `input` | `string` | Yes | A single string or a list of strings to classify. | **Returns:** `ClassificationResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_classifications", {"model": "your-model", "input": "your-input"}) ``` --- ### `create_batch_job` Create a batch job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `endpoint` | `string` | Yes | Target inference endpoint for the batch, e.g. ``'/v1/chat/completions'``, ``'/v1/embeddings'``, ``'/v1/moderations'``, or ``'/v1/ocr'``. | | `input_files` | `array` | No | List of uploaded batch input file IDs. | | `requests` | `array` | No | Inline list of request objects to batch. | | `model` | `string` | No | The model to use for batch inference (one per batch). | | `metadata` | `object` | No | Arbitrary string metadata to attach to the job. | | `timeout_hours` | `integer` | No | Timeout in hours for the batch job. | **Returns:** `BatchJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_create_batch_job", {"endpoint": "your-endpoint", "input_files": "your-input_files", "requests": "your-requests"}) ``` --- ### `create_finetuning_job` Create a fine-tuning job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | The base model to fine-tune (e.g., ``'open-mistral-7b'``). | | `hyperparameters` | `object` | Yes | Training hyperparameters, e.g. ``{"learning_rate": 0.0001, "training_steps": 10}``. | | `training_files` | `array` | No | List of training file references, e.g. ``[{"file_id": "", "weight": 1.0}]``. | | `validation_files` | `array` | No | List of uploaded validation file IDs. | | `suffix` | `string` | No | String appended to the fine-tuned model's name. | | `auto_start` | `boolean` | No | Whether to start the job immediately after validation instead of leaving it queued. | **Returns:** `FineTuningJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_create_finetuning_job", {"model": "your-model", "hyperparameters": "your-hyperparameters", "training_files": "your-training_files", "validation_files": "your-validation_files"}) ``` --- ### `delete_batch_job` :warning: Delete a batch job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `job_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch job ID to delete. | **Returns:** `BatchJobDeleted` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_delete_batch_job", {"job_id": "your-job_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete an uploaded file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to delete. | **Returns:** `FileDeleted` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_delete_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_model` :warning: Delete a fine-tuned model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuned model ID to delete. | **Returns:** `ModelDeleted` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_delete_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `embeddings` Create text embeddings | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Model ID to use (e.g., ``'mistral-embed'``). | | `input` | `string` | Yes | A single string or a list of strings to embed. | **Returns:** `Embedding` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_embeddings", {"model": "your-model", "input": "your-input"}) ``` --- ### `fim_completion` Create a fill-in-the-middle (FIM) code completion | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Code model ID to use (e.g., ``'codestral-latest'``). | | `prompt` | `string` | Yes | The code/text that precedes the insertion point. | | `suffix` | `string` | No | The code/text that follows the insertion point. | | `max_tokens` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of tokens to generate. | **Returns:** `FIMCompletion` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_fim_completion", {"model": "your-model", "prompt": "your-prompt", "suffix": "your-suffix", "max_tokens": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_batch_job` Get a batch job by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `job_id` | `string` | Yes | The batch job ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `BatchJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_get_batch_job", {"job_id": "your-job_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_file` Get file metadata by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `MistralFile` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_get_file", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_content` Download file content by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID whose content to download. | **Returns:** `bytes` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_get_file_content", {"file_id": "your-file_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_file_signed_url` Get a temporary signed download URL for a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to generate a signed URL for. | | `expiry` | `integer` | No | Hours before the URL expires (1-168, default 24). | **Returns:** `FileSignedURL` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_get_file_signed_url", {"file_id": "your-file_id", "expiry": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_finetuning_job` Get a fine-tuning job by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `job_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuning job ID to retrieve. | **Returns:** `FineTuningJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_get_finetuning_job", {"job_id": "your-job_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_model` Get a model by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The model identifier (e.g., ``'ft:mistral-small-latest:...'`` or ``'mistral-large-latest'``). | **Returns:** `MistralModel` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_get_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_batch_jobs` List batch jobs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page` | `integer` | No | Zero-indexed page number of results to return. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of jobs to return per page. | | `model` | `string` | No | Filter to batch jobs using this model. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter to jobs in this state (e.g., ``'RUNNING'``, ``'SUCCESS'``, ``'FAILED'``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_list_batch_jobs", {"page": 10, "page_size": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_files` List uploaded files | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page` | `integer` | No | Zero-indexed page number of results to return. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of files to return per page. | | `purpose` | `string` | No | Filter by purpose (e.g., ``'fine-tune'``, ``'batch'``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_list_files", {"page": 10, "page_size": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_finetuning_jobs` List fine-tuning jobs | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page` | `integer` | No | Zero-indexed page number of results to return. | | `page_size` | `integer` | No | Number of jobs to return per page. | | `model` | `string` | No | Filter to jobs fine-tuning this base model. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter to jobs in this state (e.g., ``'RUNNING'``, ``'SUCCESS'``, ``'FAILED'``). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_list_finetuning_jobs", {"page": 10, "page_size": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_models` List available models **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_list_models", {}) ``` --- ### `moderations` Run content moderation on text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | Moderation model ID (e.g., ``'mistral-moderation-latest'``). | | `input` | `string` | Yes | A single string or a list of strings to classify. | **Returns:** `ModerationResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_moderations", {"model": "your-model", "input": "your-input"}) ``` --- ### `ocr_process` Run OCR on a document | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model` | `string` | Yes | OCR model ID (e.g., ``'mistral-ocr-latest'``). | | `document` | `object` | Yes | The document to process, e.g. ``{"type": "document_url", "document_url": "https://..."}`` or ``{"type": "image_url", "image_url": "https://..."}``. | | `pages` | `array` | No | Specific zero-indexed page numbers to process. | | `include_image_base64` | `boolean` | No | Include extracted images as base64 in the response. | | `image_limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of images to extract. | | `document_annotation_format` | `object` | No | Structured-output spec (``json_schema``) for extracting fields from the whole document. | **Returns:** `OCRResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_ocr_process", {"model": "your-model", "document": "your-document", "pages": "your-pages", "include_image_base64": True}) ``` --- ### `start_finetuning_job` Start a validated fine-tuning job | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `job_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuning job ID to start. | **Returns:** `FineTuningJob` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_start_finetuning_job", {"job_id": "your-job_id"}) ``` --- ### `unarchive_model` Unarchive a fine-tuned model | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuned model ID to unarchive. | **Returns:** `ArchiveModelResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_unarchive_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_finetuned_model` Update a fine-tuned model's name or description | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `model_id` | `string` | Yes | The fine-tuned model ID to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New display name for the model. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the model. | **Returns:** `MistralModel` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_update_finetuned_model", {"model_id": "your-model_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `upload_file` Upload a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_content` | `string` | Yes | Raw file bytes to upload. | | `purpose` | `string` | Yes | Intended purpose (e.g., ``'fine-tune'``, ``'batch'``, ``'ocr'``). | | `filename` | `string` | No | Name for the uploaded file (default: ``"upload.jsonl"``). | **Returns:** `MistralFile` ```python result = kit.execute("mistral_upload_file", {"file_content": "your-file_content", "purpose": "your-purpose", "filename": "your-filename"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by model and tier — use caching to minimize API calls - Use `codestral-latest` with `mistral_fim_completion` for inline code generation - This connector's API is OpenAI-compatible, so existing chat/embedding payloads port over directly ## Related Connectors - [Openai](../openai_connector/) — GPT models - [Anthropic](../anthropic_connector/) — Claude models - [Pinecone](../pinecone/) — Vector database --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

mixpanel.readme_md: # Mixpanel > Product analytics and user behavior tracking ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Analytics-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mixpanel/mixpanel-python?style=social)](https://github.com/mixpanel/mixpanel-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/mixpanel/mixpanel-python) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Mixpanel Inc. | | **Category** | Analytics | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [mixpanel.com](https://mixpanel.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.mixpanel.com](https://developer.mixpanel.com/reference/overview) | | **Auth** | Service Account (Basic Auth) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint | | **Pricing** | Free up to 20M events, Growth from $28/month | --- ## Overview The Mixpanel API lets you track events, query analytics data, manage user profiles, and export data. Build product analytics pipelines, create funnels, analyze user retention, and automate reporting workflows. ## Use Cases - Event tracking - Funnel analysis - User retention reporting - A/B test analysis - Data export and ETL ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[mixpanel]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_MIXPANEL_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["mixpanel"], credentials={"mixpanel": "your-token"}) # Get event count for a date range result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_event_count", {"event": "your-event", "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["mixpanel"], credentials={"mixpanel": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["mixpanel"], credentials={"mixpanel": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Service Account (Basic Auth) 1. Create an account at [Mixpanel](https://mixpanel.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://mixpanel.com/settings/project#serviceaccounts) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_event_count", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_annotation` :warning: Create an annotation in Mixpanel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `date` | `string` | Yes | Annotation date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `description` | `string` | Yes | Description text for the annotation. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_create_annotation", {"date": "your-date", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_profile` :warning: Delete a user profile | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distinct_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's distinct ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_delete_profile", {"distinct_id": "your-distinct_id"}) ``` --- ### `export_data` Export raw event data | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `event` | `string` | No | Filter by specific event name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_export_data", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "event": "your-event"}) ``` --- ### `get_event_count` Get event count for a date range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event name to count. | | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_event_count", {"event": "your-event", "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_funnels` Get funnel analysis | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `funnel_id` | `integer` | Yes | The ID of the funnel to analyze. | | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | **Returns:** `MixpanelFunnel` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_funnels", {"funnel_id": 10, "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_property_values` Get property values for an event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event name. | | `property` | `string` | Yes | The property name to get values for. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of values to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_property_values", {"event": "your-event", "property": "your-property", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_retention` Get retention analysis | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event to measure retention for. | **Returns:** `MixpanelRetention` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_retention", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "event": "your-event"}) ``` --- ### `get_segmentation` Get segmentation data for an event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | The event name to segment. | | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `segment_property` | `string` | No | Optional property to segment by. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_segmentation", {"event": "your-event", "from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "segment_property": "your-segment_property"}) ``` --- ### `get_top_events` Get top events by volume | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_top_events", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_user_profile` Get a user profile by distinct ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `distinct_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique identifier for the user. | **Returns:** `MixpanelProfile` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_get_user_profile", {"distinct_id": "your-distinct_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_cohorts` List cohorts in Mixpanel **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_list_cohorts", {}) ``` --- ### `list_event_names` List all event names **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_list_event_names", {}) ``` --- ### `query_events` Query events within a date range | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `from_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `to_date` | `string` | Yes | End date in YYYY-MM-DD format. | | `event` | `string` | No | Filter by specific event name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MixpanelEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_query_events", {"from_date": "your-from_date", "to_date": "your-to_date", "event": "your-event", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `track_event` Track an event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event` | `string` | Yes | Name of the event to track. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Event properties as key-value pairs. | | `distinct_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user performing the event. | **Returns:** `MixpanelTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mixpanel_track_event", {"event": "your-event", "properties": "your-properties", "distinct_id": "your-distinct_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_annotation`, `delete_profile`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Segment](../segment/) — Customer data --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

mongodb.readme_md: # MongoDB Atlas > Cloud-hosted NoSQL document database ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/mongodb/mongo-python-driver?style=social)](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/mongodb/node-mongodb-native) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-go-driver) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | MongoDB Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [www.mongodb.com/atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas) | | **API Docs** | [www.mongodb.com](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/api/) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | 300 requests/minute (Data API) | | **Pricing** | Free tier (M0), Dedicated from $57/month | --- ## Overview The MongoDB Atlas Data API provides RESTful access to your MongoDB Atlas clusters. Query documents, run aggregation pipelines, insert and update data, and manage collections without direct driver connections. ## Use Cases - Document CRUD operations - Aggregation pipelines - Serverless data access - Edge data queries - Cross-platform data sync ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[mongodb]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_MONGODB_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["mongodb"], credentials={"mongodb": "your-token"}) # List databases available in the MongoDB cluster result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_databases", {}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["mongodb"], credentials={"mongodb": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["mongodb"], credentials={"mongodb": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [MongoDB Atlas](https://www.mongodb.com/atlas) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://cloud.mongodb.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_databases", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `aggregate` Run an aggregation pipeline on a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `pipeline` | `array` | Yes | List of aggregation stages. | **Returns:** `MongoAggregateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_aggregate", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "pipeline": "your-pipeline"}) ``` --- ### `count` Count documents in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | No | Optional MongoDB query filter. | **Returns:** `MongoCountResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_count", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `create_index` Create an index on a collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `keys` | `object` | Yes | Index key specification (e.g. ``{"field": 1}`` for ascending). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_create_index", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "keys": "your-keys"}) ``` --- ### `delete_many` :warning: Delete multiple documents matching a filter | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to match documents. | **Returns:** `MongoDeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_delete_many", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `delete_one` :warning: Delete a single document from a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to locate the document. | **Returns:** `MongoDeleteResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_delete_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `distinct` Get distinct values for a field in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `field` | `string` | Yes | Field path to collect distinct values from. | | `filter` | `object` | No | Optional MongoDB query filter. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_distinct", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "field": "your-field", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `drop_collection` :warning: Drop a collection from a database | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name to drop. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_drop_collection", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database"}) ``` --- ### `find` Find documents in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | No | MongoDB query filter document. | | `sort` | `object` | No | Sort specification (e.g. ``{"created": -1}``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of documents to return. (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[MongoDocument]` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_find", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "sort": "your-sort"}) ``` --- ### `find_one` Find a single document in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter document. | **Returns:** `MongoDocument` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_find_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `insert_many` Insert multiple documents into a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `documents` | `array` | Yes | List of documents to insert. | **Returns:** `MongoInsertResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_insert_many", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "documents": "your-documents"}) ``` --- ### `insert_one` Insert a single document into a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `document` | `object` | Yes | The document to insert. | **Returns:** `MongoInsertResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_insert_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "document": "your-document"}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List collections in a MongoDB database | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_collections", {"database": "your-database"}) ``` --- ### `list_databases` List databases available in the MongoDB cluster **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_list_databases", {}) ``` --- ### `replace_one` Replace a single document by filter | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | Query filter to match the document. | | `replacement` | `object` | Yes | The replacement document. | **Returns:** `MongoUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_replace_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "replacement": "your-replacement"}) ``` --- ### `update_many` Update multiple documents matching a filter | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to match documents. | | `update` | `object` | Yes | Update operations (e.g. ``{"$set": {"active": false}}``). | **Returns:** `MongoUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_update_many", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "update": "your-update"}) ``` --- ### `update_one` Update a single document in a MongoDB collection | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `collection` | `string` | Yes | Collection name. | | `database` | `string` | Yes | Database name. | | `filter` | `object` | Yes | MongoDB query filter to locate the document. | | `update` | `object` | Yes | Update operations (e.g. ``{"$set": {"name": "x"}}``). | **Returns:** `MongoUpdateResult` ```python result = kit.execute("mongodb_update_one", {"collection": "my-collection", "database": "your-database", "filter": "your-filter", "update": "your-update"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 300 requests/minute (Data API) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_many`, `delete_one`, `drop_collection`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

notion.readme_md: # Notion > All-in-one workspace for notes, docs, and databases ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Knowledge-0EA5E9) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-24-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/ramnes/notion-sdk-py?style=social)](https://github.com/ramnes/notion-sdk-py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/ramnes/notion-sdk-py) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Notion Labs Inc. | | **Category** | Knowledge | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [notion.so](https://notion.so) | | **API Docs** | [developers.notion.com](https://developers.notion.com/reference) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (Internal Integration) — BYOK | | **Rate Limit** | 3 requests/second average | | **Pricing** | Free for personal, Plus from $10/user/month | --- ## Overview The Notion API provides access to pages, databases, blocks, comments, and users. Query and update databases, create and modify pages, append content blocks, manage comments and threaded replies, and build integrations that sync data with your Notion workspace. This connector exposes **24 actions** spanning all of Notion's core resource families (search, pages, databases, blocks, users, comments). It is pinned to `Notion-Version: 2022-06-28` for stability — see [Versioned API](#versioned-api) below for the rationale. ## Use Cases - Knowledge base management - Content publishing & documentation automation - Project tracking via database rows - CRM in Notion databases - Threaded comment workflows (review/approval flows) ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[notion]" ``` ## Credentials Two equivalent ways to provide the integration token — the same primitives every ToolsConnector connector uses. Pick whichever fits your code; the patterns compose freely. ```python # Programmatic kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "secret_or_ntn_token"}) # Environment variable (any one of these; first match wins) # export TC_NOTION_CREDENTIALS=... # preferred # export TC_NOTION_API_KEY=... # export TC_NOTION_TOKEN=... kit = ToolKit(["notion"]) # no credentials arg — resolved from env ``` To use multiple Notion workspaces in the same process (one token per workspace), instantiate one ToolKit per credential set, or call the `Notion` class directly with `Notion(credentials=...)`. See [Credentials Guide → Multiple instances of the same tool](../../../docs/guides/credentials.md#multiple-instances-of-the-same-tool) for the full pattern reference. ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) # Confirm the integration is authed and see the bot's identity me = kit.execute("notion_get_me", {}) print(me) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (Internal Integration) 1. Go to https://www.notion.so/my-integrations 2. **+ New integration** → give it a name + select the workspace 3. Copy the **Internal Integration Token** (starts with `secret_` or `ntn_`) 4. Configure the integration's **capabilities** — by default Notion grants Read content + Update content + Insert content + Read comments + Insert comments. Enable **Read user info** if your workflow needs user details. 5. **Critical**: open each page or database the integration should access → `...` menu → **Connections** → add the integration. **Pages that are NOT shared with the integration return HTTP 404, not 403** — this is the most common onboarding bug. The connector surfaces the fix via the `e.suggestion` field on `NotFoundError`. [Get credentials →](https://www.notion.so/my-integrations) ## Error Handling Every typed exception carries a Notion-specific `e.details["notion_code"]` (the structured error code) plus an `e.suggestion` string with an actionable hint: ```python from toolsconnector.errors import ( InvalidCredentialsError, NotFoundError, PermissionDeniedError, RateLimitError, ValidationError, ) try: result = kit.execute("notion_update_block", {"block_id": "...", "content": {...}}) except PermissionDeniedError as e: # The #1 BYOK gotcha — capability missing OR page not shared if e.details.get("notion_code") == "restricted_resource": print(f"Fix: {e.suggestion}") # → "Integration is missing a required capability OR the target # page/database is not shared with this integration. Fix at # https://www.notion.so/my-integrations or open the page in # Notion → '...' menu → 'Connections' → add your integration." except NotFoundError as e: # Notion 404 is ambiguous — page may not exist, OR may not be shared print(f"Fix: {e.suggestion}") except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except InvalidCredentialsError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") # → regenerate at my-integrations except ValidationError as e: # The field-level reason is in `e.details["notion_message"]` print(f"Bad request: {e.details.get('notion_message')}") ``` ### Error matrix | Typed exception | `notion_code` | Cause | Fix | |---|---|---|---| | `InvalidCredentialsError` | `unauthorized` | Token invalid or revoked | Regenerate at https://www.notion.so/my-integrations | | `PermissionDeniedError` | `restricted_resource` | Capability not granted OR page not shared with integration | Edit integration capabilities OR add the page to Connections | | `NotFoundError` | `object_not_found` | Page missing OR not shared (ambiguous — Notion's API doesn't distinguish) | Open page → `...` → Connections → add integration | | `ValidationError` | `validation_error`, `invalid_json`, `missing_version` | Body shape mismatch | Inspect `e.details["notion_message"]` for the field-level reason | | `ConflictError` | `conflict_error` | Concurrent edit collision | Fetch latest, retry | | `RateLimitError` | `rate_limited` | Hit 3 req/s average | Sleep per `Retry-After`, then retry | | `ServerError` | `service_unavailable`, `internal_server_error`, `database_connection_unavailable`, `gateway_timeout` | Notion-side | Exponential backoff retry | ## Verification Status **All 24 actions are Tier 1 — Live verified (2026-05-14)** against a real Notion workspace. Every action was called against the actual Notion API, the response was parsed into the expected typed model, and the request shape (method, path, headers, body) was confirmed to match what the API accepts. The full typed-error matrix (`NotFoundError` from 404, `ValidationError` from 400, `InvalidCredentialsError` from 401, `PermissionDeniedError` from 403) was also exercised against real Notion error responses, not just mocks. | Action | Endpoint | Required capability | Status | |---|---|---|---| | `get_me` | `GET /v1/users/me` | (none) | ✅ Live verified | | `search` | `POST /v1/search` | Read content | ✅ Live verified | | `get_page` | `GET /v1/pages/{id}` | Read content | ✅ Live verified | | `create_page` | `POST /v1/pages` | Insert content | ✅ Live verified | | `update_page` | `PATCH /v1/pages/{id}` | Update content | ✅ Live verified | | `archive_page` | `PATCH /v1/pages/{id}` (archived=true) | Update content | ✅ Live verified | | `restore_page` | `PATCH /v1/pages/{id}` (archived=false) | Update content | ✅ Live verified | | `get_page_property` | `GET /v1/pages/{id}/properties/{prop_id}` | Read content | ✅ Live verified | | `get_database` | `GET /v1/databases/{id}` | Read content | ✅ Live verified | | `create_database` | `POST /v1/databases` | Insert content | ✅ Live verified | | `update_database` | `PATCH /v1/databases/{id}` | Update content | ✅ Live verified | | `query_database` | `POST /v1/databases/{id}/query` | Read content | ✅ Live verified (empty + filter+sort paths) | | `get_block` | `GET /v1/blocks/{id}` | Read content | ✅ Live verified | | `get_block_children` | `GET /v1/blocks/{id}/children` | Read content | ✅ Live verified | | `append_block_children` | `PATCH /v1/blocks/{id}/children` | Insert content | ✅ Live verified | | `update_block` | `PATCH /v1/blocks/{id}` | Update content | ✅ Live verified | | `delete_block` | `DELETE /v1/blocks/{id}` | Update content | ✅ Live verified | | `list_users` | `GET /v1/users` | Read user info | ✅ Live verified | | `get_user` | `GET /v1/users/{id}` | Read user info | ✅ Live verified | | `list_comments` | `GET /v1/comments?block_id=...` | Read comments | ✅ Live verified | | `add_comment` (top-level) | `POST /v1/comments` | Insert comments | ✅ Live verified | | `add_comment` (threaded via `discussion_id`) | `POST /v1/comments` | Insert comments | ✅ Live verified | | `get_comment` | `GET /v1/comments/{id}` | Read comments | ✅ Live verified | | `update_comment` | `PATCH /v1/comments/{id}` | Insert comments | ✅ Live verified | | `delete_comment` | `DELETE /v1/comments/{id}` | Insert comments | ✅ Live verified | ### Real error responses verified live | Trigger | Real response | Typed mapping | Verified | |---|---|---|---| | `get_page("00000000-0000-...")` (nonexistent UUID) | 404 `object_not_found` | `NotFoundError` | ✅ | | `create_page(properties={...invalid...})` | 400 `validation_error` | `ValidationError` | ✅ | | `aget_me()` with bogus token | 401 `unauthorized` | `InvalidCredentialsError` | ✅ | | `get_user("00000000-0000-...")` (nonexistent UUID) | 404 `object_not_found` | `NotFoundError` | ✅ | ### Threaded-comment body shape `add_comment(discussion_id=...)` sends `discussion_id` at the **top level** of the request body — NOT inside a `parent` envelope. This asymmetry vs the page-id form (which uses `{"parent": {"page_id": ...}}`) was caught and corrected during live verification. ## Required integration capabilities Notion integration tokens carry a capability profile set when the integration was created. Default capabilities cover most workflows; **Read user info** is opt-in. | Capability | Granted by default | Actions that need it | |---|---|---| | Read content | ✅ | `search`, `get_page`, `get_database`, `query_database`, `get_block`, `get_block_children`, `get_page_property` | | Insert content | ✅ | `create_page`, `create_database`, `append_block_children` | | Update content | ✅ | `update_page`, `update_database`, `update_block`, `delete_block`, `archive_page`, `restore_page` | | Read comments | ✅ | `list_comments`, `get_comment` | | Insert comments | ✅ | `add_comment`, `update_comment`, `delete_comment` | | Read user info | ❌ | `list_users`, `get_user` | Missing capabilities raise `PermissionDeniedError` with `e.details["notion_code"] == "restricted_resource"`. Note that `get_me` does NOT require Read user info — it always returns the bot's own identity. ## MCP usage For AI agents using MCP (Claude Desktop, Cursor, custom clients), use `exclude_dangerous=True` to filter out destructive actions by default: ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["notion"], credentials={"notion": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp(exclude_dangerous=True) ``` This hides the 7 destructive actions (`create_page`, `create_database`, `append_block_children`, `delete_block`, `add_comment`, `delete_comment`, `archive_page`) — 17 read-only actions remain exposed to the agent. Re-enable explicitly when you trust the agent for writes. ### Claude Desktop configuration Add to `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (macOS): ```json { "mcpServers": { "notion": { "command": "tc", "args": ["serve", "mcp", "notion", "--transport", "stdio"], "env": { "TC_NOTION_CREDENTIALS": "your-notion-integration-token" } } } } ``` ### Pagination caveat for agents Notion list endpoints return up to 100 items per call. Serialized as JSON, large workspace queries can be >50KB and consume a lot of agent context. Recommend agents call with `limit=20–25` and follow the `cursor` returned in `page_state` rather than asking for "everything in one call": ```python result = kit.execute("notion_query_database", {"database_id": "...", "limit": 20}) # Subsequent page: result2 = kit.execute( "notion_query_database", {"database_id": "...", "limit": 20, "cursor": result["page_state"]["cursor"]}, ) ``` See [docs/guides/mcp-server.md](../../../docs/guides/mcp-server.md) for the general MCP server guide. ## Out of Scope (Notion-Version: 2022-06-28 pin) These capabilities are intentionally not implemented — each is blocked by a Notion-Version bump that would also break this connector's current actions: | Capability | Why not implemented | |---|---| | **File uploads** (`/v1/files/*` 3-step flow) | Requires Notion-Version 2025-09-03+ | | **Page moves** (`PUT /v1/pages/{id}/move`) | Requires Notion-Version 2025-09-03+ | | **Markdown read/write** (`/v1/pages/{id}/markdown`) | Requires newer Notion-Version | | **Data sources** (`/v1/data_sources/*`) | 2025-09-03 splits databases into containers + data sources — breaking change for `parse_database` | | **Views** (`/v1/views/*`) | Requires Notion-Version 2025-09-03+ | | **Webhooks** | UI-only subscription model — no programmatic create endpoint exists | | **OAuth token exchange** (`/v1/oauth/*`) | Explicitly excluded by ToolsConnector's BYOK philosophy — users bring their own token | | **Hard-delete pages** (`DELETE /v1/pages/{id}`) | `archive_page` is the safer path; the destructive endpoint is intentionally not exposed | | **List all databases** (`GET /v1/databases`) | Redundant with `search(filter_type="database")` | | **2026-03-11 schema** (`is_archived` / `in_trash` rename) | Would require a connector major-version bump | If you need file uploads, page moves, or data sources, open an issue with your use case — these require committing to a Notion-Version bump and a corresponding connector major release. ## Versioned API This connector pins the `Notion-Version` header to **`2022-06-28`**. Newer Notion API versions introduced breaking changes that would silently break this connector: - **2025-09-03** — split `/v1/databases` into `/v1/databases` (containers) + `/v1/data_sources` (queryable schema). Would break every `query_database` call and `parse_database`. - **2026-03-11** — renamed `archived` → `in_trash` across all endpoints. Would break `archive_page`, `restore_page`, and the `archived` field on every parsed `NotionPage` / `NotionBlock` / `NotionDatabase`. Bumping the pinned version is a deliberate major release — the existing `test_archive_page_sends_archived_field_for_pinned_version` test is the tripwire that catches accidental bumps. See [changes by version](https://developers.notion.com/reference/changes-by-version) for the full migration matrix. ## Reference docs Cross-checked during doc verification: - [Search](https://developers.notion.com/reference/post-search) - [Create a page](https://developers.notion.com/reference/post-page) · [Retrieve a page](https://developers.notion.com/reference/retrieve-a-page) · [Update page](https://developers.notion.com/reference/patch-page) - [Query a database](https://developers.notion.com/reference/post-database-query) - [Retrieve block children](https://developers.notion.com/reference/get-block-children) - [Errors](https://developers.notion.com/reference/errors) · [Status codes](https://developers.notion.com/reference/status-codes) - [Pagination](https://developers.notion.com/reference/intro#pagination) · [Changes by version](https://developers.notion.com/reference/changes-by-version) ## Actions ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to a page or discussion thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to comment on. Used only when ``discussion_id`` is None. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Plain-text content of the comment. | | `discussion_id` | `string` | No | Optional UUID of an existing discussion thread. When set, the comment is added to that thread. | **Returns:** `NotionComment` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_add_comment", {"page_id": "page-123", "text": "Hello!", "discussion_id": "your-discussion_id"}) ``` --- ### `append_block_children` :warning: Append child blocks to a page or block | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent block or page. | | `children` | `array` | Yes | List of block objects to append, following the Notion block schema (e.g., paragraph, heading, to_do, bulleted_list_item). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_append_block_children", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "children": "your-children"}) ``` --- ### `archive_page` :warning: Archive (soft-delete) a page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to archive. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_archive_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `create_database` :warning: Create a new database | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `parent_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent page. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Title for the new database. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Database property schema. Each key is a property name and each value is a property configuration object (e.g., ``{"Name": {"title": {}}, "Tags": {"multi_select": {"options": []}}}``). | **Returns:** `NotionDatabase` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_create_database", {"parent_id": "your-parent_id", "title": "My Title", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `create_page` :warning: Create a new page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `parent_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent page or database. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Page title text. | | `properties` | `object` | No | Additional property values (database pages). | | `children` | `array` | No | Block children to populate the page body. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_create_page", {"parent_id": "your-parent_id", "title": "My Title", "properties": "your-properties", "children": "your-children"}) ``` --- ### `delete_block` :warning: Delete a block | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_delete_block", {"block_id": "your-block_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_comment` :warning: Delete a comment | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the comment to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_delete_comment", {"comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_block` Get a single block by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionBlock` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_block", {"block_id": "your-block_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_block_children` Get child blocks of a page or block | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the parent block or page. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum blocks per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionBlock]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_block_children", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `get_comment` Get a single comment by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the comment to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionComment` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_comment", {"comment_id": "your-comment_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_database` Get a database schema and metadata | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the database. | **Returns:** `NotionDatabase` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_database", {"database_id": "your-database_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_me` Get the bot user associated with the integration token **Returns:** `NotionUser` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_me", {}) ``` --- ### `get_page` Get a single page by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | The UUID of the page to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `get_page_property` Get a page property value by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page. | | `property_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the property to retrieve (found in the page's ``properties`` dict under each property's ``id`` field). | | `cursor` | `string` | No | For paginated property types, pass the ``next_cursor`` from a previous response to retrieve the next page. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Items per page for paginated property types (max 100; clamped if higher). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_page_property", {"page_id": "page-123", "property_id": "your-property_id", "cursor": "your-cursor", "limit": 100}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the user to retrieve. | **Returns:** `NotionUser` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_comments` List comments on a block or page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block or page to list comments for. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionComment]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_list_comments", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "limit": 50, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List all users in the workspace **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_list_users", {}) ``` --- ### `query_database` Query a database with optional filters and sorts | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the database to query. | | `filter` | `object` | No | Notion filter object (compound or property filter). | | `sorts` | `array` | No | List of sort objects with ``property`` and ``direction``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum results per page (max 100). (default: `50`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionPage]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_query_database", {"database_id": "your-database_id", "filter": "your-filter", "sorts": "your-sorts"}) ``` --- ### `restore_page` Restore an archived page | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to restore. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_restore_page", {"page_id": "page-123"}) ``` --- ### `search` Search pages and databases in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | Text to search for in page titles and content. (default: ``) | | `filter_type` | `string` | No | Restrict to ``"page"`` or ``"database"``. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of results per page. (default: `20`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[NotionPage]` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_search", {"query": "", "filter_type": "your-filter_type"}) ``` --- ### `update_block` Update a block's content | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `block_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the block to update. | | `content` | `object` | Yes | Block-type-specific content payload. | **Returns:** `NotionBlock` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_block", {"block_id": "your-block_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `update_comment` Update a comment's text | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `comment_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the comment to update. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | New plain-text content for the comment. | **Returns:** `NotionComment` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_comment", {"comment_id": "your-comment_id", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `update_database` Update a database's title, description, or properties | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `database_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the database to update. | | `title` | `string` | No | New title for the database, or ``None`` to keep the current title. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description text, or ``None`` to keep the current description. | | `properties` | `object` | No | Property schema updates. Each key is a property name and each value is a property configuration object. Pass ``None`` for a property value to remove it. | **Returns:** `NotionDatabase` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_database", {"database_id": "your-database_id", "title": "My Title", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `update_page` Update page properties | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `page_id` | `string` | Yes | UUID of the page to update. | | `properties` | `object` | Yes | Dict of property names to new values, following the Notion property value schema. | **Returns:** `NotionPage` ```python result = kit.execute("notion_update_page", {"page_id": "page-123", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ## Tips - **Pages must be shared with the integration.** Open each page or database → `...` menu → **Connections** → add the integration. Unshared pages return HTTP 404, not 403 — this is the most common onboarding bug. - **Rate limit is 3 requests/second average.** Bursts return `RateLimitError` with `retry_after_seconds` parsed from `Retry-After`. Use pagination + caching to minimize calls. - **Max page_size is 100.** The connector clamps `limit` values above 100. For large workspaces, follow the `cursor` from `page_state` rather than requesting larger pages. - **POST endpoints take pagination in the body, GET endpoints take it in the query string.** The connector handles both — but if you read raw responses, that's why the wire format differs. - **`discussion_id` is sticky.** Once a comment is added to a thread, every reply must reference the same `discussion_id`. The first comment returns it; pass it back into `add_comment(discussion_id=...)` for replies. - **Destructive actions** (`create_page`, `create_database`, `append_block_children`, `delete_block`, `add_comment`, `delete_comment`, `archive_page`) are flagged `dangerous=True`. Use `kit.serve_mcp(exclude_dangerous=True)` to hide them from agents by default. ## Related Connectors - [Confluence](../confluence/) — Team wiki and documentation - [Google Docs](../gdocs/) — Word-style documents - [Google Drive](../gdrive/) — File storage --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

okta.readme_md: # Okta > Enterprise identity and access management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Security-059669) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-21-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/okta/okta-sdk-python?style=social)](https://github.com/okta/okta-sdk-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/okta/okta-sdk-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/okta/okta-sdk-nodejs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Okta Inc. | | **Category** | Security | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [okta.com](https://okta.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.okta.com](https://developer.okta.com/docs/reference/) | | **Auth** | API Token, OAuth 2.0 | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint (typically 600/min) | | **Pricing** | Contact sales (SSO from $2/user/month) | --- ## Overview The Okta API provides access to users, groups, applications, and system logs. Manage enterprise identity lifecycle, configure SSO, enforce MFA, audit security events, and automate user provisioning. ## Use Cases - User lifecycle management - SSO configuration - Group and role management - Security audit logging - Application provisioning ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[okta]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_OKTA_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["okta"], credentials={"okta": "your-token"}) # List users in the Okta organization result = kit.execute("okta_list_users", {"search": "your-search", "filter": "your-filter"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["okta"], credentials={"okta": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["okta"], credentials={"okta": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Token 1. Create an account at [Okta](https://okta.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://developer.okta.com/docs/guides/create-an-api-token/main/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("okta_list_users", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `activate_user` Activate a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_activate_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `add_user_to_group` Add a user to an Okta group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_add_user_to_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `assign_user_to_app` Assign a user to an application | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `app_id` | `string` | Yes | The application's Okta ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | | `profile` | `object` | No | Optional app-specific profile attributes for the user (e.g. SSO username mappings). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_assign_user_to_app", {"app_id": "your-app_id", "user_id": "user-123", "profile": "your-profile"}) ``` --- ### `create_group` Create a new group in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | The group name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional group description. | **Returns:** `OktaGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_create_group", {"name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_user` Create a new user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `profile` | `object` | Yes | User profile fields (firstName, lastName, email, login). | | `credentials` | `object` | No | Optional credentials (password, recovery_question). | **Returns:** `OktaUser` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_create_user", {"profile": "your-profile", "credentials": "your-credentials"}) ``` --- ### `deactivate_user` :warning: Deactivate a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_deactivate_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `delete_group` :warning: Delete a group from Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_delete_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_group` Get a single group by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `OktaGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_get_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_user` Get a single user by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID or login (email). | **Returns:** `OktaUser` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_get_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_applications` List applications in the Okta organization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of applications to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaApplication]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_applications", {"limit": 200}) ``` --- ### `list_group_members` List members of a group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of members to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_group_members", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "limit": 200}) ``` --- ### `list_groups` List groups in the Okta organization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Search query to filter groups by name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of groups to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaGroup]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_groups", {"search": "your-search", "limit": 200}) ``` --- ### `list_system_logs` List system log events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `since` | `string` | No | Lower time bound (ISO 8601, e.g. ``2024-01-01T00:00:00Z``). | | `until` | `string` | No | Upper time bound (ISO 8601). | | `filter` | `string` | No | SCIM filter expression (e.g. ``eventType eq "user.session.start"``). | | `q` | `string` | No | Free-text search query across log event fields. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum events to return (max 1000, default 100). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaLogEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_system_logs", {"since": "your-since", "until": "your-until"}) ``` --- ### `list_user_apps` List applications assigned to a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_user_apps", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_user_groups` List groups a user belongs to | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_user_groups", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List users in the Okta organization | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `search` | `string` | No | Search expression (e.g. ``profile.email eq "user@example.com"``). | | `filter` | `string` | No | Filter expression using Okta filter syntax. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of users to return (max 200). (default: `200`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[OktaUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_list_users", {"search": "your-search", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `remove_user_from_group` Remove a user from a group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | The group's Okta ID. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_remove_user_from_group", {"group_id": "your-group_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `reset_password` Reset a user's password | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_reset_password", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `suspend_user` :warning: Suspend a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_suspend_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `unsuspend_user` Unsuspend a user in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_unsuspend_user", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `update_user` Update an existing user's profile in Okta | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The user's Okta ID. | | `profile` | `object` | Yes | Profile fields to update. | **Returns:** `OktaUser` ```python result = kit.execute("okta_update_user", {"user_id": "user-123", "profile": "your-profile"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint (typically 600/min) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`deactivate_user`, `delete_group`, `suspend_user`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 21 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Auth0](../auth0/) — Authentication --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*
