rds.readme_md: # AWS RDS > Managed relational databases — PostgreSQL, MySQL, Aurora ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/rds](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonRDS/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 25 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use (from $0.017/hour for db.t3.micro) | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[rds]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_RDS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["rds"], credentials={"rds": "your-token"}) # List tags for a database resource result = kit.execute("rds_list_tags_for_resource", {"resource_name": "your-resource_name"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["rds"], credentials={"rds": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["rds"], credentials={"rds": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS RDS](https://aws.amazon.com/rds/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("rds_list_tags_for_resource", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_tags_to_resource` Add tags to a database resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_name` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the RDS resource. | | `tags` | `object` | Yes | Dict of tag key-value pairs to add. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_add_tags_to_resource", {"resource_name": "your-resource_name", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `create_db_cluster` :warning: Create an Aurora database cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_cluster_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the cluster. | | `engine` | `string` | No | Database engine (aurora-mysql, aurora-postgresql). (default: `aurora-postgresql`) | | `master_username` | `string` | No | Master user name for the cluster. (default: `admin`) | | `master_user_password` | `string` | No | Password for the master user. (default: ``) | | `database_name` | `string` | No | Name of the initial database to create. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `RDSCluster` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_create_db_cluster", {"db_cluster_identifier": "your-db_cluster_identifier", "engine": "aurora-postgresql", "master_username": "admin"}) ``` --- ### `create_db_instance` :warning: Create a database instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the DB instance. | | `db_instance_class` | `string` | No | Compute and memory capacity class. (default: `db.t3.micro`) | | `engine` | `string` | No | Database engine (postgres, mysql, mariadb, oracle-ee, etc.). (default: `postgres`) | | `master_username` | `string` | No | Master user name for the DB instance. (default: `admin`) | | `master_user_password` | `string` | No | Password for the master user. (default: ``) | | `allocated_storage` | `integer` | No | Storage size in GiB. (default: `20`) | | `storage_type` | `string` | No | Storage type (gp2, gp3, io1, standard). (default: `gp3`) | | `publicly_accessible` | `boolean` | No | Whether the instance is publicly accessible. (default: `False`) | | `availability_zone` | `string` | No | Preferred availability zone. (default: ``) | | `db_name` | `string` | No | Name of the initial database to create. (default: ``) | | `vpc_security_group_ids` | `array` | No | List of VPC security group IDs. | | `db_subnet_group_name` | `string` | No | DB subnet group name. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_create_db_instance", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier", "db_instance_class": "db.t3.micro", "engine": "postgres"}) ``` --- ### `create_db_instance_read_replica` Create a read replica | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier for the read replica. | | `source_db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the source instance. | | `db_instance_class` | `string` | No | Compute class for the replica (uses source instance's class if empty). (default: ``) | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_create_db_instance_read_replica", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier", "source_db_instance_identifier": "your-source_db_instance_identifier", "db_instance_class": ""}) ``` --- ### `create_db_snapshot` Create a database snapshot | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_snapshot_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier for the new snapshot. | | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the instance to snapshot. | **Returns:** `RDSSnapshot` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_create_db_snapshot", {"db_snapshot_identifier": "your-db_snapshot_identifier", "db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier"}) ``` --- ### `create_db_subnet_group` Create a DB subnet group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_subnet_group_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the subnet group. | | `db_subnet_group_description` | `string` | Yes | Description for the subnet group. | | `subnet_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of VPC subnet IDs to include. | **Returns:** `RDSSubnetGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_create_db_subnet_group", {"db_subnet_group_name": "your-db_subnet_group_name", "db_subnet_group_description": "your-db_subnet_group_description", "subnet_ids": "your-subnet_ids"}) ``` --- ### `delete_db_cluster` :warning: Delete an Aurora database cluster | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_cluster_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the cluster to delete. | | `skip_final_snapshot` | `boolean` | No | Whether to skip creating a final snapshot. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `RDSCluster` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_delete_db_cluster", {"db_cluster_identifier": "your-db_cluster_identifier", "skip_final_snapshot": False}) ``` --- ### `delete_db_instance` :warning: Delete a database instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the instance to delete. | | `skip_final_snapshot` | `boolean` | No | Whether to skip creating a final snapshot. (default: `False`) | | `final_snapshot_identifier` | `string` | No | Identifier for the final snapshot (required if skip_final_snapshot is False). (default: ``) | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_delete_db_instance", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier", "skip_final_snapshot": False, "final_snapshot_identifier": ""}) ``` --- ### `delete_db_snapshot` :warning: Delete a database snapshot | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_snapshot_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the snapshot to delete. | **Returns:** `RDSSnapshot` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_delete_db_snapshot", {"db_snapshot_identifier": "your-db_snapshot_identifier"}) ``` --- ### `describe_db_clusters` Describe Aurora database clusters | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_cluster_identifier` | `string` | No | Optional identifier to describe a specific cluster. If empty, describes all clusters. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_db_clusters", {"db_cluster_identifier": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_db_engine_versions` Describe available database engine versions | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `engine` | `string` | No | Database engine to query (postgres, mysql, etc.). (default: `postgres`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_db_engine_versions", {"engine": "postgres"}) ``` --- ### `describe_db_instances` Describe database instances | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | No | Optional identifier to describe a specific instance. If empty, describes all instances. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_db_instances", {"db_instance_identifier": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_db_parameter_groups` Describe DB parameter groups | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_parameter_group_name` | `string` | No | Optional name to describe a specific parameter group. If empty, describes all parameter groups. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_db_parameter_groups", {"db_parameter_group_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_db_snapshots` Describe database snapshots | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | No | Filter by DB instance identifier. (default: ``) | | `db_snapshot_identifier` | `string` | No | Filter by snapshot identifier. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_db_snapshots", {"db_instance_identifier": "", "db_snapshot_identifier": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_db_subnet_groups` Describe DB subnet groups | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_subnet_group_name` | `string` | No | Optional name to describe a specific subnet group. If empty, describes all subnet groups. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_db_subnet_groups", {"db_subnet_group_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_events` Describe database events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_identifier` | `string` | No | Identifier of the source to filter events. (default: ``) | | `source_type` | `string` | No | Source type filter (db-instance, db-cluster, db-snapshot, db-security-group, db-parameter-group). (default: ``) | | `duration` | `integer` | No | Number of minutes to look back (default 60). (default: `60`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_events", {"source_identifier": "", "source_type": ""}) ``` --- ### `describe_orderable_db_instance_options` Describe orderable DB instance options | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `engine` | `string` | No | Database engine to query (postgres, mysql, etc.). (default: `postgres`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_describe_orderable_db_instance_options", {"engine": "postgres"}) ``` --- ### `list_tags_for_resource` List tags for a database resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_name` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the RDS resource. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_list_tags_for_resource", {"resource_name": "your-resource_name"}) ``` --- ### `modify_db_instance` Modify a database instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the instance to modify. | | `db_instance_class` | `string` | No | New compute and memory capacity class. (default: ``) | | `allocated_storage` | `integer` | No | New storage size in GiB (0 to keep current). (default: `0`) | | `apply_immediately` | `boolean` | No | Whether to apply changes immediately or during the next maintenance window. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_modify_db_instance", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier", "db_instance_class": "", "allocated_storage": 0}) ``` --- ### `promote_read_replica` Promote a read replica to standalone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the read replica to promote. | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_promote_read_replica", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier"}) ``` --- ### `reboot_db_instance` Reboot a database instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the instance to reboot. | | `force_failover` | `boolean` | No | Whether to force a failover from one AZ to another (Multi-AZ instances only). (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_reboot_db_instance", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier", "force_failover": False}) ``` --- ### `remove_tags_from_resource` Remove tags from a database resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_name` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the RDS resource. | | `tag_keys` | `array` | Yes | List of tag keys to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_remove_tags_from_resource", {"resource_name": "your-resource_name", "tag_keys": "your-tag_keys"}) ``` --- ### `restore_db_instance_from_snapshot` Restore a database instance from a snapshot | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier for the restored instance. | | `db_snapshot_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the snapshot to restore from. | | `db_instance_class` | `string` | No | Compute class for the restored instance (uses snapshot's class if empty). (default: ``) | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_restore_db_instance_from_snapshot", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier", "db_snapshot_identifier": "your-db_snapshot_identifier", "db_instance_class": ""}) ``` --- ### `start_db_instance` Start a stopped database instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the instance to start. | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_start_db_instance", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier"}) ``` --- ### `stop_db_instance` Stop a database instance | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `db_instance_identifier` | `string` | Yes | Identifier of the instance to stop. | **Returns:** `RDSInstance` ```python result = kit.execute("rds_stop_db_instance", {"db_instance_identifier": "your-db_instance_identifier"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 25 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_db_cluster`, `create_db_instance`, `delete_db_cluster`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 25 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

redis.readme_md: # Redis (Upstash) > Serverless Redis with HTTP API ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-18-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/upstash/redis-py?style=social)](https://github.com/upstash/redis-py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/upstash/redis-py) [![TypeScript](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/upstash/upstash-redis) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Upstash Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [upstash.com](https://upstash.com) | | **API Docs** | [upstash.com](https://upstash.com/docs/redis/overall/getstarted) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token | | **Rate Limit** | 1,000 requests/second | | **Pricing** | Free tier (10K commands/day), Pay-as-you-go from $0.2/100K commands | --- ## Overview The Upstash Redis REST API provides HTTP-based access to Redis data structures. Execute Redis commands over HTTP, manage keys, work with strings, lists, sets, hashes, and sorted sets without maintaining persistent connections. ## Use Cases - Serverless caching - Session management - Rate limiting - Real-time leaderboards - Pub/sub messaging ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[redis]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_REDIS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["redis"], credentials={"redis": "your-token"}) # Append a value to a Redis string key result = kit.execute("redis_append", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["redis"], credentials={"redis": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["redis"], credentials={"redis": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token 1. Create an account at [Redis (Upstash)](https://upstash.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.upstash.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("redis_append", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `append` Append a value to a Redis string key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | String to append. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_append", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `dbsize` Get the number of keys in the Redis database **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_dbsize", {}) ``` --- ### `delete` :warning: Delete a Redis key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name to delete. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_delete", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `exists` Check if one or more keys exist | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `keys` | `array` | Yes | List of keys to check. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_exists", {"keys": "your-keys"}) ``` --- ### `expire` Set a TTL (expiry) on a key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to set expiry on. | | `seconds` | `integer` | Yes | Time to live in seconds. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_expire", {"key": "my-key", "seconds": 10}) ``` --- ### `get` Get the value of a Redis key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_get", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `hget` Get a field from a Redis hash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the hash. | | `field` | `string` | Yes | Field name within the hash. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_hget", {"key": "my-key", "field": "your-field"}) ``` --- ### `hset` Set a field in a Redis hash | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the hash. | | `field` | `string` | Yes | Field name within the hash. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | Value to set. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_hset", {"key": "my-key", "field": "your-field", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `incr` Increment a key's integer value | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to increment. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_incr", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `keys` Find Redis keys matching a pattern | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `pattern` | `string` | No | Glob pattern (e.g. ``user:*``). Defaults to ``*``. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_keys", {"pattern": "your-pattern"}) ``` --- ### `lpush` Push values onto the head of a Redis list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the list. | | `values` | `array` | Yes | Values to push (leftmost first). | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_lpush", {"key": "my-key", "values": "your-values"}) ``` --- ### `lrange` Get a range of elements from a Redis list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key holding the list. | | `start` | `integer` | Yes | Start index (0-based, supports negative). | | `stop` | `integer` | Yes | Stop index (inclusive, supports negative). | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_lrange", {"key": "my-key", "start": 10, "stop": 10}) ``` --- ### `mget` Get the values of multiple Redis keys | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `keys` | `array` | Yes | List of Redis key names. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_mget", {"keys": "your-keys"}) ``` --- ### `mset` Set multiple Redis keys at once | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key_values` | `object` | Yes | Dict mapping key names to string values. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_mset", {"key_values": "your-key_values"}) ``` --- ### `set` Set the value of a Redis key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | Value to store. | | `ex` | `integer` | No | Optional expiry time in seconds. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_set", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value", "ex": 10}) ``` --- ### `setnx` Set a key only if it does not exist | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | Value to store. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_setnx", {"key": "my-key", "value": "my-value"}) ``` --- ### `ttl` Get the remaining TTL of a key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to check. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_ttl", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `type` Get the type of a key | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `key` | `string` | Yes | Redis key to check. | **Returns:** `RedisResult` ```python result = kit.execute("redis_type", {"key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 1,000 requests/second — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Supabase](../supabase/) — Postgres as a service --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

route53.readme_md: # AWS Route 53 > Scalable DNS and domain name registration ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Networking-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-15-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Networking | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/route53](https://aws.amazon.com/route53/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/Route53/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 5 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | $0.50/hosted zone/month + $0.40/million queries | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[route53]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_ROUTE53_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["route53"], credentials={"route53": "your-token"}) # Get a health check result = kit.execute("route53_get_health_check", {"health_check_id": "your-health_check_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["route53"], credentials={"route53": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["route53"], credentials={"route53": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS Route 53](https://aws.amazon.com/route53/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("route53_get_health_check", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `change_tags_for_resource` Change tags for a resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_type` | `string` | Yes | Resource type (``hostedzone`` or ``healthcheck``). | | `resource_id` | `string` | Yes | The resource ID. | | `add_tags` | `object` | No | Dict of tag key-value pairs to add. | | `remove_tag_keys` | `array` | No | List of tag keys to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_change_tags_for_resource", {"resource_type": "your-resource_type", "resource_id": "your-resource_id", "add_tags": "your-add_tags", "remove_tag_keys": "your-remove_tag_keys"}) ``` --- ### `create_health_check` Create a health check | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `fqdn` | `string` | Yes | Fully qualified domain name to check. | | `port` | `integer` | No | Port number for the health check. (default: `443`) | | `type` | `string` | No | Health check type (``HTTP``, ``HTTPS``, ``TCP``, ``HTTP_STR_MATCH``, ``HTTPS_STR_MATCH``). (default: `HTTPS`) | | `resource_path` | `string` | No | Path for HTTP/HTTPS checks. (default: `/`) | | `request_interval` | `integer` | No | Seconds between health checks (10 or 30). (default: `30`) | | `failure_threshold` | `integer` | No | Number of consecutive failures before unhealthy (1-10). (default: `3`) | **Returns:** `R53HealthCheck` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_create_health_check", {"fqdn": "your-fqdn", "port": 443, "type": "HTTPS"}) ``` --- ### `create_hosted_zone` Create a hosted zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | The domain name for the hosted zone (e.g. ``example.com``). | | `caller_reference` | `string` | No | A unique string to identify the request. Auto-generated if not provided. (default: ``) | | `comment` | `string` | No | Optional comment for the hosted zone. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `R53HostedZone` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_create_hosted_zone", {"name": "my-name", "caller_reference": "", "comment": ""}) ``` --- ### `delete_health_check` :warning: Delete a health check | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `health_check_id` | `string` | Yes | The health check ID to delete. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_delete_health_check", {"health_check_id": "your-health_check_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_hosted_zone` :warning: Delete a hosted zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `hosted_zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The hosted zone ID to delete. | **Returns:** `R53ChangeInfo` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_delete_hosted_zone", {"hosted_zone_id": "your-hosted_zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_record` :warning: Delete a DNS record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `hosted_zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The hosted zone ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | The DNS record name. | | `record_type` | `string` | Yes | The record type. | | `ttl` | `integer` | No | Time to live (must match the existing record). (default: `300`) | | `values` | `array` | No | Record values (must match the existing record). | **Returns:** `R53ChangeInfo` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_delete_record", {"hosted_zone_id": "your-hosted_zone_id", "name": "my-name", "record_type": "your-record_type", "ttl": 300, "values": "your-values"}) ``` --- ### `get_health_check` Get a health check | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `health_check_id` | `string` | Yes | The health check ID. | **Returns:** `R53HealthCheck` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_get_health_check", {"health_check_id": "your-health_check_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_hosted_zone` Get a hosted zone | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `hosted_zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The Route 53 hosted zone ID. | **Returns:** `R53HostedZone` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_get_hosted_zone", {"hosted_zone_id": "your-hosted_zone_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_hosted_zone_count` Get hosted zone count **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_get_hosted_zone_count", {}) ``` --- ### `list_health_checks` List health checks **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_list_health_checks", {}) ``` --- ### `list_hosted_zones` List hosted zones **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_list_hosted_zones", {}) ``` --- ### `list_resource_record_sets` List resource record sets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `hosted_zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The hosted zone ID. | | `record_type` | `string` | No | Optional filter by record type (e.g. ``A``, ``CNAME``, ``MX``). (default: ``) | | `record_name` | `string` | No | Optional filter by record name. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_list_resource_record_sets", {"hosted_zone_id": "your-hosted_zone_id", "record_type": "", "record_name": ""}) ``` --- ### `list_tags_for_resource` List tags for a resource | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `resource_type` | `string` | Yes | Resource type (``hostedzone`` or ``healthcheck``). | | `resource_id` | `string` | Yes | The resource ID. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_list_tags_for_resource", {"resource_type": "your-resource_type", "resource_id": "your-resource_id"}) ``` --- ### `test_dns_answer` Test a DNS answer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `hosted_zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The hosted zone ID. | | `record_name` | `string` | Yes | The DNS name to query. | | `record_type` | `string` | Yes | The record type to query (e.g. ``A``, ``AAAA``). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_test_dns_answer", {"hosted_zone_id": "your-hosted_zone_id", "record_name": "your-record_name", "record_type": "your-record_type"}) ``` --- ### `upsert_record` Create or update a DNS record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `hosted_zone_id` | `string` | Yes | The hosted zone ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | The DNS record name (e.g. ``www.example.com``). | | `record_type` | `string` | Yes | The record type (e.g. ``A``, ``CNAME``, ``MX``, ``TXT``). | | `ttl` | `integer` | No | Time to live in seconds (ignored for alias records). (default: `300`) | | `values` | `array` | No | List of record values for standard records. | | `alias_target` | `object` | No | Alias target configuration dict for alias records. | **Returns:** `R53ChangeInfo` ```python result = kit.execute("route53_upsert_record", {"hosted_zone_id": "your-hosted_zone_id", "name": "my-name", "record_type": "your-record_type", "ttl": 300, "values": "your-values"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 5 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_health_check`, `delete_hosted_zone`, `delete_record`) cannot be undone — use with caution --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

s3.readme_md: # AWS S3 > Scalable object storage in the cloud ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Storage-14B8A6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Storage | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/s3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/API/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) | | **Rate Limit** | 5,500 GET/sec, 3,500 PUT/sec per prefix | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use (from $0.023/GB/month) | --- ## Overview The AWS S3 API provides object storage operations for buckets and objects. Upload, download, list, and manage files. Configure lifecycle policies, access permissions, versioning, and static website hosting. ## Use Cases - File storage and retrieval - Static website hosting - Data lake storage - Backup and archival - Content distribution ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[s3]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_S3_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["s3"], credentials={"s3": "your-token"}) # Get the region (location) of an S3 bucket result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_location", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["s3"], credentials={"s3": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["s3"], credentials={"s3": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) 1. Create an account at [AWS S3](https://aws.amazon.com/s3/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_location", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `copy_object` Copy an object between S3 locations | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_bucket` | `string` | Yes | Source bucket name. | | `source_key` | `string` | Yes | Source object key. | | `dest_bucket` | `string` | Yes | Destination bucket name. | | `dest_key` | `string` | Yes | Destination object key. | **Returns:** `S3CopyResult` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_copy_object", {"source_bucket": "your-source_bucket", "source_key": "your-source_key", "dest_bucket": "your-dest_bucket", "dest_key": "your-dest_key"}) ``` --- ### `create_bucket` :warning: Create a new S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name (must be globally unique). | | `region` | `string` | No | AWS region for the bucket. Defaults to connector region. | **Returns:** `S3Bucket` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_create_bucket", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "region": "your-region"}) ``` --- ### `delete_bucket` :warning: Delete an S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_delete_bucket", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `delete_object` :warning: Delete an object from S3 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_delete_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `delete_object_tags` :warning: Delete all tags from an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `version_id` | `string` | No | Optional version ID to delete tags for a specific object version. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_delete_object_tags", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "version_id": "your-version_id"}) ``` --- ### `generate_presigned_url` Generate a pre-signed URL for an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `expiration` | `integer` | No | URL validity in seconds (default 3600 = 1 hour, max 604800 = 7 days). (default: `3600`) | | `method` | `string` | No | HTTP method the URL authorises (``GET`` or ``PUT``). (default: `GET`) | **Returns:** `S3PresignedUrl` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_generate_presigned_url", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "expiration": 3600, "method": "GET"}) ``` --- ### `get_bucket_location` Get the region (location) of an S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | **Returns:** `S3BucketLocation` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_location", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `get_bucket_policy` Get the bucket policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | **Returns:** `S3BucketPolicy` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_policy", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `get_bucket_versioning` Get bucket versioning configuration | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | **Returns:** `S3BucketVersioning` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_bucket_versioning", {"bucket": "my-bucket"}) ``` --- ### `get_object` Download an object from S3 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | **Returns:** `S3ObjectData` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `get_object_tags` Get tags on an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `version_id` | `string` | No | Optional version ID to get tags for a specific object version. | **Returns:** `S3ObjectTagSet` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_get_object_tags", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "version_id": "your-version_id"}) ``` --- ### `head_object` Get object metadata without downloading the content | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | **Returns:** `S3ObjectMetadata` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_head_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key"}) ``` --- ### `list_buckets` List all S3 buckets in the account **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_buckets", {}) ``` --- ### `list_multipart_uploads` List in-progress multipart uploads in a bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter uploads by key prefix. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum uploads per page (max 1000). (default: `1000`) | | `key_marker` | `string` | No | Key marker from a previous response for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[S3MultipartUpload]` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_multipart_uploads", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "prefix": "your-prefix", "limit": 1000}) ``` --- ### `list_object_versions` List object versions in a bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter versions by key prefix. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum versions per page (max 1000). (default: `1000`) | | `key_marker` | `string` | No | Key marker from a previous response for pagination. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[S3ObjectVersion]` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_object_versions", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "prefix": "your-prefix", "limit": 1000}) ``` --- ### `list_objects` List objects in an S3 bucket | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter objects by key prefix. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of objects per page (max 1000). (default: `1000`) | | `continuation_token` | `string` | No | Token from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[S3Object]` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_list_objects", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "prefix": "your-prefix", "limit": 1000}) ``` --- ### `put_bucket_policy` :warning: Set the bucket policy | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `policy` | `string` | Yes | JSON policy document as a string. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_put_bucket_policy", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "policy": "your-policy"}) ``` --- ### `put_bucket_versioning` :warning: Set bucket versioning configuration | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `status` | `string` | Yes | Versioning status — ``"Enabled"`` or ``"Suspended"``. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_put_bucket_versioning", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "status": "active"}) ``` --- ### `put_object` :warning: Upload an object to S3 | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Object content as bytes. | | `content_type` | `string` | No | MIME type. Defaults to ``application/octet-stream``. | **Returns:** `S3PutResult` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_put_object", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "body": "Hello!", "content_type": "your-content_type"}) ``` --- ### `set_object_tags` Set tags on an S3 object | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bucket` | `string` | Yes | Bucket name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | Object key. | | `tags` | `object` | Yes | Dictionary of tag key-value pairs (max 10 tags). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("s3_set_object_tags", {"bucket": "my-bucket", "key": "my-key", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 5,500 GET/sec, 3,500 PUT/sec per prefix — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_bucket`, `delete_bucket`, `delete_object`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gdrive](../gdrive/) — Google storage --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

salesforce.readme_md: # Salesforce > CRM, sales, service, and marketing platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Crm-F59E0B) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-21-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce?style=social)](https://github.com/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/simple-salesforce/simple-salesforce) [![CLI](https://img.shields.io/badge/CLI-SDK-4D4D4D?logo=github)](https://github.com/forcedotcom/cli) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Salesforce Inc. | | **Category** | Crm | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [salesforce.com](https://salesforce.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.salesforce.com](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Bearer Token | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by edition | | **Pricing** | Enterprise pricing (from $25/user/month) | --- ## Overview The Salesforce REST API provides access to CRM objects including leads, contacts, accounts, opportunities, and cases. Run SOQL queries, manage records, and integrate with the world's most widely-used CRM platform. ## Use Cases - CRM data sync - Lead management - Sales pipeline automation - Customer support workflows - Marketing analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[salesforce]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SALESFORCE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["salesforce"], credentials={"salesforce": "your-token"}) # Run a SOSL search result = kit.execute("salesforce_search", {"sosl": "your-sosl"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["salesforce"], credentials={"salesforce": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["salesforce"], credentials={"salesforce": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Setup 2. Apps 3. App Manager 4. Connected App [Get credentials →](https://login.salesforce.com) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("salesforce_search", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_account` :warning: Create a new Account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Account name. | | `industry` | `string` | No | Industry classification. | | `website` | `string` | No | Company website URL. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_account", {"name": "my-name", "industry": "your-industry", "website": "your-website"}) ``` --- ### `create_case` :warning: Create a new Case | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Case subject line. | | `description` | `string` | No | Detailed case description. | | `contact_id` | `string` | No | Contact ID to associate with the case. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority level (e.g., ``"High"``, ``"Medium"``). | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_case", {"subject": "Hello", "description": "your-description", "contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_contact` :warning: Create a new Contact | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `last_name` | `string` | Yes | Contact last name (required by Salesforce). | | `account_id` | `string` | No | Parent Account ID. | | `email` | `string` | No | Email address. | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Contact first name. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_contact", {"last_name": "your-last_name", "account_id": "your-account_id", "email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `create_event` :warning: Create a new Event | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Event subject line. | | `start` | `string` | Yes | Start datetime in ISO 8601 format. | | `end` | `string` | Yes | End datetime in ISO 8601 format. | | `who_id` | `string` | No | Optional Contact or Lead ID to associate. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_event", {"subject": "Hello", "start": "your-start", "end": "your-end", "who_id": "your-who_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_lead` :warning: Create a new Lead | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `company` | `string` | Yes | Company name (required by Salesforce). | | `last_name` | `string` | Yes | Contact last name (required by Salesforce). | | `email` | `string` | No | Email address. | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Contact first name. | | `phone` | `string` | No | Phone number. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_lead", {"company": "your-company", "last_name": "your-last_name", "email": "user@example.com", "first_name": "your-first_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_opportunity` :warning: Create a new Opportunity | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Opportunity name. | | `stage` | `string` | Yes | Sales stage (e.g., ``"Prospecting"``). | | `close_date` | `string` | Yes | Expected close date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `amount` | `number` | No | Monetary amount of the opportunity. | | `account_id` | `string` | No | Parent Account ID. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_opportunity", {"name": "my-name", "stage": "your-stage", "close_date": "your-close_date", "amount": 10, "account_id": "your-account_id"}) ``` --- ### `create_record` :warning: Create a new sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `fields` | `object` | Yes | Dict of field API names to values. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `create_task` :warning: Create a new Task | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Task subject line. | | `who_id` | `string` | No | Contact or Lead ID (the ``WhoId`` field). | | `what_id` | `string` | No | Account or Opportunity ID (the ``WhatId`` field). | | `due_date` | `string` | No | Due date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `priority` | `string` | No | Priority level (e.g., ``"High"``, ``"Normal"``). | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecordId` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_create_task", {"subject": "Hello", "who_id": "your-who_id", "what_id": "your-what_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_record` :warning: Delete an sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The Salesforce record ID to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_delete_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `describe_global` Describe all sObjects in the org (global describe) **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_describe_global", {}) ``` --- ### `describe_object` Describe an sObject schema | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | **Returns:** `SObjectDescription` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_describe_object", {"sobject": "your-sobject"}) ``` --- ### `get_limits` Get org API usage limits **Returns:** `SalesforceLimits` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_get_limits", {}) ``` --- ### `get_record` Get a single sObject record | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sobject` | `string` | Yes | sObject API name (e.g., ``"Account"``). | | `record_id` | `string` | Yes | The Salesforce record ID. | **Returns:** `SalesforceRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_get_record", {"sobject": "your-sobject", "record_id": "your-record_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_objects` List all sObjects in the org **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("salesforce_list_objects", {}) ``` --- ### secrets_manager.readme_md: # AWS Secrets Manager > Store, rotate, and manage access to secrets securely ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Security-059669) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-12-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-js-v3) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Security | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/secrets-manager](https://aws.amazon.com/secrets-manager/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/secretsmanager/latest/apireference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 | | **Rate Limit** | 50 requests/sec | | **Pricing** | $0.40/secret/month + $0.05/10K API calls | --- ## Overview ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[secrets_manager]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SECRETS_MANAGER_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["secrets_manager"], credentials={"secrets_manager": "your-token"}) # Generate a random password result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_get_random_password", {"password_length": 32, "exclude_characters": ""}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["secrets_manager"], credentials={"secrets_manager": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["secrets_manager"], credentials={"secrets_manager": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 1. Create an account at [AWS Secrets Manager](https://aws.amazon.com/secrets-manager/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_get_random_password", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `create_secret` Create a new secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new secret. | | `secret_string` | `string` | No | The secret value to store. (default: ``) | | `description` | `string` | No | Description of the secret. (default: ``) | | `tags` | `object` | No | Optional dict of tags to apply (key-value pairs). | **Returns:** `SMSecret` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_create_secret", {"name": "my-name", "secret_string": "", "description": ""}) ``` --- ### `delete_secret` :warning: Delete a secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret. | | `recovery_window_in_days` | `integer` | No | Number of days before permanent deletion (7-30). Ignored if force_delete is True. (default: `30`) | | `force_delete` | `boolean` | No | If True, delete immediately without recovery. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_delete_secret", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "recovery_window_in_days": 30, "force_delete": False}) ``` --- ### `describe_secret` Describe a secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret. | **Returns:** `SMSecret` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_describe_secret", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_random_password` Generate a random password | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `password_length` | `integer` | No | Length of the password (default: 32). (default: `32`) | | `exclude_characters` | `string` | No | Characters to exclude. (default: ``) | | `exclude_numbers` | `boolean` | No | If True, exclude digits. (default: `False`) | | `exclude_punctuation` | `boolean` | No | If True, exclude punctuation. (default: `False`) | | `exclude_uppercase` | `boolean` | No | If True, exclude uppercase letters. (default: `False`) | | `exclude_lowercase` | `boolean` | No | If True, exclude lowercase letters. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_get_random_password", {"password_length": 32, "exclude_characters": ""}) ``` --- ### `get_secret_value` Get the value of a secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret. | | `version_id` | `string` | No | Specific version ID to retrieve. (default: ``) | | `version_stage` | `string` | No | Version stage (default: AWSCURRENT). (default: `AWSCURRENT`) | **Returns:** `SMSecretValue` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_get_secret_value", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "version_id": "", "version_stage": "AWSCURRENT"}) ``` --- ### `list_secrets` List secrets | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `max_results` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of secrets to return (1-100). (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_list_secrets", {"max_results": 100}) ``` --- ### `put_secret_value` Update the value of a secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret. | | `secret_string` | `string` | Yes | The new secret value. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_put_secret_value", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "secret_string": "your-secret_string"}) ``` --- ### `restore_secret` Restore a deleted secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret to restore. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_restore_secret", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id"}) ``` --- ### `rotate_secret` Trigger secret rotation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret. | | `rotation_lambda_arn` | `string` | No | ARN of the Lambda function that performs the rotation. Required for first rotation. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_rotate_secret", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "rotation_lambda_arn": ""}) ``` --- ### `tag_resource` Add tags to a secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret to tag. | | `tags` | `array` | Yes | List of tag dicts, each with Key and Value. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_tag_resource", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ### `untag_resource` Remove tags from a secret | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret to untag. | | `tag_keys` | `array` | Yes | List of tag keys to remove. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_untag_resource", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "tag_keys": "your-tag_keys"}) ``` --- ### `update_secret` Update secret metadata | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `secret_id` | `string` | Yes | ARN or name of the secret. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the secret. (default: ``) | | `kms_key_id` | `string` | No | New KMS key ID for encryption. (default: ``) | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("secrets_manager_update_secret", {"secret_id": "your-secret_id", "description": "", "kms_key_id": ""}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 50 requests/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`delete_secret`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Okta](../okta/) — Identity management - [Auth0](../auth0/) — Authentication --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

segment.readme_md: # Segment > Customer data platform and event tracking ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Analytics-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-14-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/segmentio/analytics-python?style=social)](https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/segmentio/analytics-node) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Twilio (Segment) | | **Category** | Analytics | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [segment.com](https://segment.com) | | **API Docs** | [segment.com](https://segment.com/docs/api/) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token (API Token) | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/second | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Team from $120/month | --- ## Overview The Segment API provides access to sources, destinations, tracking events, and user profiles. Collect and route customer data, manage tracking plans, configure destinations, and build unified customer data pipelines. ## Use Cases - Event tracking - Customer data routing - Analytics pipeline management - Data governance - User profile unification ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[segment]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SEGMENT_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["segment"], credentials={"segment": "your-token"}) # Get a specific destination for a source result = kit.execute("segment_get_destination", {"source_id": "your-source_id", "destination_id": "your-destination_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["segment"], credentials={"segment": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["segment"], credentials={"segment": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token (API Token) 1. Create an account at [Segment](https://segment.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.segment.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("segment_get_destination", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `alias` Create an alias for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `previous_id` | `string` | Yes | The previous (anonymous) user identifier. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The new canonical user identifier. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_alias", {"previous_id": "your-previous_id", "user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `batch_identify` :warning: Send a batch of identify calls | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `users` | `array` | Yes | List of user dicts to identify. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_batch_identify", {"users": "your-users"}) ``` --- ### `batch_track` :warning: Send a batch of track events | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `events` | `array` | Yes | List of event dicts to track. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_batch_track", {"events": "your-events"}) ``` --- ### `create_source` :warning: Create a new Segment source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the source. | | `catalog_name` | `string` | Yes | Catalog name (e.g. ``"catalog/sources/javascript"``). | **Returns:** `SegmentSource` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_create_source", {"name": "my-name", "catalog_name": "your-catalog_name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_source` :warning: Delete a Segment source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The source ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_delete_source", {"source_id": "your-source_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_destination` Get a specific destination for a source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The source ID. | | `destination_id` | `string` | Yes | The destination ID. | **Returns:** `SegmentDestination` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_get_destination", {"source_id": "your-source_id", "destination_id": "your-destination_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_source` Get a specific source by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique identifier of the source. | **Returns:** `SegmentSource` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_get_source", {"source_id": "your-source_id"}) ``` --- ### `group` Associate a user with a group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `group_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the group. | | `traits` | `object` | No | Group traits such as name, industry, employees, etc. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_group", {"user_id": "user-123", "group_id": "your-group_id", "traits": "your-traits"}) ``` --- ### `identify` Identify a user with traits | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `traits` | `object` | No | User traits such as name, email, plan, etc. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_identify", {"user_id": "user-123", "traits": "your-traits"}) ``` --- ### `list_destinations` List destinations for a source | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique identifier of the source. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_list_destinations", {"source_id": "your-source_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_sources` List sources in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of sources to return. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SegmentSource]` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_list_sources", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_warehouses` List connected warehouses **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_list_warehouses", {}) ``` --- ### `page` Track a page view | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `name` | `string` | No | Name of the page being viewed. | | `properties` | `object` | No | Additional properties for the page view. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_page", {"user_id": "user-123", "name": "my-name", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ### `track` Track an event for a user | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the user. | | `event` | `string` | Yes | Name of the event to track. | | `properties` | `object` | No | Properties associated with the event. | **Returns:** `SegmentTrackResult` ```python result = kit.execute("segment_track", {"user_id": "user-123", "event": "your-event", "properties": "your-properties"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`batch_identify`, `batch_track`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is 100 requests/second — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`batch_identify`, `batch_track`, `create_source`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Mixpanel](../mixpanel/) — Product analytics --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection
