sendgrid.readme_md: # SendGrid > Transactional and marketing email delivery ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Marketing-EC4899) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/sendgrid/sendgrid-python?style=social)](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/sendgrid/sendgrid-nodejs) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Twilio (SendGrid) | | **Category** | Marketing | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [sendgrid.com](https://sendgrid.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.sendgrid.com](https://docs.sendgrid.com/api-reference) | | **Auth** | API Key | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan | | **Pricing** | Free up to 100 emails/day, Essentials from $19.95/month | --- ## Overview The SendGrid API provides email sending, template management, contact lists, and analytics. Send transactional and marketing emails at scale, manage dynamic templates, track opens and clicks, and maintain sender reputation. ## Use Cases - Transactional email - Marketing campaigns - Email template management - Delivery analytics - Sender reputation management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[sendgrid]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SENDGRID_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["sendgrid"], credentials={"sendgrid": "your-token"}) # List marketing contacts from SendGrid result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_contacts", {"limit": 50}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["sendgrid"], credentials={"sendgrid": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["sendgrid"], credentials={"sendgrid": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key 1. Create an account at [SendGrid](https://sendgrid.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://app.sendgrid.com/settings/api_keys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_contacts", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_contacts` :warning: Add or update marketing contacts in SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contacts` | `array` | Yes | List of contact dicts with at least ``email`` key. | **Returns:** `SendGridJobId` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_add_contacts", {"contacts": "your-contacts"}) ``` --- ### `add_to_suppression` :warning: Add emails to global suppression group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `emails` | `array` | Yes | List of email addresses to globally suppress. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_add_to_suppression", {"emails": "your-emails"}) ``` --- ### `create_list` :warning: Create a new contact list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new list. | **Returns:** `SendGridList` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_create_list", {"name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_contact` :warning: Delete a contact by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `contact_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid contact ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_delete_contact", {"contact_id": "your-contact_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_bounces` Get bounced email addresses | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of bounce records to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_bounces", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_email_activity` Query email activity feed | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | No | SGQL-style filter query string (e.g. ``"status=delivered AND to_email=user@example.com"``). If omitted, returns the most recent messages. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages to return (default 10). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_email_activity", {"query": "is:unread", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_global_suppressions` Get global suppressions with limit | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of suppression records to return. | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Starting index for pagination. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_global_suppressions", {"limit": 10, "offset": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_spam_reports` Get spam report records | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of spam reports to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_spam_reports", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_stats` Retrieve email statistics from SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `start_date` | `string` | Yes | Start date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. | | `end_date` | `string` | No | End date in ``YYYY-MM-DD`` format. Defaults to today. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_stats", {"start_date": "your-start_date", "end_date": "your-end_date"}) ``` --- ### `get_template` Retrieve a single SendGrid template by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `template_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid template ID. | **Returns:** `SendGridTemplate` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_template", {"template_id": "your-template_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_template_version` Get a specific template version | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `template_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid template ID. | | `version_id` | `string` | Yes | The template version ID. | **Returns:** `SendGridTemplateVersion` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_get_template_version", {"template_id": "your-template_id", "version_id": "your-version_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_contacts` List marketing contacts from SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of contacts to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SendGridContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_contacts", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_lists` List all contact lists in SendGrid **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_lists", {}) ``` --- ### `list_sender_identities` List verified sender identities **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_sender_identities", {}) ``` --- ### `list_suppressions` List global email suppressions **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_suppressions", {}) ``` --- ### `list_templates` List transactional templates from SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of templates to return. (default: `50`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SendGridTemplate]` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_list_templates", {"limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `remove_from_suppression` :warning: Remove an email from global suppression group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The email address to remove from global suppression. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_remove_from_suppression", {"email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `search_contacts` Search marketing contacts using a SGQL query | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | SGQL query string. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SendGridContact]` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_search_contacts", {"query": "is:unread"}) ``` --- ### `send_email` :warning: Send an email via SendGrid | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `from_email` | `string` | Yes | Sender email address. | | `subject` | `string` | Yes | Email subject line. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Email body content. | | `content_type` | `string` | No | MIME type of content (``text/plain`` or ``text/html``). Defaults to ``text/plain``. (default: `text/plain`) | **Returns:** `SendGridResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_send_email", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_email": "your-from_email", "subject": "Hello", "content": "your-content", "content_type": "text/plain"}) ``` --- ### `send_template_email` :warning: Send an email using a dynamic template | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient email address. | | `from_email` | `string` | Yes | Sender email address. | | `template_id` | `string` | Yes | The SendGrid template ID to use. | | `dynamic_data` | `object` | No | Dynamic template data (Handlebars variables). | **Returns:** `SendGridResponse` ```python result = kit.execute("sendgrid_send_template_email", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_email": "your-from_email", "template_id": "your-template_id", "dynamic_data": "your-dynamic_data"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_contacts` for filtered queries and `list_contacts` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is Varies by plan — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_contacts`, `add_to_suppression`, `create_list`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Mailchimp](../mailchimp/) — Email marketing --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

shopify.readme_md: # Shopify > E-commerce platform for online stores ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Ecommerce-84CC16) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-27-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/Shopify/shopify-api-python?style=social)](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-api-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-api-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/Shopify/shopify-api-js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Shopify Inc. | | **Category** | Ecommerce | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [shopify.com](https://shopify.com) | | **API Docs** | [shopify.dev](https://shopify.dev/docs/api/admin-rest) | | **Auth** | API Key (Admin API Access Token) | | **Rate Limit** | 40 requests/second (Plus: 80/sec) | | **Pricing** | Basic from $39/month | --- ## Overview The Shopify Admin API provides access to products, orders, customers, inventory, and fulfillments. Manage your online store, process orders, track inventory, handle refunds, and build custom e-commerce integrations. ## Use Cases - Product catalog management - Order processing - Inventory tracking - Customer management - Fulfillment automation ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[shopify]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SHOPIFY_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["shopify"], credentials={"shopify": "your-token"}) # List products from your Shopify store result = kit.execute("shopify_list_products", {"limit": 50, "since_id": "your-since_id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["shopify"], credentials={"shopify": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["shopify"], credentials={"shopify": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (Admin API Access Token) 1. Create an account at [Shopify](https://shopify.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://shopify.dev/docs/apps/build/authentication/access-tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("shopify_list_products", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_order` :warning: Cancel an order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_cancel_order", {"order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `complete_draft_order` :warning: Complete a draft order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `draft_order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify draft order ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyDraftOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_complete_draft_order", {"draft_order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `count_products` Count products in your Shopify store **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_count_products", {}) ``` --- ### `create_customer` :warning: Create a new customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | Customer email address (required). | | `first_name` | `string` | No | Customer's first name. | | `last_name` | `string` | No | Customer's last name. | **Returns:** `ShopifyCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_customer", {"email": "user@example.com", "first_name": "your-first_name", "last_name": "your-last_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_discount` :warning: Create a price rule / discount | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | The discount title. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | The discount value (e.g. ``"-10.0"`` for 10 off). | | `type` | `string` | Yes | Value type (``"percentage"`` or ``"fixed_amount"``). | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_discount", {"title": "My Title", "value": "my-value", "type": "your-type"}) ``` --- ### `create_draft_order` :warning: Create a draft order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `line_items` | `array` | Yes | List of line item dicts, each containing ``variant_id`` and ``quantity`` (or ``title``, ``price``, and ``quantity`` for custom items). | | `customer` | `object` | No | Optional customer dict with an ``id`` key to associate the draft order with an existing customer. | | `note` | `string` | No | Optional note text for the draft order. | **Returns:** `ShopifyDraftOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_draft_order", {"line_items": "your-line_items", "customer": "your-customer", "note": "your-note"}) ``` --- ### `create_product` :warning: Create a new product in your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `title` | `string` | Yes | Product title. | | `body_html` | `string` | No | HTML description of the product. | | `vendor` | `string` | No | Product vendor name. | | `product_type` | `string` | No | Product type/category. | | `variants` | `array` | No | List of variant dicts (price, sku, etc.). | **Returns:** `ShopifyProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_product", {"title": "My Title", "body_html": "your-body_html", "vendor": "your-vendor"}) ``` --- ### `create_product_variant` :warning: Create a variant for a product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | | `option1` | `string` | Yes | Value for the first product option (e.g. size/colour). | | `price` | `string` | Yes | Variant price as a decimal string (e.g. ``"29.99"``). | | `sku` | `string` | No | Optional stock keeping unit identifier. | **Returns:** `ShopifyVariant` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_product_variant", {"product_id": 10, "option1": "your-option1", "price": "your-price", "sku": "your-sku"}) ``` --- ### `create_webhook` :warning: Create a webhook subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `topic` | `string` | Yes | Event topic to subscribe to (e.g. ``"orders/create"``, ``"products/update"``). | | `address` | `string` | Yes | HTTPS URL that will receive webhook POST requests. | | `format_` | `string` | No | Payload format (``"json"`` or ``"xml"``). (default: `json`) | **Returns:** `ShopifyWebhook` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_create_webhook", {"topic": "your-topic", "address": "your-address", "format_": "json"}) ``` --- ### `delete_product` :warning: Delete a product from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_delete_product", {"product_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `fulfill_order` :warning: Fulfill an order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID to fulfill. | | `tracking_number` | `string` | No | Optional shipment tracking number. | | `tracking_company` | `string` | No | Optional shipping carrier name. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_fulfill_order", {"order_id": 10, "tracking_number": "your-tracking_number", "tracking_company": "your-tracking_company"}) ``` --- ### `get_customer` Get a single Shopify customer by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify customer ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_get_customer", {"customer_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_order` Get a single Shopify order by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_get_order", {"order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_product` Get a single Shopify product by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | **Returns:** `ShopifyProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_get_product", {"product_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_collections` List collections from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of collections to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_collections", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_customers` List customers from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of customers to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `integer` | No | Only return customers after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyCustomer]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_customers", {"limit": 50, "since_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_draft_orders` List draft orders from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of draft orders to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `integer` | No | Only return draft orders after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyDraftOrder]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_draft_orders", {"limit": 50, "since_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_fulfillments` List fulfillments for an order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_fulfillments", {"order_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_inventory_levels` List inventory levels by location | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `location_id` | `integer` | No | Optional location ID to filter by. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_inventory_levels", {"location_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_locations` List locations for your Shopify store **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_locations", {}) ``` --- ### `list_orders` List orders from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (open, closed, cancelled, any). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of orders to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `integer` | No | Only return orders after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyOrder]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_orders", {"status": "active", "limit": 50}) ``` --- ### `list_product_variants` List variants for a product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_product_variants", {"product_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_products` List products from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of products to return (1-250). (default: `50`) | | `since_id` | `integer` | No | Only return products after this ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ShopifyProduct]` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_products", {"limit": 50, "since_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_webhooks` List webhooks from your Shopify store | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of webhooks to return (max 250). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_list_webhooks", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `update_inventory` Update inventory quantity for an item | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `inventory_item_id` | `integer` | Yes | The inventory item ID. | | `available` | `integer` | Yes | The new available quantity. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_update_inventory", {"inventory_item_id": 10, "available": 10}) ``` --- ### `update_order` Update an existing order | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `order_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify order ID. | | `note` | `string` | No | New note for the order. | **Returns:** `ShopifyOrder` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_update_order", {"order_id": 10, "note": "your-note"}) ``` --- ### `update_product` :warning: Update an existing Shopify product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product_id` | `integer` | Yes | The Shopify product ID. | | `title` | `string` | No | New product title. | | `body_html` | `string` | No | New HTML description. | **Returns:** `ShopifyProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("shopify_update_product", {"product_id": 10, "title": "My Title", "body_html": "your-body_html"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 40 requests/second (Plus: 80/sec) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_order`, `complete_draft_order`, `create_customer`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 27 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Stripe](../stripe/) — Payments --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

slack.readme_md: # Slack > Team messaging, channels, and collaboration ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-51-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/slackapi/python-slack-sdk?style=social)](https://github.com/slackapi/python-slack-sdk) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/slackapi/python-slack-sdk) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/slackapi/node-slack-sdk) [![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-SDK-ED8B00?logo=github)](https://github.com/slackapi/java-slack-sdk) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Salesforce (Slack Technologies) | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [slack.com](https://slack.com) | | **API Docs** | [api.slack.com](https://api.slack.com/methods) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0, Bot Token | | **Rate Limit** | 1 request/second (Tier 2) | | **Pricing** | Free tier available, Pro from $8.75/user/month | --- ## Overview The Slack Web API provides comprehensive access to Slack workspaces. Send and manage messages, create and manage channels, upload files, manage users, set reminders, and build interactive bot experiences. Supports both bot tokens and user tokens. ## Use Cases - Team notifications and alerts - Customer support bots - DevOps alerting - Workflow automation - Internal tooling ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[slack]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SLACK_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["slack"], credentials={"slack": "your-token"}) # List messages in a channel result = kit.execute("slack_list_messages", {"channel": "general", "limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["slack"], credentials={"slack": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["slack"], credentials={"slack": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 1. Slack API 2. Your Apps 3. Create New App 4. Bot Token [Get credentials →](https://api.slack.com/apps) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("slack_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_bookmark` Add a bookmark to a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | Display title for the bookmark. | | `link` | `string` | Yes | URL for the bookmark. | | `emoji` | `string` | No | Optional emoji to display (e.g. ``:link:``). | **Returns:** `Bookmark` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_add_bookmark", {"channel_id": "C01234567", "title": "My Title", "link": "your-link", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `add_reaction` Add a reaction emoji to a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field) to react to. | | `emoji` | `string` | Yes | Emoji name without colons (e.g. ``thumbsup``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_add_reaction", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `add_reminder` Create a reminder | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `text` | `string` | Yes | The reminder text. | | `time` | `string` | Yes | When to trigger — Unix timestamp, or natural language like ``in 15 minutes``, ``every Thursday``. | | `user` | `string` | No | Optional user ID. Defaults to the authed user. | **Returns:** `Reminder` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_add_reminder", {"text": "Hello!", "time": "your-time", "user": "your-user"}) ``` --- ### `archive_channel` :warning: Archive a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to archive. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_archive_channel", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `auth_test` Test authentication and get workspace info **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_auth_test", {}) ``` --- ### `create_channel` :warning: Create a new channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the channel (lowercase, no spaces, max 80 characters). | | `is_private` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, create a private channel. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_create_channel", {"name": "my-name", "is_private": False}) ``` --- ### `create_usergroup` :warning: Create a user group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the user group. | | `handle` | `string` | Yes | Short mention handle (e.g. ``engineering`` for ``@engineering``). "channels": "your-channels"}) ``` --- ### `delete_file` :warning: Delete a file | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `file_id` | `string` | Yes | The file ID to delete. | (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[ScheduledMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_scheduled_messages", {"channel": "general", "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_thread_replies` List replies in a message thread | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the thread. | | `thread_ts` | `string` | Yes | Timestamp of the parent message. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of replies to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[Message]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_thread_replies", {"channel": "general", "thread_ts": "your-thread_ts", "limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `list_usergroups` List user groups in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `include_users` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, include the list of user IDs for each user group. (default: `False`) | | `include_disabled` | `boolean` | No | If ``True``, include disabled user groups. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_usergroups", {"include_users": False, "include_disabled": False}) ``` --- ### `list_users` List users in the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of users to return (max 1000). (default: `100`) | | `cursor` | `string` | No | Pagination cursor from a previous response. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SlackUser]` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_list_users", {"limit": 100, "cursor": "your-cursor"}) ``` --- ### `lookup_user_by_email` Look up a user by email address | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The email address to look up. | **Returns:** `SlackUser` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_lookup_user_by_email", {"email": "user@example.com"}) ``` --- ### `pin_message` Pin a message to a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field) to pin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_pin_message", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp"}) ``` --- ### `remove_bookmark` :warning: Remove a bookmark from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `bookmark_id` | `string` | Yes | The bookmark ID. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The channel ID. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_remove_bookmark", {"bookmark_id": "your-bookmark_id", "channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `remove_reaction` Remove a reaction emoji from a message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field). | | `emoji` | `string` | Yes | Emoji name without colons (e.g. ``thumbsup``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_remove_reaction", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp", "emoji": "your-emoji"}) ``` --- ### `rename_channel` :warning: Rename a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to rename. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | New channel name (lowercase, no spaces, max 80 chars). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_rename_channel", {"channel": "general", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `schedule_message` :warning: Schedule a message for future delivery | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to send to. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Message text (supports Slack mrkdwn formatting). | | `post_at` | `integer` | Yes | Unix timestamp for when to send the message. | | `thread_ts` | `string` | No | Optional parent message timestamp for threading. | **Returns:** `ScheduledMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_schedule_message", {"channel": "general", "text": "Hello!", "post_at": 10, "thread_ts": "your-thread_ts"}) ``` --- ### `search_messages` Search for messages across the workspace | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `query` | `string` | Yes | Search query (supports Slack search modifiers like ``from:@user``, ``in:#channel``, ``has:link``). | | `sort` | `string` | No | Sort field — ``timestamp`` or ``score``. (default: `timestamp`) | | `sort_dir` | `string` | No | Sort direction — ``asc`` or ``desc``. (default: `desc`) | | `count` | `integer` | No | Number of results per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `integer` | No | Page number (1-based). (default: `True`) | | `unfurl_media` | `boolean` | No | Whether to unfurl media content. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `Message` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_send_message", {"channel": "general", "text": "Hello!", "thread_ts": "your-thread_ts", "unfurl_links": True}) ``` --- ### `set_channel_purpose` Set the purpose of a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `purpose` | `string` | Yes | New purpose string (max 250 characters). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_channel_purpose", {"channel": "general", "purpose": "your-purpose"}) ``` --- ### `set_channel_topic` Set the topic of a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `topic` | `string` | Yes | New topic string (max 250 characters). | **Returns:** `Channel` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_channel_topic", {"channel": "general", "topic": "your-topic"}) ``` --- ### `set_presence` Set the bot user's presence status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `presence` | `string` | Yes | Either ``auto`` or ``away``. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_presence", {"presence": "your-presence"}) ``` --- ### `set_status` :warning: Set the authenticated user's status | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `status_text` | `string` | Yes | Status text to display (max 100 characters). | | `status_emoji` | `string` | No | Optional emoji code (e.g. ``:house_with_garden:``). Pass an empty string to clear the emoji. | | `expiration` | `integer` | No | Optional Unix timestamp for when the status should expire. Pass ``0`` to keep the status indefinitely. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_set_status", {"status_text": "your-status_text", "status_emoji": "your-status_emoji", "expiration": 10}) ``` --- ### `unarchive_channel` Unarchive a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID to unarchive. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_unarchive_channel", {"channel": "general"}) ``` --- ### `unpin_message` Unpin a message from a channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID. | | `timestamp` | `string` | Yes | Message timestamp (``ts`` field) to unpin. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_unpin_message", {"channel": "general", "timestamp": "your-timestamp"}) ``` --- ### `update_message` :warning: Update an existing message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channel` | `string` | Yes | Channel ID containing the message. | | `ts` | `string` | Yes | Timestamp (``ts``) of the message to update. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | New message text (supports Slack mrkdwn formatting). | **Returns:** `Message` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_update_message", {"channel": "general", "ts": "your-ts", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `update_usergroup` :warning: Update an existing user group | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `usergroup_id` | `string` | Yes | The user group ID (e.g. ``S01234ABCDE``). | | `name` | `string` | No | Optional new display name. | | `handle` | `string` | No | Optional new mention handle. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional new description. | | `channels` | `string` | No | Optional comma-separated channel IDs to set as default channels. | **Returns:** `SlackUserGroup` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_update_usergroup", {"usergroup_id": "your-usergroup_id", "name": "my-name", "handle": "your-handle"}) ``` --- ### `upload_file` :warning: Upload a file to Slack channels | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `channels` | `string` | Yes | Comma-separated channel IDs to share the file in. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | File content as a string. | | `filename` | `string` | Yes | Name for the uploaded file. | | `title` | `string` | No | Optional display title for the file. | **Returns:** `SlackFile` ```python result = kit.execute("slack_upload_file", {"channels": "your-channels", "content": "your-content", "filename": "your-filename", "title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use `search_messages` for filtered queries and `list_bookmarks` for paginated browsing - Rate limit is 1 request/second (Tier 2) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`archive_channel`, `create_channel`, `create_usergroup`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration - [Telegram](../telegram/) — Bot messaging --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.

sqs.readme_md: # AWS SQS > Fully managed message queuing service ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Message_Queue-EF4444) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/boto/boto3?style=social)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/boto/boto3) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Amazon Web Services | | **Category** | Message Queue | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [aws.amazon.com/sqs](https://aws.amazon.com/sqs/) | | **API Docs** | [docs.aws.amazon.com](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSSimpleQueueService/latest/APIReference/) | | **Auth** | AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) | | **Rate Limit** | Unlimited (Standard), 300 msg/sec (FIFO) | | **Pricing** | Free tier (1M requests/month), then $0.40/million | --- ## Overview The AWS SQS API provides message queuing operations for decoupling distributed systems. Send, receive, and delete messages. Manage queues, configure dead-letter queues, and build reliable event-driven architectures. ## Use Cases - Microservice decoupling - Task queuing - Event-driven architectures - Batch processing - Dead-letter queue management ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[sqs]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SQS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["sqs"], credentials={"sqs": "your-token"}) # List SQS queues in the account result = kit.execute("sqs_list_queues", {"prefix": "your-prefix"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["sqs"], credentials={"sqs": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["sqs"], credentials={"sqs": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### AWS SigV4 (Access Key + Secret Key) 1. Create an account at [AWS SQS](https://aws.amazon.com/sqs/) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/home#/security_credentials) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("sqs_list_queues", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_permission` :warning: Add permission to a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `label` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the permission being set (max 80 chars, alphanumeric, hyphens, and underscores). | | `aws_account_ids` | `array` | Yes | List of AWS account IDs to grant permission to. | | `actions` | `array` | Yes | List of SQS actions to allow (e.g. ``SendMessage``, ``ReceiveMessage``, ``DeleteMessage``, ``ChangeMessageVisibility``, ``GetQueueAttributes``, ``GetQueueUrl``, or ``*``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_add_permission", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "label": "your-label", "aws_account_ids": "your-aws_account_ids", "actions": "your-actions"}) ``` --- ### `change_message_visibility` Change message visibility timeout | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `receipt_handle` | `string` | Yes | The receipt handle of the message. | | `timeout` | `integer` | Yes | New visibility timeout in seconds (0-43200). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_change_message_visibility", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "receipt_handle": "your-receipt_handle", "timeout": 10}) ``` --- ### `create_queue` Create a new SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new queue. | | `attributes` | `object` | No | Optional queue attributes (e.g. VisibilityTimeout). | **Returns:** `SQSQueue` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_create_queue", {"queue_name": "your-queue_name", "attributes": "your-attributes"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `receipt_handle` | `string` | Yes | The receipt handle of the message to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_delete_message", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "receipt_handle": "your-receipt_handle"}) ``` --- ### `delete_queue` :warning: Delete an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_delete_queue", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url"}) ``` --- ### `get_queue_attributes` Get attributes of an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | **Returns:** `SQSQueueAttributes` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_get_queue_attributes", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url"}) ``` --- ### `get_queue_url` Get queue URL by name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_name` | `string` | Yes | The name of the SQS queue. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_get_queue_url", {"queue_name": "your-queue_name"}) ``` --- ### `list_dead_letter_queues` List dead letter source queues | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `source_queue_arn` | `string` | Yes | The ARN of the dead-letter queue to query. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_list_dead_letter_queues", {"source_queue_arn": "your-source_queue_arn"}) ``` --- ### `list_queues` List SQS queues in the account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `prefix` | `string` | No | Filter queues to those whose name starts with this prefix. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SQSQueue]` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_list_queues", {"prefix": "your-prefix"}) ``` --- ### `purge_queue` :warning: Purge all messages from an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue to purge. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_purge_queue", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url"}) ``` --- ### `receive_messages` Receive messages from an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue to poll. | | `max_messages` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages to receive (1-10). (default: `1`) | | `wait_time` | `integer` | No | Long-poll wait time in seconds (0-20). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_receive_messages", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "max_messages": 1, "wait_time": 10}) ``` --- ### `remove_permission` :warning: Remove permission from a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the SQS queue. | | `label` | `string` | Yes | The identifier of the permission to remove (the label used when calling ``add_permission``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_remove_permission", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "label": "your-label"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` Send a message to an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the target SQS queue. | | `message_body` | `string` | Yes | The message body string. | | `delay_seconds` | `integer` | No | Delay in seconds before the message becomes visible. | **Returns:** `SQSSendResult` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_send_message", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "message_body": "your-message_body", "delay_seconds": 10}) ``` --- ### `send_message_batch` Send a batch of messages to an SQS queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the target SQS queue. | | `entries` | `array` | Yes | List of message entries, each containing ``Id`` and ``MessageBody``, and optionally ``DelaySeconds``. | **Returns:** `SQSBatchResult` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_send_message_batch", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "entries": "your-entries"}) ``` --- ### `set_queue_attributes` Set attributes on a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue. | | `attributes` | `object` | Yes | Dict of attribute names to values. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_set_queue_attributes", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "attributes": "your-attributes"}) ``` --- ### `tag_queue` Add tags to a queue | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `queue_url` | `string` | Yes | The URL of the queue. | | `tags` | `object` | Yes | Dict of tag keys to values. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("sqs_tag_queue", {"queue_url": "your-queue_url", "tags": "your-tags"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Use batch operations (`send_message_batch`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls - Rate limit is Unlimited (Standard), 300 msg/sec (FIFO) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_permission`, `delete_message`, `delete_queue`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Rabbitmq](../rabbitmq/) — Message broker --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

stripe.readme_md: # Stripe > Payment processing, subscriptions, and billing ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Finance-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-40-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/stripe/stripe-python?style=social)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-node) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-go) [![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-SDK-ED8B00?logo=github)](https://github.com/stripe/stripe-java) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Stripe Inc. | | **Category** | Finance | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [stripe.com](https://stripe.com) | | **API Docs** | [docs.stripe.com](https://docs.stripe.com/api) | | **Auth** | API Key (Secret Key) | | **Rate Limit** | 100 read/sec, 100 write/sec | | **Pricing** | 2.9% + 30c per transaction | --- ## Overview The Stripe API provides a complete payment infrastructure. Process payments, manage subscriptions, handle invoices, issue refunds, and build checkout experiences. Supports cards, bank transfers, and 100+ payment methods worldwide. ## Use Cases - Payment processing - Subscription management - Invoice generation - Marketplace payments - Revenue analytics ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[stripe]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_STRIPE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["stripe"], credentials={"stripe": "your-token"}) # List events from your Stripe account result = kit.execute("stripe_list_events", {"type": "your-type", "limit": 10}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["stripe"], credentials={"stripe": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["stripe"], credentials={"stripe": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (Secret Key) 1. Dashboard 2. Developers 3. API Keys [Get credentials →](https://dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("stripe_list_events", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `cancel_payment_intent` Cancel a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The PaymentIntent ID to cancel. | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_cancel_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id"}) ``` --- ### `cancel_subscription` :warning: Cancel a subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subscription_id` | `string` | Yes | The subscription ID to cancel. | | `at_period_end` | `boolean` | No | If True, cancel at end of current billing period. If False, cancel immediately. (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `StripeSubscription` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_cancel_subscription", {"subscription_id": "your-subscription_id", "at_period_end": True}) ``` --- ### `capture_payment_intent` :warning: Capture a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The PaymentIntent ID to capture. | | `amount_to_capture` | `integer` | No | Amount to capture in the smallest currency unit. Omit to capture the full authorized amount. | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_capture_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id", "amount_to_capture": 10}) ``` --- ### `close_dispute` :warning: Close a Stripe dispute | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dispute_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe dispute ID to close. | **Returns:** `StripeDispute` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_close_dispute", {"dispute_id": "your-dispute_id"}) ``` --- ### `confirm_payment_intent` :warning: Confirm a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The PaymentIntent ID to confirm. | | `payment_method` | `string` | No | Optional payment method ID to attach before confirming (e.g. ``pm_...``). | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_confirm_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id", "payment_method": "your-payment_method"}) ``` --- ### `create_charge` :warning: Create a charge | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `amount` | `integer` | Yes | Amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents). | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | | `customer` | `string` | No | Customer ID to charge. | | `source` | `string` | No | Payment source token or ID. | | `description` | `string` | No | Arbitrary description for the charge. | | `metadata` | `object` | No | Key-value metadata to attach. | **Returns:** `StripeCharge` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_charge", {"amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency", "customer": "your-customer", "source": "your-source"}) ``` --- ### `create_checkout_session` :warning: Create a Stripe Checkout Session | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `line_items` | `array` | Yes | List of line item dicts, each with ``price`` (price ID) and ``quantity``. Example: ``[{"price": "price_xxx", "quantity": 1}]``. | | `mode` | `string` | Yes | Checkout mode: ``payment``, ``subscription``, or ``setup``. | | `success_url` | `string` | Yes | URL to redirect to after successful payment. | | `cancel_url` | `string` | Yes | URL to redirect to if the user cancels. | **Returns:** `StripeCheckoutSession` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_checkout_session", {"line_items": "your-line_items", "mode": "your-mode", "success_url": "your-success_url", "cancel_url": "your-cancel_url"}) ``` --- ### `create_customer` :warning: Create a new Stripe customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | No | Customer email address. | | `name` | `string` | No | Customer full name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Arbitrary description for the customer. | | `metadata` | `object` | No | Key-value metadata to attach to the customer. | **Returns:** `StripeCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_customer", {"email": "user@example.com", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_payment_intent` :warning: Create a Stripe PaymentIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `amount` | `integer` | Yes | Amount in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents). | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | | `customer` | `string` | No | Optional customer ID to associate with the intent. | | `description` | `string` | No | Arbitrary description for the payment. | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_payment_intent", {"amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency", "customer": "your-customer", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `create_payout` :warning: Create a payout to your bank account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `amount` | `integer` | Yes | Amount to pay out in the smallest currency unit (e.g. cents). | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | **Returns:** `StripePayout` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_payout", {"amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency"}) ``` --- ### `create_price` :warning: Create a Stripe price | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product` | `string` | Yes | The product ID to attach this price to. | | `unit_amount` | `integer` | Yes | Price amount in cents. | | `currency` | `string` | Yes | Three-letter ISO currency code (e.g. ``usd``). | | `recurring_interval` | `string` | No | If set, makes this a recurring price. One of ``day``, ``week``, ``month``, or ``year``. | **Returns:** `StripePrice` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_price", {"product": "your-product", "unit_amount": 10, "currency": "your-currency", "recurring_interval": "your-recurring_interval"}) ``` --- ### `create_product` :warning: Create a Stripe product | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | Product name. | | `description` | `string` | No | Product description. | | `metadata` | `object` | No | Key-value metadata to attach. | **Returns:** `StripeProduct` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_product", {"name": "my-name", "description": "your-description", "metadata": "your-metadata"}) ``` --- ### `create_setup_intent` Create a Stripe SetupIntent | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Optional customer ID to attach the payment method to. | | `payment_method_types` | `array` | No | List of payment method types to accept (e.g. ``["card"]``). Defaults to Stripe's automatic detection if omitted. | **Returns:** `StripeSetupIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_setup_intent", {"customer": "your-customer", "payment_method_types": "your-payment_method_types"}) ``` --- ### `create_subscription` :warning: Create a subscription | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | Yes | The customer ID to subscribe. | | `price` | `string` | Yes | The price ID for the subscription. | | `trial_days` | `integer` | No | Number of days for a free trial period. | **Returns:** `StripeSubscription` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_create_subscription", {"customer": "your-customer", "price": "your-price", "trial_days": 10}) ``` --- ### `delete_customer` :warning: Delete a Stripe customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe customer ID (e.g. ``cus_...``). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_delete_customer", {"customer_id": "your-customer_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_balance` Retrieve the current Stripe account balance **Returns:** `StripeBalance` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_balance", {}) ``` --- ### `get_charge` Retrieve a single Stripe charge by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `charge_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe charge ID (e.g. ``ch_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeCharge` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_charge", {"charge_id": "your-charge_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_customer` Retrieve a single Stripe customer by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe customer ID (e.g. ``cus_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_customer", {"customer_id": "your-customer_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_dispute` Retrieve a single Stripe dispute by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `dispute_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe dispute ID (e.g. ``dp_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeDispute` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_dispute", {"dispute_id": "your-dispute_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_event` Retrieve a single Stripe event by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `event_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe event ID (e.g. ``evt_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeEvent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_event", {"event_id": "your-event_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_invoice` Retrieve a single Stripe invoice by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `invoice_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe invoice ID (e.g. ``in_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeInvoice` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_invoice", {"invoice_id": "your-invoice_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_payment_intent` Retrieve a single Stripe PaymentIntent by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payment_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe PaymentIntent ID (e.g. ``pi_...``). | **Returns:** `PaymentIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_payment_intent", {"payment_intent_id": "your-payment_intent_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_payout` Retrieve a single Stripe payout by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `payout_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe payout ID (e.g. ``po_...``). | **Returns:** `StripePayout` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_payout", {"payout_id": "your-payout_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_setup_intent` Retrieve a single Stripe SetupIntent by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `setup_intent_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe SetupIntent ID (e.g. ``seti_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeSetupIntent` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_setup_intent", {"setup_intent_id": "your-setup_intent_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_subscription` Retrieve a single Stripe subscription by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `subscription_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe subscription ID (e.g. ``sub_...``). | **Returns:** `StripeSubscription` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_get_subscription", {"subscription_id": "your-subscription_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_charges` List charges from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter charges by customer ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of charges to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Charge ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeCharge]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_charges", {"customer": "your-customer", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_customers` List customers from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of customers to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Customer ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeCustomer]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_customers", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_disputes` List disputes from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of disputes to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Dispute ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeDispute]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_disputes", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_events` List events from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `type` | `string` | No | Filter by event type (e.g. ``charge.succeeded``, ``invoice.payment_failed``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of events to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Event ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeEvent]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_events", {"type": "your-type", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_invoices` List invoices from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter invoices by customer ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of invoices to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Invoice ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeInvoice]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_invoices", {"customer": "your-customer", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_payment_intents` List PaymentIntents from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter by customer ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of PaymentIntents to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | PaymentIntent ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[PaymentIntent]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_payment_intents", {"customer": "your-customer", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_payment_methods` List payment methods for a customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | Yes | The customer ID to list payment methods for. | | `type` | `string` | No | Filter by payment method type (e.g. ``card``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of payment methods to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Payment method ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripePaymentMethod]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_payment_methods", {"customer": "your-customer", "type": "your-type", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_payouts` List payouts from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of payouts to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Payout ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripePayout]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_payouts", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_prices` List prices from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `product` | `string` | No | Filter prices by product ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of prices to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Price ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripePrice]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_prices", {"product": "your-product", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_products` List products from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of products to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Product ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeProduct]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_products", {"limit": 10, "starting_after": "your-starting_after"}) ``` --- ### `list_refunds` List refunds from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `charge` | `string` | No | Filter refunds by charge ID. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of refunds to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Refund ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeRefund]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_refunds", {"charge": "your-charge", "limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_subscriptions` List subscriptions from your Stripe account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer` | `string` | No | Filter by customer ID. | | `status` | `string` | No | Filter by status (e.g. ``active``, ``past_due``, ``canceled``, ``all``). | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of subscriptions to return (1-100). (default: `10`) | | `starting_after` | `string` | No | Subscription ID to paginate after (cursor). | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[StripeSubscription]` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_list_subscriptions", {"customer": "your-customer", "status": "active"}) ``` --- ### `refund_charge` :warning: Refund a charge | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `charge_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the charge to refund. | | `amount` | `integer` | No | Amount to refund in cents. Omit for full refund. | | `reason` | `string` | No | Reason for the refund: ``duplicate``, ``fraudulent``, or ``requested_by_customer``. | **Returns:** `StripeRefund` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_refund_charge", {"charge_id": "your-charge_id", "amount": 10, "reason": "your-reason"}) ``` --- ### `update_customer` :warning: Update a Stripe customer | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `customer_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe customer ID (e.g. ``cus_...``). | | `email` | `string` | No | New email address for the customer. | | `name` | `string` | No | New name for the customer. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the customer. | | `metadata` | `object` | No | Key-value metadata to set on the customer. | **Returns:** `StripeCustomer` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_update_customer", {"customer_id": "your-customer_id", "email": "user@example.com", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `void_invoice` :warning: Void a Stripe invoice | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `invoice_id` | `string` | Yes | The Stripe invoice ID to void. | **Returns:** `StripeInvoice` ```python result = kit.execute("stripe_void_invoice", {"invoice_id": "your-invoice_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 100 read/sec, 100 write/sec — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`cancel_subscription`, `capture_payment_intent`, `close_dispute`) cannot be undone — use with caution - Use cursor-based pagination for large result sets — pass the `cursor` from previous responses - This connector has 40 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Plaid](../plaid/) — Banking data --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

PageState.description:
Tracks pagination state across pages.

Supports cursor-based, offset-based, and page-number-based pagination
strategies. Connectors populate whichever fields match their API's
pagination model and ignore the rest.

Attributes:
    cursor: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination.
    offset: Numeric offset for offset-based pagination.
    page_number: Current page number for page-number-based pagination.
    total_count: Total number of items available on the server, if known.
    has_more: Whether additional pages exist beyond the current one.
    extra: Arbitrary connector-specific pagination metadata.
