supabase.readme_md: # Supabase > Open-source Firebase alternative with Postgres ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Database-10B981) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/supabase/supabase-py?style=social)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-py) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-py) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/supabase/supabase-js) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Supabase Inc. | | **Category** | Database | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [supabase.com](https://supabase.com) | | **API Docs** | [supabase.com](https://supabase.com/docs/guides/api) | | **Auth** | API Key (anon/service_role) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by plan | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Pro from $25/month | --- ## Overview The Supabase API provides RESTful access to your Postgres database, authentication, and storage. Query tables, manage rows, handle file uploads, and build full-stack applications with Supabase's auto-generated API. ## Use Cases - Database CRUD operations - User authentication - File storage - Real-time subscriptions - Serverless backends ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[supabase]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_SUPABASE_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["supabase"], credentials={"supabase": "your-token"}) # Get a single record from a Supabase table by ID result = kit.execute("supabase_get_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["supabase"], credentials={"supabase": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["supabase"], credentials={"supabase": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key (anon/service_role) 1. Create an account at [Supabase](https://supabase.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://supabase.com/dashboard/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("supabase_get_record", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `auth_sign_up` :warning: Sign up a new user via Supabase Auth | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `email` | `string` | Yes | The new user's email address. | | `password` | `string` | Yes | The new user's password. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_auth_sign_up", {"email": "user@example.com", "password": "your-password"}) ``` --- ### `call_edge_function` Call a Supabase Edge Function | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `name` | `string` | Yes | The edge function name. | | `body` | `object` | No | Optional JSON body to send to the function. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_call_edge_function", {"name": "my-name", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `count_exact` Get an exact count of records in a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `filter` | `object` | No | Optional PostgREST filter dict. | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_count_exact", {"table": "your-table", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `count_records` Count records in a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Name of the table. | | `filter` | `object` | No | PostgREST filter dict (e.g. ``{"status": "eq.active"}``). | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_count_records", {"table": "your-table", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `delete_record` :warning: Delete a record from a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `id` | `string` | Yes | Primary key value of the record to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_delete_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id"}) ``` --- ### `get_record` Get a single record from a Supabase table by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `id` | `string` | Yes | Primary key value of the record. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_get_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id"}) ``` --- ### `insert_many` :warning: Insert multiple records into a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `records` | `array` | Yes | List of dicts, each mapping column names to values. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_insert_many", {"table": "your-table", "records": "your-records"}) ``` --- ### `insert_record` Insert a record into a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to value for the new row. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_insert_record", {"table": "your-table", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `list_tables` List tables available in the Supabase project **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_list_tables", {}) ``` --- ### `query_table` Query records from a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `select` | `string` | No | Comma-separated column names (PostgREST select). | | `filter` | `object` | No | Dict of column-name to PostgREST filter expression. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of records to return. (default: `100`) | | `offset` | `integer` | No | Number of records to skip. (default: `0`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[SupabaseRecord]` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_query_table", {"table": "your-table", "select": "your-select", "filter": "your-filter"}) ``` --- ### `rpc` Call a Supabase RPC (stored procedure) | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `function_name` | `string` | Yes | Name of the Postgres function. | | `params` | `object` | No | Keyword arguments to pass to the function. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRPCResult` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_rpc", {"function_name": "your-function_name", "params": "your-params"}) ``` --- ### `select_columns` Select specific columns from a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `columns` | `array` | Yes | List of column names to include in the response. | | `filter` | `object` | No | Optional PostgREST filter dict. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of records to return. (default: `100`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_select_columns", {"table": "your-table", "columns": "your-columns", "filter": "your-filter", "limit": 100}) ``` --- ### `update_record` Update a record in a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `id` | `string` | Yes | Primary key value of the record to update. | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to new value. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_update_record", {"table": "your-table", "id": "your-id", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `update_records` Update records matching a filter in a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `filter_column` | `string` | Yes | Column to filter on. | | `filter_value` | `string` | Yes | PostgREST filter expression (e.g. ``"eq.active"``). | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to new value. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_update_records", {"table": "your-table", "filter_column": "your-filter_column", "filter_value": "your-filter_value", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `upsert_record` Upsert a record into a Supabase table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Table name. | | `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict of column-name to value. | **Returns:** `SupabaseRecord` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_upsert_record", {"table": "your-table", "data": "your-data"}) ``` --- ### `upsert_records` :warning: Upsert records into a table | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `table` | `string` | Yes | Name of the table. | | `records` | `array` | Yes | List of record dicts to upsert. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("supabase_upsert_records", {"table": "your-table", "records": "your-records"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by plan — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`auth_sign_up`, `delete_record`, `insert_many`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Airtable](../airtable/) — Spreadsheet database - [Firestore](../firestore/) — Document database - [Mongodb](../mongodb/) — NoSQL database - [Redis](../redis/) — Key-value store --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

teams.readme_md: # Microsoft Teams > Team collaboration, messaging, and meetings ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-17-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python?style=social)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/microsoftgraph/msgraph-sdk-python) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Microsoft | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [teams.microsoft.com](https://teams.microsoft.com) | | **API Docs** | [learn.microsoft.com](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/api/resources/teams-api-overview) | | **Auth** | OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint | | **Pricing** | Included with Microsoft 365 | --- ## Overview The Microsoft Teams API (via Microsoft Graph) provides access to teams, channels, messages, and members. Send messages, manage team membership, create channels, and build collaboration workflows for Microsoft 365. ## Use Cases - Team messaging automation - Channel management - Meeting scheduling - Notification bots - Workflow integrations ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[teams]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_TEAMS_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["teams"], credentials={"teams": "your-token"}) # List messages in a Teams channel result = kit.execute("teams_list_messages", {"team_id": "T01234567", "channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["teams"], credentials={"teams": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["teams"], credentials={"teams": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### OAuth 2.0 (Microsoft Graph) 1. Create an account at [Microsoft Teams](https://teams.microsoft.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://portal.azure.com/#view/Microsoft_AAD_RegisteredApps) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("teams_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_member` :warning: Add a member to a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The Azure AD object ID of the user to add. | | `roles` | `array` | No | Optional list of roles to assign (e.g. ``["owner"]``). Defaults to member if omitted. | **Returns:** `TeamsMember` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_add_member", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "user_id": "user-123", "roles": "your-roles"}) ``` --- ### `create_channel` Create a new channel in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Display name for the new channel. | | `description` | `string` | No | Optional description for the channel. | **Returns:** `TeamsChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_create_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "name": "my-name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `delete_channel` :warning: Delete a channel from a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel to delete. | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_delete_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_channel` Get a single channel by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | **Returns:** `TeamsChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_get_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567"}) ``` --- ### `get_team` Get a single team by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | **Returns:** `Team` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_get_team", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_user_presence` Get a user's presence status in Teams | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `user_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the user. | **Returns:** `TeamsPresence` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_get_user_presence", {"user_id": "user-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_channels` List channels in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_channels", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_chat_messages` List messages in a Teams chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the chat. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TeamsMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_chat_messages", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "limit": 50, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) ``` --- ### `list_chats` List chats the user is part of | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of chats to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_chats", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `list_members` List members of a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_members", {"team_id": "your-team_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List messages in a Teams channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of messages per page (max 50). (default: `50`) | | `page_url` | `string` | No | Full ``@odata.nextLink`` URL for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TeamsMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_messages", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "limit": 50, "page_url": "your-page_url"}) ``` --- ### `list_teams` List teams the user has joined **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_list_teams", {}) ``` --- ### `remove_member` :warning: Remove a member from a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `member_id` | `string` | Yes | The membership ID of the member to remove (this is the conversation member ID, not the user ID). | **Returns:** `NoneType` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_remove_member", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "member_id": "your-member_id"}) ``` --- ### `reply_to_message` :warning: Reply to a message in a Teams channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `message_id` | `string` | Yes | The ID of the message to reply to. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Reply content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `TeamsMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_reply_to_message", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "message_id": "your-message_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `send_chat_message` :warning: Send a message to a Teams chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the chat. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Message content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `TeamsMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_send_chat_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `send_message` :warning: Send a message to a Teams channel | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `content` | `string` | Yes | Message content (HTML supported). | **Returns:** `TeamsMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_send_message", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "content": "your-content"}) ``` --- ### `update_channel` Update a channel in a team | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `team_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the team. | | `channel_id` | `string` | Yes | The unique ID of the channel. | | `display_name` | `string` | No | New display name for the channel. | | `description` | `string` | No | New description for the channel. | **Returns:** `TeamsChannel` ```python result = kit.execute("teams_update_channel", {"team_id": "your-team_id", "channel_id": "C01234567", "display_name": "your-display_name", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_member`, `delete_channel`, `remove_member`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Telegram](../telegram/) — Bot messaging --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

telegram.readme_md: # Telegram > Bot API for messaging and group management ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-26-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/python-telegram-bot/python-telegram-bot?style=social)](https://github.com/python-telegram-bot/python-telegram-bot) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/python-telegram-bot/python-telegram-bot) [![C++ (TDLib)](https://img.shields.io/badge/C++_(TDLib)-SDK-00599C?logo=github)](https://github.com/tdlib/td) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Telegram FZ-LLC | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [telegram.org](https://telegram.org) | | **API Docs** | [core.telegram.org](https://core.telegram.org/bots/api) | | **Auth** | Bot Token | | **Rate Limit** | 30 messages/second (group), 1 msg/sec (per chat) | | **Pricing** | Free | --- ## Overview The Telegram Bot API lets you send and receive messages, manage groups and channels, handle inline queries, and create interactive bot experiences. Build notification bots, customer support flows, and command-driven automation. ## Use Cases - Notification bots - Group management - Inline bot interactions - Customer support - Automated responses ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[telegram]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_TELEGRAM_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["telegram"], credentials={"telegram": "your-token"}) # Send a text message to a chat result = kit.execute("telegram_send_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "text": "Hello!", "parse_mode": "your-parse_mode"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["telegram"], credentials={"telegram": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["telegram"], credentials={"telegram": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bot Token 1. Create an account at [Telegram](https://telegram.org) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://t.me/BotFather) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("telegram_send_message", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `answer_callback_query` Answer a callback query from an inline keyboard | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `callback_query_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the callback query. | | `text` | `string` | No | Optional notification text (0-200 characters). | | `show_alert` | `boolean` | No | If True, show an alert instead of a toast. (default: `False`) | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_answer_callback_query", {"callback_query_id": "your-callback_query_id", "text": "Hello!", "show_alert": False}) ``` --- ### `ban_chat_member` :warning: Ban a user from a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `user_id` | `integer` | Yes | Unique identifier of the user to ban. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_ban_chat_member", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "user_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the chat. | | `message_id` | `integer` | Yes | Identifier of the message to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_delete_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "message_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `delete_webhook` Delete the current webhook **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_delete_webhook", {}) ``` --- ### `edit_message` Edit a text message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the chat containing the message. | | `message_id` | `integer` | Yes | Identifier of the message to edit. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | New text for the message. | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_edit_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "message_id": 10, "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `export_chat_invite_link` Export an invite link for a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | **Returns:** `str` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_export_chat_invite_link", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_chat` Get information about a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | **Returns:** `TelegramChat` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_get_chat", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_chat_administrators` Get the list of administrators in a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_get_chat_administrators", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_chat_member` Get information about a chat member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `user_id` | `integer` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target user. | **Returns:** `TelegramChatMember` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_get_chat_member", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "user_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `get_chat_members_count` Get the number of members in a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | **Returns:** `int` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_get_chat_members_count", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_me` Get information about the bot **Returns:** `TelegramUser` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_get_me", {}) ``` --- ### `get_updates` Get incoming updates for the bot | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `offset` | `integer` | No | Identifier of the first update to be returned. Set to ``last_update_id + 1`` to acknowledge previous updates. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of updates to retrieve (1-100). (default: `100`) | | `timeout` | `integer` | No | Timeout in seconds for long polling (0 for short polling). | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_get_updates", {"offset": 10, "limit": 100}) ``` --- ### `leave_chat` :warning: Leave a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the chat to leave. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_leave_chat", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id"}) ``` --- ### `pin_message` Pin a message in a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `message_id` | `integer` | Yes | Identifier of the message to pin. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_pin_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "message_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `send_contact` Send a contact to a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | | `phone_number` | `string` | Yes | Contact phone number. | | `first_name` | `string` | Yes | Contact first name. | | `last_name` | `string` | No | Optional contact last name. | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_send_contact", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "phone_number": "your-phone_number", "first_name": "your-first_name", "last_name": "your-last_name"}) ``` --- ### `send_document` Send a document to a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | | `document_url` | `string` | Yes | URL of the document to send. | | `caption` | `string` | No | Optional document caption (up to 1024 characters). | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_send_document", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "document_url": "your-document_url", "caption": "your-caption"}) ``` --- ### `send_location` Send a location to a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | | `latitude` | `number` | Yes | Latitude of the location (-90 to 90). | | `longitude` | `number` | Yes | Longitude of the location (-180 to 180). | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_send_location", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "latitude": 10, "longitude": 10}) ``` --- ### `send_message` Send a text message to a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier for the target chat or username (e.g. ``@channelusername``). | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Text of the message (up to 4096 characters). | | `parse_mode` | `string` | No | Parse mode for formatting (``Markdown``, ``HTML``, or ``MarkdownV2``). | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_send_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "text": "Hello!", "parse_mode": "your-parse_mode"}) ``` --- ### `send_photo` Send a photo to a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | | `photo_url` | `string` | Yes | URL of the photo to send. | | `caption` | `string` | No | Optional photo caption (up to 1024 characters). | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_send_photo", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "photo_url": "your-photo_url", "caption": "your-caption"}) ``` --- ### `send_poll` :warning: Send a poll to a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier or username of the target chat. | | `question` | `string` | Yes | Poll question (1-300 characters). | | `options` | `array` | Yes | List of 2-10 answer options. | | `is_anonymous` | `boolean` | No | Whether the poll is anonymous (default True). (default: `True`) | **Returns:** `TelegramMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_send_poll", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "question": "your-question", "options": "your-options", "is_anonymous": True}) ``` --- ### `set_chat_description` :warning: Set the description of a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `description` | `string` | Yes | New chat description (0-255 characters). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_set_chat_description", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "description": "your-description"}) ``` --- ### `set_chat_title` :warning: Set the title of a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `title` | `string` | Yes | New chat title (1-128 characters). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_set_chat_title", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "title": "My Title"}) ``` --- ### `set_webhook` Set a webhook URL for receiving updates | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `url` | `string` | Yes | HTTPS URL to send updates to. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_set_webhook", {"url": "https://example.com"}) ``` --- ### `unban_chat_member` Unban a user from a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `user_id` | `integer` | Yes | Unique identifier of the user to unban. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_unban_chat_member", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "user_id": 10}) ``` --- ### `unpin_all_messages` :warning: Unpin all messages in a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_unpin_all_messages", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id"}) ``` --- ### `unpin_message` Unpin a message in a chat | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `chat_id` | `string` | Yes | Unique identifier of the target chat. | | `message_id` | `integer` | No | Identifier of the message to unpin. If not specified, the most recent pinned message is unpinned. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("telegram_unpin_message", {"chat_id": "your-chat_id", "message_id": 10}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 30 messages/second (group), 1 msg/sec (per chat) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`ban_chat_member`, `delete_message`, `leave_chat`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 26 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

trello.readme_md: # Trello > Visual project management with boards and cards ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Project_Management-8B5CF6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-25-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/sarumont/py-trello?style=social)](https://github.com/sarumont/py-trello) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/sarumont/py-trello) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Atlassian | | **Category** | Project Management | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [trello.com](https://trello.com) | | **API Docs** | [developer.atlassian.com](https://developer.atlassian.com/cloud/trello/rest/api-group-actions/) | | **Auth** | API Key + Token | | **Rate Limit** | 100 requests/10 seconds per token | | **Pricing** | Free tier, Standard from $5/user/month | --- ## Overview The Trello API provides access to boards, lists, cards, members, and labels. Create and organize Kanban workflows, manage task cards, track progress, and build custom project management integrations. ## Use Cases - Kanban workflow management - Task tracking - Team collaboration - Sprint boards - Content planning ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[trello]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_TRELLO_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["trello"], credentials={"trello": "your-token"}) # List boards for a Trello member result = kit.execute("trello_list_boards", {"member": "me"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["trello"], credentials={"trello": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["trello"], credentials={"trello": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### API Key + Token 1. Create an account at [Trello](https://trello.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://trello.com/power-ups/admin) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("trello_list_boards", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_attachment` :warning: Add an attachment to a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | | `url` | `string` | No | URL to attach to the card. | | `name` | `string` | No | Display name for the attachment. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_add_attachment", {"card_id": "your-card_id", "url": "https://example.com", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `add_comment` :warning: Add a comment to a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | | `text` | `string` | Yes | Comment text. | **Returns:** `TrelloComment` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_add_comment", {"card_id": "your-card_id", "text": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `add_label` Add a label to a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | | `label_id` | `string` | Yes | The label ID to add. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_add_label", {"card_id": "your-card_id", "label_id": "your-label_id"}) ``` --- ### `archive_card` :warning: Archive a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID to archive. | **Returns:** `TrelloCard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_archive_card", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `archive_list` :warning: Archive a Trello list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello list ID to archive. | **Returns:** `TrelloList` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_archive_list", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `create_card` :warning: Create a new Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The list ID where the card will be created. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Card name/title. | | `desc` | `string` | No | Optional card description. | **Returns:** `TrelloCard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_create_card", {"list_id": "list-123", "name": "my-name", "desc": "your-desc"}) ``` --- ### `create_checklist` :warning: Create a checklist on a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new checklist. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_create_checklist", {"card_id": "your-card_id", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `create_label` :warning: Create a label on a Trello board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Label name. | | `color` | `string` | No | Label colour (e.g. ``"green"``, ``"red"``, ``"blue"``). Pass ``None`` for no colour. | **Returns:** `dict` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_create_label", {"board_id": "your-board_id", "name": "my-name", "color": "your-color"}) ``` --- ### `create_list` :warning: Create a new list on a Trello board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | | `name` | `string` | Yes | Name for the new list. | **Returns:** `TrelloList` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_create_list", {"board_id": "your-board_id", "name": "my-name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_card` :warning: Delete a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_delete_card", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_board` Get a single Trello board by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | **Returns:** `TrelloBoard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_get_board", {"board_id": "your-board_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_card` Get a single Trello card by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | **Returns:** `TrelloCard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_get_card", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_list` Get a single Trello list by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello list ID. | **Returns:** `TrelloList` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_get_list", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_attachments` List attachments on a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_attachments", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_board_members` List members of a Trello board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_board_members", {"board_id": "your-board_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_boards` List boards for a Trello member | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `member` | `string` | No | Member ID or ``me`` for the authenticated user. (default: `me`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TrelloBoard]` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_boards", {"member": "me"}) ``` --- ### `list_card_actions` List actions (activity log) on a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_card_actions", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_cards` List cards in a Trello list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello list ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TrelloCard]` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_cards", {"list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `list_checklists` List checklists on a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_checklists", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_labels` List all labels on a Trello board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_labels", {"board_id": "your-board_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_lists` List all lists on a Trello board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TrelloList]` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_lists", {"board_id": "your-board_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_members` List members of a Trello board | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `board_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello board ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_list_members", {"board_id": "your-board_id"}) ``` --- ### `move_card` :warning: Move a Trello card to a different list | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID to move. | | `list_id` | `string` | Yes | The destination list ID. | **Returns:** `TrelloCard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_move_card", {"card_id": "your-card_id", "list_id": "list-123"}) ``` --- ### `unarchive_card` Unarchive a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID to unarchive. | **Returns:** `TrelloCard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_unarchive_card", {"card_id": "your-card_id"}) ``` --- ### `update_card` :warning: Update a Trello card | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `card_id` | `string` | Yes | The Trello card ID to update. | | `name` | `string` | No | New card name. | | `desc` | `string` | No | New card description. | | `list_id` | `string` | No | Move card to a different list. | **Returns:** `TrelloCard` ```python result = kit.execute("trello_update_card", {"card_id": "your-card_id", "name": "my-name", "desc": "your-desc"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is 100 requests/10 seconds per token — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_attachment`, `add_comment`, `archive_card`) cannot be undone — use with caution - This connector has 25 actions — use `ToolKit(include_actions=[...])` to expose only what your agent needs ## Related Connectors - [Jira](../jira/) — Issue tracking - [Asana](../asana/) — Work management - [Linear](../linear/) — Modern issue tracker --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

twilio.readme_md: # Twilio > Communication APIs for SMS, voice, and video ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Communication-3B82F6) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-20-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/twilio/twilio-python?style=social)](https://github.com/twilio/twilio-python) [![Python](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-SDK-3776AB?logo=github)](https://github.com/twilio/twilio-python) [![Node](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node-SDK-339933?logo=github)](https://github.com/twilio/twilio-node) [![Go](https://img.shields.io/badge/Go-SDK-00ADD8?logo=github)](https://github.com/twilio/twilio-go) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Twilio Inc. | | **Category** | Communication | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [twilio.com](https://twilio.com) | | **API Docs** | [www.twilio.com](https://www.twilio.com/docs/usage/api) | | **Auth** | Basic Auth (Account SID + Auth Token) | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by service | | **Pricing** | Pay-per-use (SMS from $0.0079/msg) | --- ## Overview The Twilio API provides SMS messaging, voice calls, and communication services. Send and receive text messages, make and manage phone calls, verify phone numbers, and build multi-channel communication workflows. ## Use Cases - SMS notifications - Two-factor authentication - Voice call automation - Phone number verification - WhatsApp messaging ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[twilio]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_TWILIO_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["twilio"], credentials={"twilio": "your-token"}) # List SMS/MMS messages from your Twilio account result = kit.execute("twilio_list_messages", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_": "your-from_"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["twilio"], credentials={"twilio": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["twilio"], credentials={"twilio": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Basic Auth (Account SID + Auth Token) 1. Create an account at [Twilio](https://twilio.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://console.twilio.com/) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("twilio_list_messages", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `check_verification` Check a verification code via Twilio Verify | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `service_sid` | `string` | Yes | The Verify service SID (e.g. ``VA...``). | | `to` | `string` | Yes | The phone number or email that was verified. | | `code` | `string` | Yes | The 4-10 character verification code to check. | **Returns:** `TwilioVerificationCheck` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_check_verification", {"service_sid": "your-service_sid", "to": "recipient@example.com", "code": "your-code"}) ``` --- ### `create_conversation` :warning: Create a new Twilio conversation | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `friendly_name` | `string` | Yes | Human-readable name for the conversation (max 256 characters). | **Returns:** `TwilioConversation` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_create_conversation", {"friendly_name": "your-friendly_name"}) ``` --- ### `create_verify_service` :warning: Create a Twilio Verify service | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `friendly_name` | `string` | Yes | Human-readable name for the service (up to 32 characters). | **Returns:** `TwilioVerifyService` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_create_verify_service", {"friendly_name": "your-friendly_name"}) ``` --- ### `delete_message` :warning: Delete a message from your Twilio account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_sid` | `string` | Yes | The Twilio message SID (e.g. ``SM...``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_delete_message", {"message_sid": "your-message_sid"}) ``` --- ### `delete_recording` :warning: Delete a call recording by SID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `sid` | `string` | Yes | The recording SID (e.g. ``RE...``). | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_delete_recording", {"sid": "your-sid"}) ``` --- ### `get_account` Retrieve your Twilio account details **Returns:** `TwilioAccount` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_get_account", {}) ``` --- ### `get_account_usage` Get account usage records for the current billing period **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_get_account_usage", {}) ``` --- ### `get_call` Retrieve a single Twilio call by SID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `call_sid` | `string` | Yes | The Twilio call SID (e.g. ``CA...``). | **Returns:** `TwilioCall` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_get_call", {"call_sid": "your-call_sid"}) ``` --- ### `get_message` Retrieve a single Twilio message by SID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_sid` | `string` | Yes | The Twilio message SID (e.g. ``SM...``). | **Returns:** `TwilioMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_get_message", {"message_sid": "your-message_sid"}) ``` --- ### `list_calls` List voice calls from your Twilio account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | No | Filter by recipient phone number. | | `from_` | `string` | No | Filter by caller phone number. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum calls per page (max 1000). (default: `20`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TwilioCall]` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_list_calls", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_": "your-from_"}) ``` --- ### `list_conversations` List conversations from your Twilio account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of conversations to return. (default: `20`) | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_list_conversations", {"limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_messages` List SMS/MMS messages from your Twilio account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | No | Filter by recipient phone number. | | `from_` | `string` | No | Filter by sender phone number. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum messages per page (max 1000). (default: `20`) | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[TwilioMessage]` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_list_messages", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_": "your-from_"}) ``` --- ### `list_phone_numbers` List incoming phone numbers on your Twilio account **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_list_phone_numbers", {}) ``` --- ### `list_recordings` List call recordings from your Twilio account | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum number of recordings to return. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_list_recordings", {"limit": 10}) ``` --- ### `lookup_phone` Look up information about a phone number | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `phone_number` | `string` | Yes | Phone number in E.164 or national format. | | `fields` | `string` | No | Comma-separated data packages to include (e.g. ``"line_type_intelligence,caller_name"``). | **Returns:** `TwilioLookupResult` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_lookup_phone", {"phone_number": "your-phone_number", "fields": "your-fields"}) ``` --- ### `make_call` :warning: Initiate a voice call via Twilio | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient phone number in E.164 format. | | `from_` | `string` | Yes | Caller phone number (must be a Twilio number). | | `url` | `string` | Yes | TwiML URL that controls the call flow. | **Returns:** `TwilioCall` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_make_call", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_": "your-from_", "url": "https://example.com"}) ``` --- ### `send_mms` :warning: Send an MMS message with media via Twilio | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient phone number in E.164 format. | | `from_` | `string` | Yes | Sender phone number (must be a Twilio number). | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Message text content. | | `media_url` | `string` | Yes | Public URL of the media to attach. | **Returns:** `TwilioMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_send_mms", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_": "your-from_", "body": "Hello!", "media_url": "your-media_url"}) ``` --- ### `send_sms` :warning: Send an SMS or MMS message via Twilio | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient phone number in E.164 format. | | `from_` | `string` | Yes | Sender phone number (must be a Twilio number). | | `body` | `string` | Yes | Message text content. | **Returns:** `TwilioMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_send_sms", {"to": "recipient@example.com", "from_": "your-from_", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ### `send_verification` :warning: Send a verification token via Twilio Verify | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `service_sid` | `string` | Yes | The Verify service SID (e.g. ``VA...``). | | `to` | `string` | Yes | Recipient phone number (E.164) or email address. | | `channel` | `string` | No | Delivery channel (``sms``, ``call``, ``email``, or ``whatsapp``). (default: `sms`) | **Returns:** `TwilioVerification` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_send_verification", {"service_sid": "your-service_sid", "to": "recipient@example.com", "channel": "sms"}) ``` --- ### `update_message` Update or redact a Twilio message | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `message_sid` | `string` | Yes | The Twilio message SID (e.g. ``SM...``). | | `body` | `string` | No | New message body text (set to ``""`` to redact). | **Returns:** `TwilioMessage` ```python result = kit.execute("twilio_update_message", {"message_sid": "your-message_sid", "body": "Hello!"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by service — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`create_conversation`, `create_verify_service`, `delete_message`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Gmail](../gmail/) — Email automation - [Slack](../slack/) — Team messaging - [Discord](../discord/) — Community messaging - [Teams](../teams/) — Microsoft collaboration --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

vercel.readme_md: # Vercel > Frontend deployment and serverless platform ![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Devops-06B6D4) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-16-purple) [![GitHub stars](https://img.shields.io/github/stars/vercel/vercel?style=social)](https://github.com/vercel/vercel) [![TypeScript (source)](https://img.shields.io/badge/TypeScript_(source)-SDK-3178C6?logo=github)](https://github.com/vercel/vercel) | | | |---|---| | **Company** | Vercel Inc. | | **Category** | Devops | | **Protocol** | REST | | **Website** | [vercel.com](https://vercel.com) | | **API Docs** | [vercel.com](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api) | | **Auth** | Bearer Token | | **Rate Limit** | Varies by endpoint | | **Pricing** | Free tier (Hobby), Pro from $20/user/month | --- ## Overview The Vercel API provides access to projects, deployments, domains, and environment variables. Deploy applications, manage domains, configure environment settings, and automate your frontend CI/CD pipeline. ## Use Cases - Deployment automation - Domain management - Environment variable management - Project configuration - Build monitoring ## Installation ```bash pip install "toolsconnector[vercel]" ``` Set your credentials: ```bash export TC_VERCEL_CREDENTIALS=your-token ``` ## Quick Start ```python from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit kit = ToolKit(["vercel"], credentials={"vercel": "your-token"}) # List Vercel projects result = kit.execute("vercel_list_projects", {"limit": 20, "page": "your-page"}) print(result) ``` ### MCP Server ```python kit = ToolKit(["vercel"], credentials={"vercel": "your-token"}) kit.serve_mcp() # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly ``` ### OpenAI Function Calling ```python kit = ToolKit(["vercel"], credentials={"vercel": "your-token"}) tools = kit.to_openai_tools() # Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...) ``` ## Authentication ### Bearer Token 1. Create an account at [Vercel](https://vercel.com) 2. Navigate to API settings or developer console 3. Generate an API key or access token [Get credentials →](https://vercel.com/account/tokens) ## Error Handling ```python from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError try: result = kit.execute("vercel_list_projects", {}) except RateLimitError as e: print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s") except AuthError as e: print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}") ``` ## Actions ### `add_domain` :warning: Add a domain to a Vercel project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | | `domain` | `string` | Yes | The domain name to add. | **Returns:** `VercelDomain` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_add_domain", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "domain": "your-domain"}) ``` --- ### `create_env_var` :warning: Create an environment variable for a Vercel project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | | `key` | `string` | Yes | The environment variable name. | | `value` | `string` | Yes | The environment variable value. | | `target` | `string` | Yes | Deployment target(s): ``production``, ``preview``, or ``development``. Comma-separate for multiple. | **Returns:** `VercelEnvVar` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_create_env_var", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "key": "my-key", "value": "my-value", "target": "your-target"}) ``` --- ### `delete_deployment` :warning: Delete a deployment | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deployment_id` | `string` | Yes | The deployment ID or URL. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_delete_deployment", {"deployment_id": "your-deployment_id"}) ``` --- ### `delete_env_var` :warning: Delete an environment variable from a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | | `env_id` | `string` | Yes | The environment variable ID to delete. | **Returns:** `bool` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_delete_env_var", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "env_id": "your-env_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_deployment` Get a single Vercel deployment by ID | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deployment_id` | `string` | Yes | The deployment ID or URL. | **Returns:** `VercelDeployment` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_get_deployment", {"deployment_id": "your-deployment_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_deployment_logs` Get logs for a deployment | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deployment_id` | `string` | Yes | The deployment ID. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_get_deployment_logs", {"deployment_id": "your-deployment_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_project` Get a single Vercel project by ID or name | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | **Returns:** `VercelProject` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_get_project", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `get_project_env_vars` Get environment variables for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_get_project_env_vars", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_aliases` List deployment aliases | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | No | Optional project ID to filter by. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_list_aliases", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_deployments` List Vercel deployments | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | No | Filter deployments by project ID or name. | | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum deployments per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor (``until`` timestamp) for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[VercelDeployment]` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_list_deployments", {"project_id": "your-project_id", "limit": 20}) ``` --- ### `list_domains` List domains for a Vercel project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_list_domains", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_env_vars` List environment variables for a Vercel project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_list_env_vars", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ### `list_projects` List Vercel projects | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `limit` | `integer` | No | Maximum projects per page (max 100). (default: `20`) | | `page` | `string` | No | Cursor (``until`` timestamp) for the next page. | **Returns:** `PaginatedList[VercelProject]` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_list_projects", {"limit": 20, "page": "your-page"}) ``` --- ### `list_team_members` List team members **Returns:** `list` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_list_team_members", {}) ``` --- ### `redeploy` Redeploy an existing deployment | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `deployment_id` | `string` | Yes | The deployment ID to redeploy. | **Returns:** `VercelDeployment` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_redeploy", {"deployment_id": "your-deployment_id"}) ``` --- ### `trigger_deploy` :warning: Trigger a new deployment for a project | Parameter | Type | Required | Description | |---|---|---|---| | `project_id` | `string` | Yes | The project ID or name. | **Returns:** `VercelDeployment` ```python result = kit.execute("vercel_trigger_deploy", {"project_id": "your-project_id"}) ``` --- ## Tips - Rate limit is Varies by endpoint — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls - Actions marked as destructive (`add_domain`, `create_env_var`, `delete_deployment`) cannot be undone — use with caution ## Related Connectors - [Cloudflare](../cloudflare/) — CDN and security - [Datadog](../datadog/) — Monitoring - [Pagerduty](../pagerduty/) — Incident management - [Dockerhub](../dockerhub/) — Container registry --- *This connector is part of [ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) — the universal tool-connection primitive for Python and AI agents.*

webhook.readme_md:
# Webhook

> Send and manage HTTP webhooks to any endpoint

![Category](https://img.shields.io/badge/category-Custom-6366F1) ![Protocol](https://img.shields.io/badge/protocol-REST-blue) ![Actions](https://img.shields.io/badge/actions-12-purple)

| | |
|---|---|
| **Company** | ToolsConnector |
| **Category** | Custom |
| **Protocol** | REST |
| **Website** | [github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) |
| **API Docs** | [github.com](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector) |
| **Auth** | Bearer Token, API Key, Custom Headers |
| **Rate Limit** | N/A (depends on target) |
| **Pricing** | Free (built-in connector) |

---

## Overview

The Webhook connector lets you send HTTP requests to any URL endpoint. Fire webhooks, manage retry logic, track delivery status, and build event-driven integrations with external services that accept webhooks.

## Use Cases

- Event notifications
- Service integration
- Custom API calls
- Webhook delivery with retry
- Cross-service triggers

## Installation

```bash
pip install "toolsconnector[webhook]"
```

Set your credentials:

```bash
export TC_WEBHOOK_CREDENTIALS=your-token
```

## Quick Start

```python
from toolsconnector.serve import ToolKit

kit = ToolKit(["webhook"], credentials={"webhook": "your-token"})

# Check if an endpoint is reachable
result = kit.execute("webhook_check_endpoint", {"url": "https://example.com"})
print(result)
```

### MCP Server

```python
kit = ToolKit(["webhook"], credentials={"webhook": "your-token"})
kit.serve_mcp()  # Claude Desktop / Cursor connects instantly
```

### OpenAI Function Calling

```python
kit = ToolKit(["webhook"], credentials={"webhook": "your-token"})
tools = kit.to_openai_tools()
# Pass to: openai.chat.completions.create(tools=tools, ...)
```

## Authentication

### Bearer Token

1. Create an account at [Webhook](https://github.com/sachinshelke/ToolsConnector)
2. Navigate to API settings or developer console
3. Generate an API key or access token

## Error Handling

```python
from toolsconnector.errors import RateLimitError, AuthError

try:
    result = kit.execute("webhook_check_endpoint", {})
except RateLimitError as e:
    print(f"Rate limited. Retry in {e.retry_after_seconds}s")
except AuthError as e:
    print(f"Auth failed: {e.suggestion}")
```

## Actions

<!-- ACTIONS_START -->

### `check_endpoint`

Check if an endpoint is reachable

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The URL to check. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_check_endpoint", {"url": "https://example.com"})
```

---


### `send_batch` :warning:

Send a batch of webhooks

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `payloads` | `array` | Yes | List of JSON-serialisable payload dicts. |

**Returns:** `WebhookBatchResult`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_batch", {"url": "https://example.com", "payloads": "your-payloads"})
```

---


### `send_form` :warning:

Send form-encoded data

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `data` | `object` | Yes | Form fields as key-value string pairs. |
| `headers` | `object` | No | Additional HTTP headers. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_form", {"url": "https://example.com", "data": "your-data", "headers": "your-headers"})
```

---


### `send_graphql` :warning:

Send a GraphQL query

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The GraphQL endpoint URL. |
| `query` | `string` | Yes | The GraphQL query/mutation string. |
| `variables` | `object` | No | Optional GraphQL variables dict. |
| `headers` | `object` | No | Additional HTTP headers. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_graphql", {"url": "https://example.com", "query": "is:unread", "variables": "your-variables", "headers": "your-headers"})
```

---


### `send_json` :warning:

Send a JSON payload

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `data` | `object` | Yes | JSON-serialisable data dict. |
| `headers` | `object` | No | Additional HTTP headers. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_json", {"url": "https://example.com", "data": "your-data", "headers": "your-headers"})
```

---


### `send_multipart` :warning:

Send a multipart form-data request

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `files` | `object` | Yes | Dict mapping field names to tuples of
``(filename, content_bytes, content_type)``. |
| `data` | `object` | No | Optional form fields to include alongside files. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_multipart", {"url": "https://example.com", "files": "your-files", "data": "your-data"})
```

---


### `send_webhook` :warning:

Send a webhook request

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `payload` | `object` | Yes | JSON-serialisable payload dict. |
| `method` | `string` | No | HTTP method (default ``POST``). (default: `POST`) |
| `headers` | `object` | No | Additional HTTP headers. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_webhook", {"url": "https://example.com", "payload": "your-payload", "method": "POST", "headers": "your-headers"})
```

---


### `send_with_api_key` :warning:

Send a webhook with API key authentication

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `payload` | `object` | Yes | JSON-serialisable payload dict. |
| `api_key` | `string` | Yes | The API key value. |
| `header_name` | `string` | No | Header name for the API key
(default ``X-API-Key``). |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_with_api_key", {"url": "https://example.com", "payload": "your-payload", "api_key": "your-api_key", "header_name": "your-header_name"})
```

---


### `send_with_basic_auth` :warning:

Send a webhook with HTTP Basic authentication

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `payload` | `object` | Yes | JSON-serialisable payload dict. |
| `username` | `string` | Yes | Basic auth username. |
| `password` | `string` | Yes | Basic auth password. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_with_basic_auth", {"url": "https://example.com", "payload": "your-payload", "username": "your-username", "password": "your-password"})
```

---


### `send_with_hmac` :warning:

Send an HMAC-signed webhook

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `payload` | `object` | Yes | JSON-serialisable payload dict. |
| `secret` | `string` | Yes | HMAC shared secret. |
| `header_name` | `string` | No | Header name for the signature
(default ``X-Hub-Signature-256``). (default: `X-Hub-Signature-256`) |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_with_hmac", {"url": "https://example.com", "payload": "your-payload", "secret": "your-secret", "header_name": "X-Hub-Signature-256"})
```

---


### `send_with_retry` :warning:

Send a webhook with automatic retries

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `payload` | `object` | Yes | JSON-serialisable payload dict. |
| `max_retries` | `integer` | No | Maximum retry attempts (default 3). |
| `delay` | `number` | No | Initial delay between retries in seconds (default 1.0). |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_with_retry", {"url": "https://example.com", "payload": "your-payload", "max_retries": 10, "delay": 10})
```

---


### `send_xml` :warning:

Send an XML payload

| Parameter | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `url` | `string` | Yes | The target URL. |
| `data` | `object` | Yes | Dict to convert to XML. |
| `headers` | `object` | No | Additional HTTP headers. |

**Returns:** `WebhookResponse`

```python
result = kit.execute("webhook_send_xml", {"url": "https://example.com", "data": "your-data", "headers": "your-headers"})
```

---

## Tips

- Use batch operations (`send_batch`) for bulk operations instead of individual calls
- Rate limit is N/A (depends on target) — use pagination and caching to minimize API calls
- Actions marked as destructive (`send_batch`, `send_form`, `send_graphql`) cannot be undone — use with caution
